titleZh: 第一讲：奥尔梅克|玛雅文明的“母亲”请我们吃玉米

paragraphs.en: Hello everyone, and welcome back to our lecture series on World History. Today is the first lesson in our Third Series: The Classical Era. And we will begin this lesson by looking at a part of the world which we have so far ignored,America and the NativeAmericans. Since this story begins all the way back in the ice age, we will be summarizing a large period of time. This is what that looks like on our big timeline. Our story begins about 15,000 years ago when the first Humans entered theAmericas. No previous hominid species ever made it as far as North and South
paragraphs.zh: 大家好，欢迎回到世界历史系列课 堂。今天是我们第三期的第一节课： 古典时代。 这一课，我们将从美洲和美洲原住 民开始讲起。这一部分，在我们之 前的课程中一直没有提起过。 故事还要从冰河时代开始讲起，因 此会跨越很长一段时间。这是这段 时间在大时间线上的样子。 我们的故事从 15,000 年开始，那 时，美洲大地上第一次出现了人类 的脚印。 以前的原始人类物种从未到过北美 和南美。我们从未在此发现过直立

paragraphs.en: America. We have never found fossils of Homo erectus, or Neanderthals or any other human species in this part of the world. Only Homo sapiens have ever lived in theAmericas. By 12,000 BC, they had settled both North and SouthAmerica, and the human expansion across the earth was finally complete. The main theory for how they reachedAmerica was that they crossed over between Russian and Alaska. Since this was during the last ice-age, there was so much water trapped in the glaciers that the sea level was several hundred meters lower than it is today. This created a land bridge that
paragraphs.zh: 人、尼安德特人或世界上任何其他 人类物种的化石。 只有智人曾在美洲生活过。 到公元前 12,000 年，他们已经在北 美和南美定居下来，人类在地球上 的扩张终于完成了。 关于智人是如何抵达美洲大陆的， 主流的观点是：他们跨越了俄罗斯 和阿拉斯加州。 而这一切发生在最后一次冰河时 代，大量的水被困在冰山中，当时 的海平面比现在高了数百米。 这就形成了连接俄罗斯和阿拉斯加

paragraphs.en: connected Russia andAlaska. Scientists call this land bridge Beringia. Imagine what it must have been to be among those intrepid first explorers ofAmerica. Moving across a frozen landscape, into a place no human had ever gone before. And you have only stone-age tools, your courage, and your intelligence to keep you alive. There is also another theory that perhaps some of these migrants sailed boats along the coast and moved intoAmerica that way. The debate over which path they took is still a big topic in archaeology, but either way, these first settlers were brave, intelligent
paragraphs.zh: 的陆桥。科学家们将其称为白令陆 桥。 想象一下这些勇敢无畏的初代美洲 探险者当时的境况。 穿过冰冻的地貌，进入一个人类从 未抵达过的地方。 而且你只能依靠石器、勇气和智慧 来维持生命。 还有说法认为，这些移民中，可能 有一部分人是沿着海岸，走水路进 入了美洲大陆。 关于他们是通过何种途径进入美洲 的，至今仍是考古界的一大课题。 但不管怎样，这些初代移民都是勇 敢、聪明、坚强的。他们是真正的

paragraphs.en: and tough. They were true pioneers in every sense of the word. I wish we knew some of their stories, because it must have been a great adventure! The main evidence for this migration comes from several archeological sites. The two most famous are Clovis in New Mexico in the United States, and Monte Verde in Chile. In both of these places archaeologists have found stone tools, and they have been able to use radio-carbon dating to estimate the age of organic materials that were found at the same location. The Clovis spear-point is from around 11,000 BC, and the Monte Verde spear point is from 13,000 BC.
paragraphs.zh: 先驱。 我希望我们能知道一些他们的故 事，因为这一定是一次伟大的冒险！ 这次移民的主要证据来自几个考古 遗址。最著名的两个是美国新墨西 哥州的克洛维斯和智利的蒙特贝尔 德。 考古学家在这两处都发现了石器工 具，他们已经能够使用放射性碳测 定法来估计在同一地点发现的有机 材料的原始年代。 克洛维斯矛头来自公元前 11,000 年，而蒙特贝尔德矛头则来自于公 元前 13,000 年。

paragraphs.en: So we know that people were living in both North and SouthAmerica at least that long ago, and maybe even a few thousand years earlier than that. These earliestAmericans hunted animals that no longer exist, like the GiantArmadillo or Wooly Mammoth, and they probably lived in small mobile bands, like most hunter-gatherers across the world. But then, around 9500 BC, the climate changed, the ice ages ended, and the world entered our current warm period, which has been stable for the last 10,000 years. All across the world, people seem to have responded to this change in the climate by inventing farming. They started to farm in the Fertile
paragraphs.zh: 我们由此得知，早在公元前 13,000 年，就已经有人在北美和南美定居 了，甚至有可能比那还要早了几千 年。 被这些最早期美洲人围捕的猎物已 经不复存在了，比如大型犰狳或长 毛猛犸象。他们很有可能和大多数 狩猎采集者一样，以小团队为单位 自由移动。 但是，大约在公元前 9500 年，气候 改变了，冰河时代结束了，地球进 入了我们现在的温暖期，并且在过 去的 1 万年里一直保持稳定。 在世界各地，人们都通过发明农业 来应对气候变化。 最初在肥沃的新月地带，然后是中

paragraphs.en: Crescent, and then in China, New Guinea, and Mexico. All of this happened approximately between 9000 and 7000 BC. I highlighted Southern Mexico in yellow, because that it what we are talking about today. The lowlands of southern Mexico are where farming in theAmericas was born. This climate was warm and wet, and the soil was rich. Here on the map I have marked out this area in yellow, so you know exactly where this story takes place. Here in lowland Mexico, the Native people learned to farm. They discovered plants that could be domesticated, and they began to prepare the soil and grow them, just
paragraphs.zh: 国、新几内亚和墨西哥。 所 有 这 些 都 发 生 在 公 元 前 9000-7000 年之间。 我用黄色标出了墨西哥南部，因为 这就是我们今天要讲的内容。 墨西哥南部的低地是美洲农业的诞 生地。 这里气候温暖潮湿，土壤肥沃。 在地图上，我用黄色标出了这个区 域，即故事发生的地方。 在墨西哥低地，当地人学会了耕种。 他们发现了可以被驯化的植物，他 们开始开垦土壤并种植这些植物， 就同新月沃土的纳图夫人和南庄头 的中国人一样。

paragraphs.en: like the Natufians in the Fertile Crescent or the Chinese at Nan Zhuang Tou. But of course, the NativeAmericans were working with different plant species. There was no wheat or barley inAmerica. But there WAS another grass which produced edible seeds. This plant is called Teosinte, and it still grows wild in Mexico today. The NativeAmericans began to plant Teosinte deliberately, and selected the seeds that produced the biggest and largest kernels of grain. As they did this, the genome of the Teosinte plant changed rapidly, and several extreme mutations were selected for. The Teosinte plant began producing hundreds of kernels instead of just a dozen.
paragraphs.zh: 当然，美洲原住民种植的是不同的 植物。美洲本土并没有大麦或小麦。 但是，有另一种植物的种子是可以 食用的：墨西哥玉米（也称“大刍 草”）。今天，在墨西哥依然有野生 玉米存在。 美洲原住民开始种植大刍草，并且 挑选出果实最大、最饱满的种子。 在此过程中，大刍草的基因组迅速 发生了变化。在人为干预下，发生 了基因突变。大刍草开始由每株十 几个玉米粒变为数百个。

paragraphs.en: Over several hundred years, these first NativeAmerican farmers turned Teosinte into Corn, one of the greatest crops ever developed. But corn was not the only plant that these NativeAmerican farmers domesticated. They also created potatoes, sunflowers, tomatoes, squash, peppers, cassava, and pineapple. But that wasn't all. They also domesticated beans, vanilla, papaya, guava, avocado, agave, cacao (the main ingredient in chocolate), and tobacco. Have you ever eaten any of these plants or smoked a cigarette? If you have, then you have an ancient NativeAmerican farmer to thank for giving the human race these crops.
paragraphs.zh: 在几百年的时间里，这些初代美洲 土著农民把大刍草培育成了玉米— —有史以来最伟大的农作物之一。 但玉米并不是这些美洲土著农民驯 养的唯一一种植物。他们还驯化了 土豆、向日葵、西红柿、南瓜、辣 椒、木薯和菠萝。 但这还不是全部。他们还驯化了黄 豆、香草、木瓜、番石榴、鳄梨、 龙舌兰、可可（巧克力的主要成分） 和烟草。 你有没有吃过这些植物，或抽过 烟？如果有过，你就应该感谢古老 的美洲原住民给予人类这些作物。

paragraphs.en: (Of course, if I could change history I would tell them not to bother with Tobacco, THAT plant definitely caused more harm than good!) The NativeAmericans also discovered that three crops worked really well in combination. Corn is a tough plant that grows tall and produces lots of food. But it takes nitrogen out of the soil. However, if you plant beans next to the corn, the beans will grow up the corn stalk and replenish the nitrogen. Then if you plant squash next to the beans, they will spread out along the ground and prevent weeds from growing. Additionally, these three foods: beans, corn and squash, will provide the human body with all the nutrition it needs to stay healthy.
paragraphs.zh: （当然，如果我能改变历史，我就 会告诉他们不要为烟草费心，那只 会是弊大于利！） 美洲原住民还发现，有三种作物的 组合效果非常好。玉米是一种坚韧 的植物，长得很高，能产出大量的 食物。但是它会吸收土壤中的氮。 然而，如果你在玉米旁边种下豆类， 豆类就会沿着玉米杆生长，并补充 氮元素。然后，如果你把南瓜种在 豆类旁边，它们就会沿着地面扩散， 防止杂草生长。 此外，这三种食物：豆类、玉米和 南瓜，为人体提供保持健康所需的 所有营养。

paragraphs.en: These three plants were such a powerful combination, that they formed the staple of most Native American diets, and were called "The Three Sisters". Anyway, farming began between 8000 and 5000 BC, and it caused an explosion in the population over the next several thousand years. We have seen this historical pattern several times before. In the lowlands of Mexico, along the Coatzacoalcos River, farming was particularly productive, because in this part of the world you can have 3 harvests in a year! This surplus let the population grow and grow until by 1500 BC, the first cities inAmerica had begun to develop. These cities belonged to a society
paragraphs.zh: 这三种植物组成了一个非常强大的 组合，它们构成了大多数美洲原住 民饮食的主食，被称为“三姐妹”。 总 之 ， 农 业 发 展 于 公 元 前 8000-5000 年间，在接下来的几千 年里，它引起了人口的爆炸性增长。 我们以前曾见过这种历史模式。 在墨西哥的低地，沿着夸察夸尔科 斯河，农业的生产效率特别高，因 为在这一地区，你可以在一年内收 获三次。 农作物盈余使得人口不断增长，到 了公元前 1500 年，美洲的第一批城 市开始发展起来了。 如今，我们将这些城市所属的社会

paragraphs.en: that we now call the "Olmec". The Olmec are probably most famous for the giant stone heads that they carved. These were first discovered in the 1930s, and they astonished the archaeologists who dug them up. Of course, the Olmec also made many things out of wood and other organic material, but in the wet climate of Mexico all of that has long since biodegraded.And we only have the ceramic and stone artifacts today. The first great Olmec city was at a place we now call San Lorenzo. Of course today it looks like a jungle, but 3000 years ago it was a thriving city. And we know this because of the amazing artifacts that we have
paragraphs.zh: 称为“奥尔梅克”。 奥尔梅克人最出名的可能是他们雕 刻的巨石。这些都是在 20 世纪 30 年代首次发现的，震惊了当时挖掘 的考古学家。 当然，奥尔梅克人也用木头和其他 有机材料制造了很多东西，但是在 墨西哥潮湿的气候下，所有这些都 早已被生物降解了。保留到今天的 只有陶瓷和石器。 第一个了不起的奥尔梅克城市位于 今天的圣洛伦索。当然，今天这里 已经一片荒芜，但 3000 年前，这里 曾是一个繁荣的城市。 我们之所以能知道这一点，是因为 我们在圣洛伦索发现了令人惊叹的

paragraphs.en: discovered there in San Lorenzo. We have discovered giant stone heads, like the one you see here. And we have also found masks carved out of jadeite. One theory is the heads are images of Olmec kings, and the masks were worn by the kings during religious ceremonies. As in other parts of the world, we see again the connection between religious power and political power. We have also found a mysterious carved stone block covered in symbols. This stone is called the Cascajal Block, and it might be the earliest writing inAmerica, but like the Vinca symbols in Serbia or the Jiahu symbols in China, we just don't have enough evidence to be sure.
paragraphs.zh: 文物。 我们发现了巨大的石制头像，就像 你在这里看到的一样。我们还发现 了用翡翠雕刻的面具。 有一种理论认为，这些头像是奥尔 梅克国王的肖像，在宗教仪式上， 国王会佩戴这种面具。 和世界其他地方一样，我们再次看 到了宗教力量与政治权力之间的联 系。 我们还发现了一块刻有神秘符号的 石砌块——卡司卡哈尔石块。它可 能承载着美洲大路上最古老的文 字，一如塞尔维亚的温卡符号和中 国的贾湖契刻符号。我们只是缺少 足够的证据来证实这一点。

paragraphs.en: Afew dozen kilometers to the south of San Lorenzo is another archaeological site called El Manati. This area is a volcanic lake and peat bog, where the Olmec tossed sacrificial objects into sacred pools as an offering to the gods. Fortunately, the low-oxygen environment of the peat bog has preserved some of the only wooden artifacts from the Olmec society. And even more astonishing, it has preserved several rubber balls! 3000 years ago, the Olmec learned how to use the local rubber trees and create the worlds first rubber objects! One of the things they made were rubber balls, which they used in a sport we now call the
paragraphs.zh: 在圣洛伦索以南几十公里的地方是 另一个考古遗址——马纳蒂遗址。 这个地区是一个火山湖和泥炭沼 泽。在此，奥尔梅克人将祭祀物品 扔进神圣的水池，作为献给神的祭 品。 幸运的是，泥炭沼泽的低氧环境保 留了奥尔梅克社会中仅有的一些木 制文物。 更让人吃惊的是，它还保存了几个 橡皮球！ 3000 年前，奥尔梅克人学会了使用 当地的橡胶树，并创造出了世界上 第一件橡胶制品！ 他们创造的其中一件物品就是橡皮 球。用于一项运动，我们现在称之 为“中美洲球赛”。

paragraphs.en: "Mesoamerican Ball Game ". Playing courts for this sport are found in archaeological sites all across Mexico. Then about 900 BC, San Lorenzo declined, and a new Olmec city developed: La Venta. Here is what the archaeological site looks like today. Unlike San Lorenzo, La Venta was planned. It had clearly defined streets, monumental carved statues, like "The Grandmother" statue you see on the right. And at the center of the city was the very first pyramid in theAmericas: the Great Pyramid of La Venta. There are all sorts of monumental buildings at La Venta. Most of them built out of imported stone.
paragraphs.zh: 考古学家在墨西哥各地的考古遗址 中都发现了这项运动的场地。 大约在公元前 900 年，圣洛伦索衰 败了，另一个奥尔梅克城市兴起了： 拉本塔。 如今，该考古遗址的以如此样貌呈 现。 与圣洛伦索不同，拉本塔是一座提 前规划好的城市。城内有分明的街 道和纪念雕像。比如右图中的“祖 母雕像”。 城市中心坐落着美洲的第一座金字 塔：拉本塔金字塔。 拉本塔城中有各种各样宏伟的建 筑，其中大部分都是由进口的石料 制成的。

paragraphs.en: Here is a map of La Venta and a picture of another monumental structure called ComplexA. We have also discovered carved altars that played some sort of religious function, and the earliest example of the Mesoamerican dragon figure. Another thing that we've noticed at La Venta, is that the people tended to bury many of their artifacts. One example would be the buried mosaic in the picture on the right, or the Offering Number 4 in the picture on the left. Offering Number 4 is a collection of many carved serpentine stones. But this type of stone is not found near La Venta; it needs to be imported from over 650 kilometers away, so it would have been very expensive.
paragraphs.zh: 这是一张拉本塔的地图，以及另一 个名为“Complex A”的复杂建筑。 我们还发现了一些有雕刻的祭坛， 它扮演了某种宗教的角色，也是中 美洲龙最早的例子。 我们还注意到另一件事：拉本塔人 似乎倾向于埋葬其手工艺品。其中 一个例子是右图中的镶嵌细工，以 及左图中的 4 号祭品。 4 号祭品是多件蛇纹石雕刻的合 集。但拉本塔并不生产这种石头； 它需要从 650 公里以外的地方进 口，所以必然非常昂贵。

paragraphs.en: By burying these stones, the King of La Venta would have been making symbolic ritual sacrifices, much the same way that ancient Kings in other cultures like to be buried with gold. The source of the Serpentine stone was a place that we now call "Chalcatzingo." This settlement was NOT an Olmec town. Instead it was one of their trading partners, located hundreds of kilometers away in the mountains. This is proof that the Olmec were connected to the rest of Mexico by extensive trade networks. They probably traded their rubber and corn for items that they lacked like stone.
paragraphs.zh: 埋葬这些石头的同时，拉本塔国王 还会做一些象征性的祭司行为，就 如同其他文化中的帝王喜欢与金子 一同下葬一样。 蛇纹石的来源地，如今被称为查尔 卡钦戈。 查尔卡钦戈并不是奥尔梅克城镇。 相反，它是奥尔梅克的贸易伙伴之 一，位于数百公里外的山区。 这证明了奥尔梅克人通过广泛的贸 易网络与墨西哥其他地区联系在一 起。他们可能用其橡胶和玉米来交 换他们缺少的东西。

paragraphs.en: Here is what Chalcotzingo looks like today.As you can see it is located up in the mountains, not down in the jungle like La Venta. But Chalcotzingo is also interesting because it has an Olmec style ball court.And it has Olmec-style rock carvings, like this one of a jaguar attacking a man. The ball game would have required an Olmec-made rubber ball, and there are no Jaguars near Chalcotzingo. Both of these prove that Olmec cultural influence spread far beyond its core territory. But then, around 400 BC, Olmec society entered into a decline and collapsed. The reasons for this are unclear,
paragraphs.zh: 这就是查尔卡钦戈如今的样子。正 如你所看到的，它位于山中，而不 是像拉本塔那样位于丛林。 但查尔卡钦戈也很有趣，因为它有 一个奥尔梅克风格的球场，还有奥 尔梅克风格的石刻，例如图片上这 个：一只美洲虎正在攻击一个人。 想要完成一场球赛，就需要一个奥 尔梅克的球。并且，查尔卡钦戈附 近并没有美洲虎。 这两种情况都证明了奥尔梅克文化 的影响远远超出了其核心领域。 但是，大约在公元前 400 年，奥尔 梅克社会陷入衰退，最终土崩瓦解 了。 造成这种情况的原因尚不清楚，也

paragraphs.en: maybe it was a drought, maybe it was a series of volcanic eruptions. But the major settlements like La Venta were abandoned, and Olmec society disappeared. But the Olmec collapse did not mean the end of civilization in Mexico. People kept living in that part of the world and farming, and many aspects of Olmec culture, like its artistic style, or the Mesoamerican Ball Game, continued. And then new societies emerged as the successors to the Olmec. But that of course is a story for our next class. Okey everyone,I hope you enjoyed this lesson.And I hope you are curious to learn more.I have some
paragraphs.zh: 许是干旱，也许是一系列的火山爆 发。 但是像拉本塔这样的主要定居点被 遗弃了，奥尔梅克社会也消失了。 但是奥尔梅克的衰竭并不意味着墨 西哥文明的终结。人们继续生活在 世界的这一地区，并保持耕种。奥 尔梅克文化的许多方面，比如它的 艺术风格、中美洲球赛等，都延续 了下来。 然后，新的社会成为了奥尔梅克的 继承者。 但这将是我们下节课的一个故事。 希望你们喜欢这节课。如果想要了 解更多，我有一些不错的推荐。

paragraphs.en: recommendations for you if that’s the case. If you want to see Olmec artifacts in person,the best museum in the world is the ZalapaAnthropology Museum in Vero-Cruz,Mexico. I also highly recommend a documentary series by the BBC called “The Lost Kingdoms of CentralAmerica”. Ok,that’s it.Thank you very much.
paragraphs.zh: 如果你想亲眼见到奥尔梅克的文 武，最好的去处是位于墨西哥维拉 克鲁兹的萨拉帕人类学博物馆。 我还强烈推荐 BBC 的系列记录片 “中美洲失落的王国”。 今天就到这里，谢谢大家！

titleZh: 第二讲：玛雅文明|末日未至，预言已失

paragraphs.en: Hello everyone and welcome back to our lecture series on the Native Americans. Today we will learn about the great civilization which followed the Olmec: the Maya. We will focus this lecture on the 1300 years between 400 BC and 900AD, as this is the time between the collapse of the Olmec and the collapse of the Classical Mayan Civilization. This is what that looks like on our timeline. In our last lecture we ended our story around 400 BC, when the Olmec civilization collapsed, and their great city of La Venta was abandoned. As I mentioned last time, the cause of this collapse is still unknown.
paragraphs.zh: 大家好，欢迎回到我们关于美洲原 住民的系列课程。今天我们将一起 来学习继奥尔梅克文化之后的又一 个伟大文明：玛雅文明。 让我们看看日历。从公元前 400 年 奥尔梅克文明的崩溃，再到公元900 年古玛雅文明的崩溃，在这段长达 1300 年的时间里，发生了什么呢？ 上节课我们讲到，在大约公元前400 年，奥尔梅克文明崩溃，他们的大 城市拉文塔被荒废了。 目前还不知道是什么原因导致了文 明的崩溃。

paragraphs.en: But this collapse did not mean the end of the Olmec people. Instead their society changed, absorbed new immigrants, and developed into a different culture. The next 600 years of history, between 350 BC and 250AD are known as the Epi-Olmec period. The Epi-Olmec built new settlements during this time, like Tres Zapotes or Cerro de las Mesas. And in some ways, the Epi-Olmec even surpassed their ancestors. The most significant example of that is this artifact. It is a stone stele called the "The Mojarra Stele 1" On the left side is an intricate carving of a man in ceremonial dress. But on the right side is
paragraphs.zh: 但这场崩溃并不意味着奥尔梅克人 的灭绝。相反，他们的社会吸收了 新移民，渐渐发展成为了一种新的 文化。 接下来的公元前 350 年到公元 250 年之间的这段 600 年历史，被我们 称为后奥尔梅克时期。 在后奥尔梅克时期，人们建立了像 特雷斯·扎普提斯或塞罗·德拉斯 梅萨斯这样新的定居点。 从某个角度来说，后奥尔梅克人的 成就甚至超越了他们的前人。最有 力的证据就是这个工件。 这是一个石碑，叫做"莫加拉石碑 石碑左面雕刻着一个穿着礼服的 人，非常复杂精细。石碑右面的雕 刻则更有价值：它是中美洲历史上

paragraphs.en: something even more advanced: the first example of writing in the history of Mesoamerica. And we can even decipher the date on this monument. By our calendar it corresponds to 143AD. Then, about 100 years later, and a little to the east of the Olmec territory, in the Yucatan peninsula, a new culture began to rise, and build great cities. These people were the Mayans. The Mayans are probably most famous for their great stone pyramid temples, built in cities like Calakmul, Chichen Itza, and Tikal. When Classical Mayan civilization collapsed around 900AD, these cities were abandoned, and the houses of the common people rotted away, because they were made out
paragraphs.zh: 最早的文字记录。 我们甚至可以破译这座纪念碑上的 日期。它相当于公元 143 年。 大约 100 年后，在奥尔梅克以东的 尤卡坦半岛，一个新的文明开始崛 起，并建立了大城市。他们就是玛 雅人。 玛雅人最著名的可能就是他们的巨 石金字塔神庙了，这些建筑分布在 卡拉克穆尔, 奇琴伊察和蒂卡尔这 样的城市里。 公元 900 年左右，随着古玛雅文明 的崩溃，这些城市遭到废弃。普通 居民的房子很快腐烂了，因为它们 是用木头、茅草和泥土建成的。

paragraphs.en: of wood and thatch and mud. But the great stone pyramids survived, even as the trees grew back and those cities became jungle again. But then the outside world began to re-discover these places in the 19th century. Explorers found these giant stone buildings, overgrown with vegetation, and sitting all alone in the middle of the wilderness. And so began the broader world's fascination with the Classical Mayan civilization. And even more exciting, there was writing carved into those great stone buildings! The Mayans were the only Native American culture to develop a full
paragraphs.zh: 但是即使树木重新占领城市，这些 巨石金字塔仍然在丛林中幸存了下 来。 这些地方在 19 世纪被外部世界重 新发现了。 探险家们到来时，这些巨石建筑长 满了植物，孤零零地坐立在荒野之 上。 外界的人们被古玛雅文明所深深吸 引了。 更令人兴奋的是，在那些巨大的石 头建筑上居然雕刻有文字！ 玛雅人是唯一发展出了完整文字体 系的美洲原住民。

paragraphs.en: writing system. Admittedly, this system was complex and involved learning over 700 different symbols. So it was restricted to the elite. But it was a true writing system. Think about that. The idea of writing was only invented by 4 different societies in all of human history: TheAncient Egyptians and Sumerians in 3000 BC, theAncient Chinese in 1300 BC, and the Mayans around 100AD. Every writing system in the world can be traced back to those 4 origin points. According to what I've read, each symbol is called a "glyph" and it represents a noun-phrase or a verb phrase. Each line is 2 glyphs long and you read from left to right and
paragraphs.zh: 诚然，这个书写体系需要学习超过 700 种不同的符号，非常复杂，所 以它仅限于精英阶层。但这是一个 实打实的文字系统。 想一想。在人类历史上，只有 4 个 文明创造了文字：公元前 3000 年的 古埃及人和苏美尔人，公元前 1300 年的古中国人和公元 100 年左右的 玛雅人。 世界上所有的文字系统都可以追溯 到这四个源点。 据我了解，每个符号都被称为“字 形”，它代表一个名词短语或一个动 词短语。每一行有 2 个字形，书写 顺序是从左到右，从上到下。写到 了这两列的底部，接下来就跳到下

paragraphs.en: top to bottom. Then once you come to the bottom of the two columns you jump over to the top of the next two columns. But the Mayans did not just make carvings in stone, they also had their own type of paper and their own books! They made a special kind of bark paper from the inner bark of the Amate tree, which is native to Mexico. On this slide, I have a picture of the tree of the far left, and a picture of this bark paper in the middle.And on the right is an example of one of these Mayan books, which today we call "The Dresden Codex". But as I did the research for this lesson, I learned something very sad. Only 4 Mayan books still exist
paragraphs.zh: 两列的顶部。 但玛雅人不只是在石头上雕刻，他 们甚至有纸和书！ 他们用原产于墨西哥的阿马特树的 树皮为原料，制作了一种特殊的树 皮纸。 在这张幻灯片上，最左边就是这种 树的图片，中间是一张树皮纸。右 边是一本玛雅人的书，我们称它为 “德累斯顿抄本”。 但是当我为这节课做研究的时候， 我了解到了令人遗憾的消息。当下 只有 4 本玛雅书籍幸存于世：德累

paragraphs.en: today: The Dresden Codex, the Madrid Codex, the Paris Codex and the Grolier Codex. That's it, that's all that survives of an entire civilization's paper literature! (These glyphs you see on that paper - they were all drawn by hand. It took a lot of artistic skill to write Mayan.) The reason for this is the Spanish. After they conquered the Maya in the 1500s, Spanish officials and priests regarded their literature as worthless and evil, and they destroyed it. One of the great destroyers of Mayan culture was Bishop Diego de Landa who wrote these words about how he burned the Mayan books:
paragraphs.zh: 斯顿抄本、马德里抄本、巴黎抄本 和格罗里埃抄本。 这四本书就是整个文明的仅存的所 有纸质作品！ （这些抄本上的文字都是手写的。 书写玛雅文字需要很强的艺术技 巧。） 这一切的始作俑者是西班牙人。他 们在 16 世纪征服了玛雅人之后，西 班牙的官员和牧师认为玛雅文学作 品毫无价值且内容邪恶，于是将其 毁灭。 玛雅文化的主要破坏者之一是迭 戈·德·兰达主教，他写下了这些 关于他在焚烧玛雅书籍时的记录：

paragraphs.en: “We found a large number of books in these characters and, as they contained nothing in which were not to be seen as superstition and lies of the devil, we burned them all, which they (the Maya) regretted to an amazing degree, and which caused them much affliction.” The Spanish destroyed over 99% of Mayan literature.All that survives are four short books, and the stone carvings on the Classical temples. So we are missing a great deal of information about the Maya. In fact, Michael D. Coe, an Anthropologist and Professor at Yale who is a expert on the Maya, has made this comparison: “Our knowledge of ancient Maya thought must represent only a tiny fraction of the whole picture, for of
paragraphs.zh: “我们在这些人身上发现了大量的 书籍，由于书中充斥着迷信的内容 和魔鬼的谎言，我们将其付之一炬， 玛雅人对此痛惜不已，这对他们是 一种极大的折磨。” 西班牙人摧毁了超过 99%的玛雅文 学。现存的只有那四本书籍，以及 古典庙宇上的石刻。 所以我们失去了很多关于玛雅人的 信息。事实上，人类学家、耶鲁大 学教授、玛雅问题专家迈克·D·科 做了这样的比较: “我们对于古代玛雅人的认识只是 冰山一角，成千上万的书籍记录下 了他们的知识和宗教仪式，却只有

paragraphs.en: the thousands of books in which the full extent of their learning and ritual was recorded, only four have survived to modern times, as though all that posterity knew of ourselves were to be based upon three prayer books and Pilgrims Progress.” So our records are fragmentary.And we can still only decipher about 80% of the Mayan words. But even so, we have learned some fascinating things about them. For one thing, we know that the Mayans used a base-20 number system, rather than base-10 as we do. They probably chose 20 because that is the number of fingers and toes. The Mayans wrote their numbers
paragraphs.zh: 四本得以幸存。这就好比我们的后 人仅仅通过三本祈祷书和《天路历 程》来了解我们。” 所以我们的记录是零碎的。我们目 前只能破译大约 80%的玛雅文字。 但即便如此，我们还是了解到了一 些有趣的内容。 首先，我们知道玛雅人使用的是 20 进制的数字系统，这和我们的 10 进 制不同。 他们之所以选择这个数字，可能因 为 20 是手指和脚趾的数目之和。 玛雅人用 3 个符号来书写数字：一

paragraphs.en: with 3 symbols: a dot represented one, a bar represented 5, and a shell-symbol represented 0 and was used as a place holder. The Mayans wrote their numbers vertically. Each row represented a power of 20 instead of a power of 10 like we use. So the bottom row represented multiples of 1. The second row represented multiples of 20. The third row represented multiples of 400.And the fourth row represented multiples of 8000. Here are some examples of how that system worked. These are the Mayan equivalents for 806; 10,145; 2005; and 16,125. The Mayans were also good at astronomy.And they actually had 3 different calendar systems.
paragraphs.zh: 个点代表 1，一个条代表 5，一个贝 壳代表 0，也可以当做占位符使用。 玛雅人书写数字是由上而下的。每 一行表示 20 的幂，这也和我们使用 10 的幂不同。 最下面一行表示 1 的倍数。第二行 表示 20 的倍数。第三行表示 400 的 倍数。第四行是 8000 的倍数。 这里有一些例子帮助你理解怎么读 懂玛雅人的数字。这些玛雅数字等 同于我们的 806；10145；2005 和 玛雅人在天文学方面也颇有成就。 他们有三个不同的日历系统。

paragraphs.en: Like all agricultural societies, the Mayans knew that the year was 365 days long. So they had a 365-day solar calendar like we do. They called this calendar the "Haab." But the Mayans did not divide the year into 30-day months based on the moon. Instead, the Mayan months were 20-days long.And instead of just using their numbers, they created 20 special symbols for each day of the month. Then they divided the year into 18 months, and each of the 18 months also had its own special symbol in Mayan writing. But of course 18 times 20 is only 360 days. So the Mayans added 5 special days at the end of the year.
paragraphs.zh: 和所有的农耕文明一样，玛雅人知 道一年有 365 天。他们的阳历被称 为“哈布历”。 但是玛雅人并没有根据月亮来将一 年划分为 12 个月，每月 30 天。 相反，玛雅人的一个月只有 20 天。 他们不使用数字，而是为一个月里 的每一天创造了 20 个特殊的符号。 然后他们将一年分为 18 个月，这 18个月中的每个月在玛雅文字中也 都有各自的特殊符号。 由于 18 乘以 20 是 360 天，所以玛 雅人在年底增加了 5 个特殊的日 子。这几天被认为是不吉利的。

paragraphs.en: These days were considered unlucky. But the Solar "Haab" calendar was not the only Mayan calendar. They also had a special ritual calendar called the "Tzolk'in". This calendar existed because the Mayans also had a reverence for the number 13. We speculate that this is because the human body has 13 major joints: The neck, the shoulders, the elbows, the wrists, the hips, the knees and the ankles. So if you multiply 13 by 20 you get 260, which was the number of days in the Tzolk'in Calendar. This calendar may have developed because 260 is also the approximate length of a pregnancy, or at least the
paragraphs.zh: 但是，太阳历“哈布历”并不是玛 雅人唯一的历法。他们还有一种特 殊的仪式日历，叫做“卓尔金历”。 这一历法之所以存在，是因为玛雅 人对数字 13 怀有崇敬之情。 我们猜测，这是由于人体有 13 个主 要关节，包括脖子，肩膀，肘部， 手腕，臀部，膝盖和脚踝。 13 乘以 20 得到 260，这就是卓尔金 历上的天数。 这个历法的发展也可能是因为 260 天是怀孕的大概时长，或者说是女 性最后一次月经到婴儿出生间隔的

paragraphs.en: time between the first missed period and birth. But of course 365 and 260 do not match up very well. The least common multiple for these numbers is 18,980. That means that it takes 52 years for the calendars to line up again. This means that each day can have a Haab-calendar date, and a Tzolkin-calendar date, and the combination of the two will not repeat for 52 years. And since 52 years is a good portion of a human life, you can think of this system as giving each day in your life a distinct name. The Haab date is its first name, the Tzolkin date is its second name. This might seem like a strange
paragraphs.zh: 时间。 当然 365 和 260 这两个数字并不相 匹配。这两个数的最小公倍数是 也就是说两个日历需要 52 年才能 再次重合。 这意味着每一天都可以由一个哈布 历的日期和一个卓尔金历的日期组 成，并且这个组合在未来 52 年内都 不带重样的。 人生并没有多少个 52 年，因此在这 个日历系统里，你生命中的每一天 都是独一无二的。哈布历日是姓， 卓尔金历日是名。 乍看之下，这个日历系统似乎非常

paragraphs.en: system at first, but the traditional Chinese calendar does something similar, except it does this for years instead of days. That calendar combines the 12 ZodiacAnimals with the 5 Elements. Each year gets its own elementAND animal, and the system repeats itself every 60 years instead of every 52 years. For example, 2018 is an Earth-Dog year. But so was 1958. Of course this system only works for a period of 52 years. If you want to keep track of longer periods of time you need another system.And this is why the Mayans had a third calendar called the "Long Count calendar." In this system 1 Day was called a K'in.
paragraphs.zh: 奇怪，但中国的传统历法其实也有 相似之处。不同之处在于，它是以 年而不是天为周期循环的。 这种历法结合了十二生肖和金木水 火土五种元素。每一年都由一种元 素和生肖组成，这个历法每 60 年重 复一次，而不是每 52 年重复一次。 例如，2018 年和 1958 年一样，都 是土-狗年。 当然，这个历法只能以 52 年为周期 来记录。如果想要记录更长的时间， 就需要另一个历法。因此玛雅人还 有第三种历法，叫做“长计历”。 在这个系统中，一天被称为金。

paragraphs.en: 20 Days were a "Winal", the Mayan month. 18 Winals were a "Tun", or 360 days, so a Tun was very close to a solar year. 20 Tun were a K'atun, or 7200 days. And 20 K'atun were a B'ak'tun or 144,000 days, which is about 394 years. Now the Mayans believed that the Earth was created onAugust 11th, 3114 BC. This meant that the first Baktun was completed on November, 13, 2720 BC. The second Baktun was completed on Feb 16th, 2325 BC. The third Baktun was completed on May 21st, 1931 BC.
paragraphs.zh: 20 天是一个“乌内尔”，也就是玛 雅人的一个月。 18 乌内尔是一“盾”，即 360 天， 所以一盾的长度接近一年。 20 盾是一卡盾，也就是 7200 天。 20 卡盾是一伯克盾，或者说 144000 天，大约是 394 年。 玛雅人认为地球是在公元前 3114 年 8 月 11 日诞生的。 这意味着第一个伯克盾结束于公元 前 2720 年 11 月 13 日。 第二个伯克盾结束于公元前 2325 年 2 月 16 日。 第三个伯克盾结束于公元前 1931 年 5 月 21 日。

paragraphs.en: And so on... If you look carefully at the calendar you can see that the 12th Baktun was completed on September 18th, 1618AD. And the 13th Baktun was completed just a few years ago on December 21st, 2012. This wasALSO the first day of winter that year. Because the Mayan's have a special reverence for the number 13, that date was very special for them. This is similar to how the whole world had a special celebration on January 1st, 2000. I created this lesson in the Year 2018, in the 8th month of that year, on the 30th day of that month. But for the classical Mayans, that same date would be the 13th
paragraphs.zh: 如此循环下去… 如果你再看看日历，你会发现第 12 个伯克盾结束于1618年9月18日。 第 13 个伯克盾是在几年前的 2012 年 12 月 21 日结束的。这也是那年 冬季的第一天。 因为玛雅人对数字 13 有一种特殊 的崇敬之情，所以这一天对他们来 说非常特别。这就好比全世界的人 们在2000年1月1日都举行了庆祝 活动。 我在 2018 年的第 8 个月的第 30 天 编写了这门课程。 但对于一个玛雅人来说，这一天是 创世以来的第13个伯克盾的第0个

paragraphs.en: Baktun, the 0th Katun, the 5th Tun, the 13th Winal, and the 18th Day since the creation of the world. But, for all of their mathematical, astronomical and literary sophistication, the Mayans were still limited by one major problem. Like the ancient Sumerians and Greeks, they were divided into different city states.And these states were constantly at war with each other. The fragments of Mayan history that do survive are filled with tales of war, conquest, murder, alliances, peace treaties and betrayals. The main focus for all the Mayan kings was fighting wars with other Mayan Kings. They also had to conduct a foreign
paragraphs.zh: 卡盾的第5个盾的第13个乌内尔的 第 18 天。 但是，尽管玛雅人在数学、天文学 和文学方面都取得了成就，但他们 的发展仍然受到一个主要问题的限 制。 就像古苏美尔人和古希腊人一样， 他们是由不同的城邦组成的。各个 政权之间经常发生战争。 玛雅文明存留下来的历史碎片充满 了战争、征服、谋杀、结盟、和谈 和背叛的故事。 所有玛雅文明的统治者都把精力放 在和其他统治者交战上。 他们还不得不在外交上与强大的特

paragraphs.en: policy and deal with the powerful city of Teotihuacan, further to the west in central Mexico. We know from temple inscriptions that Teotihuacan would sometimes meddle in internal Mayan politics, and they even organized a coup in Tikal is 378AD. This period of great city states with monumental stone buildings, is known as the "Classical Mayan Period" and it lasted for over 600 years, from the 3rd centuryAD until the 10th centuryAD. But then in the early 900s, the Classical Mayan civilization ran into trouble. We think that they experienced a severe drought and a long environmental crisis that harmed the production of food. Since the Mayan Kings were
paragraphs.zh: 奥蒂瓦坎打交道。特奥蒂瓦坎城位 于墨西哥中西部。从神庙铭文中我 们了解到，特奥蒂瓦坎有时会干涉 玛雅人的内部政治，公元 378 年他 们甚至在蒂卡尔策动了一场政变。 公元3-10世纪的这600多年被称为 “古玛雅时期”，在此期间，玛雅文 明创造了无数的伟大城邦与不朽的 石砌建筑。 但是到了 9 世纪初，古玛雅文明陷 入了危机。我们推测他们经历了严 重的干旱和长期的环境危机，严重 影响了粮食生产。 玛雅的统治者由于受传统思想的限

paragraphs.en: limited by ancient traditions, and fixated upon warfare, they were unable to organize a response to this crisis. And then in the 900s the entire system collapsed. The population plummeted, the Kings lost power, and many of the great cities were abandoned. One of those great cities is called Toniná today, and in that city there is a monument called Monument 101. On this monument we see written the very last long-count date. It was carved in the year 909 AD. After that, the Kings of Tonina stopped building these types of monuments and a few decades later the city was abandoned. But the end of the classical Mayan
paragraphs.zh: 制，专注于战争，他们并没有及时 地组织应对好这场危机。 到了 9 世纪末，社会秩序终于崩溃 了。玛雅文明的人口锐减，统治者 失去权力，许多大城市被遗弃了。 其中一个城市现在叫作托尼纳。在 那里有一个纪念碑，叫做“纪念碑 101”。这座纪念碑上刻有最后一个 长计历日期。它雕刻于公元 909 年。 在那之后，托尼纳的统治者没有再 建造这类纪念碑，几十年后，这座 城市被遗弃了。 但古玛雅时期的结束并不意味着玛

paragraphs.en: period did not mean the end of the Mayan people. Mayan society recovered and their population bounced back. Two of the greatest cities during this time period were Zaculeu and Mayapan. This next period of Mayan society is called the Post-classic period, and it lasted another 6 centuries, from around 950AD until 1511AD. But then, in 1511, the Spanish arrived. Over the next 200 years they conquered the Mayans, and defeated the last independent Mayan Kingdom in 1697. As we've already seen, this had a devastating impact on Mayan culture. But the diseases brought by the Spanish, like Smallpox, also
paragraphs.zh: 雅人的灭绝。 随着玛雅社会的复苏，他们的人口 数量重新上升。 这一时期最主要的两个城市是扎克 鲁和玛雅潘。 玛雅社会的下一个时期被称为后古 时期，它从公元 950 年到 1511 年， 又持续了 6 个世纪。 在 1511 年，西班牙人踏上了这片地 区。在接下来的 200 年里，他们征 服了玛雅人，并在 1697 年打败了最 后一个独立的玛雅王国。 正如我们已经看到的，这对玛雅文 化产生了毁灭性的影响。西班牙人 带来的疾病，比如天花，也使玛雅 人几乎灭绝。

paragraphs.en: decimated the Mayan population. The chart on the right is for the population in Central Mexico, but the situation in the Yucatan was quite similar. But although the Spanish conquest was a terrible event in Mayan history, perhaps the greatest calamity they ever faced, it did not wipe them out as a people, or a culture, or a language. The Mayan survived as a people, and preserved their language, which is a big reason why we can decipher their ancient writing. There are 6 million Mayan people living in the world today. In fact, people of Mayan descent are 40% of the Population of Guatemala, and 60% of the Inhabitant of the Yucatan peninsula.
paragraphs.zh: 右边的图表是墨西哥中部地区的人 口，尤卡坦半岛的情况也与此很相 似。 但是，尽管西班牙的征服是玛雅人 历史上的一个劫难，甚至是可以说 是灭顶之灾，但最终并没有使玛雅 人的民族、文化及语言灭绝。 玛雅人作为一个民族幸存了下来， 并且保留了他们的语言。这是我们 能够破译他们古代文字的一个重要 原因。 目前，世界上有 600 万玛雅人。事 实上，玛雅人后裔占危地马拉人口 的 40%，尤卡坦半岛居民的 60%。

paragraphs.en: Okay everyone, I am afraid we are out of time. There are many more societies and civilizations that I would like to discuss. There are the Teotihuacan people, who I only briefly mentioned a few slides ago. There are the Zapotec people, also in Mesoamerica. In SouthAmerica there is the Norte Chico culture, or the later Calima culture. And then in NorthAmerica there are people like the Hopewell culture, theAnasazi, and the culture at Poverty point. Not to mention Caribbean cultures like the Taino. But we have many more parts of the
paragraphs.zh: 好了各位，尽管这节课的时间已经 不多了，但是我还有更多的社会和 文明想和你们讨论。 比如我在前面简单提到过的特奥蒂 瓦坎人。 还有同样在中美洲的萨巴特克人。 在南美洲还有小北文明，或后来的 卡利玛文明。 在北美，则有霍普韦尔文化，阿纳 萨齐文化，还有波弗蒂角地区的原 住民文化。 更不用说加勒比海地区的泰诺人文 化了。 但是我们还有更多的地方需要讨

paragraphs.en: world that we need to discuss. So we will have to leaveAmerica behind and turn our attention back to Eurasia. I hope that this lesson has made you curious to learn more about the great civilizations and fascinating societies of the NativeAmericans. Please join us next time as we return to the Middle East and learn about two people who filled the power vacuum left by the Bronze Age Collapse, the Phoenicians and the Jews. Now,if you want to learn more,I have some recommendations for you. The first is a video called "Deciphering the Mayan Script", which was a lecture by Michael D Coe at Berkley on how they learned
paragraphs.zh: 论。因此，我们将不得不暂时离开 美洲，将注意力重新转向欧亚大陆。 我希望这一课能让你们对伟大而迷 人的美洲原住民文明和社会产生兴 趣。 下节课我们将回到中东地区，一起 来了解两个文明：腓尼基人和犹太 人，正是他们填补了青铜时代崩溃 后留下来的权力真空。 如果你想了解更多，我推荐一些资 源给你。 首先是名为“破译玛雅文字”的视 频，这是迈克尔·D·科在伯克利的 一场演讲，内容是关于他们如何学 习玛雅语。

paragraphs.en: to read Mayan. The second is a BBC documentary called "Spirits of the Jaguar 2: Forest of the Maya". I also recommend National Geographic's "Palenque and the Ancient Mayan Word" and the book Collapse: How Societies Choose to Fail or Succeed by Jared Diamond. Okay, thank you very much!
paragraphs.zh: 其次是 BBC 纪录片“美洲豹 2：玛 雅森林的精灵”。 我还推荐（美国）国家地理杂志的 “帕伦克和古玛雅语”和贾雷德·戴 蒙德的《崩溃:社会如何选择成败兴 亡》。 谢谢大家！

titleZh: 第三讲：腓尼基人与犹太人

paragraphs.en: Hello everyone, welcome back to our lecture series on World History. Today we are going to learn about two groups of people, who although few in number, had an outsized influence on human history. The period we’ll be focusing on is the 500 years between 1,200 and 700 BC. Here is what that looks like on our timeline. If you remember, our last series ended with the BronzeAge collapse, which occurred in the decades between 1250 BC and 1150 BC. This was followed by a dark age which lasted for nearly 400 years, ending only around the 9th century BC.
paragraphs.zh: 大家好，欢迎回到世界历史系列课 程。今天，我们将学习两大人群。 他们虽然人数不多，但对人类历史 有着巨大的影响。 我们将视野聚焦在公元前 1200 年 到公元前 700 年的五百年间，这是 这段时间在时间线上的样子。 我们的上一期课程以青铜时代的崩 溃结束，它发生在公元前1250-1150 年之间。紧随其后的是一个持续了 近 400 年的黑暗时代，直到公元前 9 世纪才结束。

paragraphs.en: As you can see from this map, the Eastern Mediterranean was a complex system of many different advanced societies that traded, fought wars and conducted diplomacy with each other. This is what that world looked like in 1300 BC, before the collapse. But this is what is looked like in 1100 BC, a hundred years afterwards. Only the Egyptian and Assyrian civilizations survived the crisis, and Egypt's power shrank back to only the Nile River itself. With the Egyptians gone in Canaan, there was now a power vacuum in the region that allowed new people to move in and compete for power. With the Mycenaeans gone in Greece there was also an opportunity for another people to
paragraphs.zh: 从这张地图上可以看出，东地中海 地区形成了一个复杂的系统，由许 多不同的先进社会组成，它们相互 交易、外交，也打过仗。这就是公 元前 1300 年，青铜时代崩溃之前的 世界。 但几百年后，到了公元前 1100 年， 这片区域变成了这样。只有埃及和 亚述文明在危机中幸存了下来，尼 罗河成为埃及仅存的力量。 随着埃及人离开迦南，该地区出现 了权力真空，允许新的人群进入并 争夺权力。 随着迈塞纳人离开希腊，其他民族 也有机会领导地中海的商业和航 海。

paragraphs.en: became the leading merchants and sailors of the Mediterranean Sea. After 1200 BC, both of these roles were filled by tribes of people who spoke Semitic languages. The first group were called the Phoenicians. They lived along the coast in the northern part of the region. The word "Phoenician" comes from the Greek word "Phoinike" which means "purple". In fact, another name for this part of the world is "Canaan", which comes from the Hurrian word "Kinahhu" which also means "purple". And this part of the world was given that name, because a predatory snail can be found on its beaches: Murex brandaris. If you gather hundreds of
paragraphs.zh: 在公元前 1200 年之后，这两个角色 都是由说着闪族语言的人来承担 的。 首先是腓尼基人。他们住在该地区 北部的沿海地区。 “腓尼基”这个词来源于希腊词 “Phoinike”，意思是“紫色”。 事实上，这个地区的另一个名字是 “迦南”，来源于胡利安语中的 “Kinahhu”，意思也是“紫色”。 该地区之所以会得到如此名称，是 因为海滩上有一种食肉性的贝类： 染料骨螺。如果你收集数百个染料 骨螺，就能够制作出一种名为“骨

paragraphs.en: them together, it is possible to make a reddish/purple dye called "Tyrian Purple" out of their bodies. This dye was a valuable trade good, desired all across the world.And it gave the Phoenicians something very valuable with which to trade. So the Phoenicians began building excellent ships out of the rot-resistant cedar trees that grow in their part of the world. During the Dark age, they became the best sailors in the world and began restoring overseas trade. Here on the left is a carving of a Phoenicians ship from a sarcophagus, and on this right is an illustration of one of those ships as it leaves a port. The Phoenicians focused their trade on the southern half of the
paragraphs.zh: 螺紫”的红紫色染料。 这种染料是一种有价值的贸易商 品，在全世界都很受欢迎。它令腓 尼基人有了贸易的资本。 于是，腓尼基人开始用当地特产的 耐腐蚀性香柏木制造极好的船只。 在黑暗时代，他们成为了世界上最 好的水手，并开始恢复海外贸易。 左是是石棺上雕刻的腓尼基人的 船，右边是其中一艘船离开港口的 插图。 腓尼基人把他们的贸易集中在地中 海的南半部。他们与迦南、埃及、

paragraphs.en: Mediterranean. They traded with Canaan, Egypt, NorthAfrica, Spain and the Mediterranean Islands. Later on, they conducted more and more trade with Greece as the Greek economy recovered from the collapse. The Phoenicians also set up colonies. They established successful settlements on Cyprus, NorthAfrica, Southern Spain, Western Sicily, Sardinia and several other islands. One of their greatest colonies was founded in NorthAfrica, in what is now modern Tunisia. This city was called "Carthage" and it will play a big role in our story later on. As the Phoenicians conducted all of this trade, they needed a way to keep records and make contracts. So
paragraphs.zh: 北非、西班牙和地中海岛屿进行贸 易。后来，随着希腊经济从崩溃中 复苏，他们与希腊进行了越来越多 的贸易。 腓尼基人也建立了殖民地。他们在 塞浦路斯、北非、西班牙南部、西 西里西部、撒丁岛和其他几个岛屿 上都成功地建立了定居点。 他们最伟大的殖民地之一是在北非 建立的，即现在的突尼斯。这个城 市在当时被称为“迦太基”，在我们 以后的故事中，它将扮演一个重要 的角色。 随着腓尼基人贸易的发展，他们需 要一种方法来管理记录和签订合 同。于是，他们从埃及人的阅读和

paragraphs.en: they were inspired by the ability of the Egyptians to read and write. The Phoenicians realized that writing was a very valuable tool. But as they studied the Egyptian writing system, they made a critical innovation. You see, ancient Egyptian had 3 types of symbols. Some hieroglyphs had no connection to sounds and merely described ideas, like the symbols for woman, life, stele or to fly. We call these types of writing symbols "ideograms". And other hieroglyphs WERE sound symbols, but only for very short sounds, either a vowel or consonant. We call these types of writing symbols "phonograms". But the vast majority of hieroglyphs describe full syllables, a
paragraphs.zh: 书写能力中得到了启发。 腓尼基人意识到文字是非常有价值 的工具。但当他们研究埃及的文字 系统时，他们做出了关键性的改革。 你看，古埃及有三种文字类型。其 中一部分象形文字与发音无关，只 是描述了一些概念：例如女人、生 命、石碑或飞翔。这类文字被称为 “表意文字”。 另外一部分象形文字是代表发音的 符号，但只有非常短的声音，要么 是元音，要么是辅音。我们把这些 类型的书写符号称为“形声字”。 但是绝大多数的象形文字都描述了 完整的音节，即辅音和元音的组合。

paragraphs.en: combination of a consonant and vowel. These types of symbols are "syllabograms". The Phoenicians were the first people to realize that you didn't need the syllabograms or the ideograms to write. You just needed a small list of phonograms, and you could put those together in combinations to make the sound of every word in the language. So the Phoenicians threw away the syllabograms and ideograms and kept only phonograms. The Phoenicians had just created the world’s first alphabet! Now instead of having to memorize thousands of symbols if you wanted to become literate, you only had to
paragraphs.zh: 这些类型的符号是“音节文字”。 书写过程中，音节文字和表意文字 都不是必要的。腓尼基人率先意识 到了这一点。 你只需要一个简单的音位表，就可 以通过排列组合表达出该语言中所 有的发音。 因此，腓尼基人抛弃了音节文字和 表意文字，只保留了形声字。 腓尼基人创造了世界上第一个字母 表！ 如此一来，你若不想做文盲，就只 需记住二十几个字母，而无需背诵 成千上万个符号了。

paragraphs.en: memorize about two dozen. This made learning how to read or write, much much easier. Every alphabet in the whole world is descended from this very first Phoenician alphabet.And today, every writing system on earth, except Chinese, uses an alphabet system. If you look closely you can figure out which Hieroglyphic symbols the Phoenicians adopted for their alphabet. In Phoenician, the word for "ox" is "Alp." So they took the Egyptian hieroglyph for ox and simplified it to just 3 lines.And they called that letter "Alp". The second letter represented the "B" sound. In Phoenician, the word
paragraphs.zh: 这使得学习读写变得容易多了。 世界上的每一个字母表都是第一个 腓尼基字母表的后裔。今天，地球 上的每一个书写系统，除了中文， 都有字母系统。 如果你仔细观察，就能发现腓尼基 人在他们的字母表中采用了哪些象 形文字。 腓尼基人用”Alp”表示“牛”。因 此他们采用了埃及象形文字中表示 “牛”的符号，并将其简化为 3 笔。 他们将这个字母念作"Alp"。 第二个字母代表“B”音。腓尼基语 中，代表“房屋”的词是“Bet”，

paragraphs.en: for "house" is "bet", so a simplified Egyptian symbol for house became their second letter. This is similar to how we teach little kids the alphabet by saying "Ais for Apple, B is for Book, C is for Cat". They did this until they had a list of 22 symbols that could express every sound in their language. And of course, this alphabet was so useful that it spread quickly throughout the Mediterranean. So now I've given you an introduction to the Phoenicians. The other important Semitic tribe from this time and place lived a little to the southeast of the Phoenicians, up in the Hills of Canaan.
paragraphs.zh: 因此埃及文字中代表“房屋”的词 被简化后，成为了腓尼基字母表中 的第二个字母。 这比较类似于我们向小孩教字母表 时，会告诉他们“A 是 Apple 苹果， B 是 Book 书，C 是 Cat 猫”。 腓尼基人一直都是这么做的，直到 他们有了一个包含 22 个符号的列 表，可以用来表达该语言中的每一 个声音。 当然，这个字母表非常有用，并且 迅速地在整个地中海地区传播开 来。 现在腓尼基人讲完了。 另一个来自该时代和地点的重要闪 族部落位于腓尼基人的东南方向， 坐落于迦南的山丘上。

paragraphs.en: These people were called the Israelites. While the Phoenicians created the alphabet, the Jews wrote the most important book with theAlphabet. Here is a picture of the Canaanite hills today. I hope this gives you some idea about what this part of the world looks like. Archaeologists have done excavations of this hill region, and they have discovered a distinctive kind of house inhabited by these early Israelites. They call it the 4-Room House. Here is an illustration of what these "Four Room Houses" looked like. On one side was a room for the animals, a stable. On the other side was an area for working and
paragraphs.zh: 这些人被称为以色列人。 当腓尼基人创造了字母表时，犹太 人用它写下了最重要的一本书（《圣 经》）。 今天的迦南群山如图所示。希望通 过照片能让你对这个地区的样貌有 所了解。 考古学家已经对这片山区进行了发 掘，他们发现了一种独特的房子， 这些房子是由早期的以色列人居住 的。他们把它称作“四室别墅”。 这是一张“四室别墅”的示意图。 一侧是供牲畜使用的马厩。另一侧 是厨房和工作间。中间有一个庭院， 除了正午时分，通常为两间房屋的

paragraphs.en: cooking.And then in-between would be a courtyard, which was usually shaded by the other two rooms, except for the middle of the day. This courtyard often had a cistern for storing rainwater and a firepit. Then at the end of the courtyard was the main room where people lived and up on the roof they often put up an awning for shade so that they could live or sleep outside in hot weather. These houses were gathered into villages and towns which probably looked a lot like this. But of course this raises a question. How do we know that the people living in these hill towns were Israelites? Well, the truth is, we cannot know
paragraphs.zh: 阴影所覆盖。院子中通常会有一个 蓄水池，用于储藏雨水，和一个火 坑。 院子的尽头是人们居住的主要房 间。他们通常会在屋顶放上遮阳棚， 这样一来，在炎热的夏天，人们就 可以在户外活动或睡觉。 这些房屋聚集在一起，形成了村镇， 看起来就如图片中的这样。 但当然，这就出现了一个问题。我 们怎么知道住在这些山城的人是以 色列人呢？ 实际上，我们也不能确定。但我们

paragraphs.en: for sure, but there is some evidence. The first piece of evidence is a Stele made by the Egyptian King Merneptah. Around 1208 BC, he launched a raid into Canaan and claims to have defeated many of the local people including the Israelites. Here is part of what he writes in the Stele.All of these names are people he brags about defeating.As part of his bragging he says: Israel is laid waste and his seed is not. Hurru has become a widow because of Egypt. This is the first non-biblical evidence for the existence of ancient Israel. So the Merneptah stele is the first
paragraphs.zh: 发现了一些证据。首先是埃及国王 麦伦普塔的石碑。 大约在公元前 1208 年，他发动了对 迦南的突袭，并声称已经击败了包 括以色列人在内的许多当地人。 这是他所写的石碑内容的一部分。 所有这些名字都是他吹嘘被自己打 败的人。以下是他炫耀的一部分： 以色列是浪费，他的种子不是。 由于埃及，赫鲁鲁以成为寡妇。 这是第一个圣经以外的证据，证明 了古代以色列的存在。 麦伦普塔石碑是第一个证据。第二

paragraphs.en: piece of evidence, the second is found in the trash piles outside these hill villages. In the trash piles of these villages, archaeologists have found the bones of cattle, goats, and sheep, but no pigs. This is evidence that the Jewish dietary taboo against eating pork may have already existed by the 1100s BC. It is evidence that the people living in these villages were the ancestors of the Jews: the Israelites. By estimating the number and sizes of the hill villages, archaeologists can get a rough idea of the population. In the 1200s BC, the Israelite population was probably around 15,000. But it grew over the next 300 years to about 100,000 people
paragraphs.zh: 个证据是在山村外的垃圾堆发现 的。考古学家们在这些垃圾堆里发 现了牛、山羊、绵羊的骨头，却唯 独没有发现猪骨。 这是犹太人饮食禁忌的证据，可能 早在公元前 10 世纪就已经存在了。 这些证据表明了住在这些村庄里的 人是犹太人的祖先：以色列人。 通过估算山村的数量和大小，考古 学家可以粗略地了解这些村庄的人 口情况。 公元前 13 世纪，以色列的人口数量 大概在 15,000 人左右。但其人口在 接下来的 300 年间稳步增长，到了 公元前 10 世纪，人口数量已经足够

paragraphs.en: by the 900s, which is enough to form a small state. And once you have a state, you can have a king. The Bible tells us that the first King of Israel was a man named Saul. He was succeeded by a man named David. In the bible the God of Israel is named Y-H-W-H. This name is so holy, that the Jews never say it out-loud today. Instead they are silent for a moment when they come to his name, or they say "Adonai" which means "Lord" And since the ancient Hebrew alphabet does not include vowels, we cannot be sure of the proper pronunciation. One guess is "Yahweh" but nobody knows for
paragraphs.zh: 组成一个小国家。 一旦有了国家，就会有国王。《圣经》 告诉我们，以色列的第一个国王是 扫罗王。 大卫王继承了他的王位。 《圣经》中，以色列的王名为 这个名字是如此的神圣，以至于犹 太人至今都从未有大声说出来。相 反，每当提及这个名字时，他们便 会沉默片刻，或者说“阿多”，意思 是“主”。 由于古代希伯来语字母表中没有元 音，所以我们不能确定其准确的发 音。一个猜测是“耶和华”，但没人 知道确切的答案。

paragraphs.en: sure. The Israelites only believed in one God. They were Monotheists. And you can see this in one of their most important prayers: She-ma Yis-ra-elAdonai El-oh-hey-nu,Adonai eh-chad. Which means "Listen Israel, the Lord is our god, the Lord is one." The third King of Israel was Solomon, the son of David. He built the Israelite Temple to God in their capital city of Jerusalem. According to tradition, he ruled from about 970-931 BC. After Solomon, the Kingdom of Israel became divided into two Kingdoms. In the south was the Kingdom of
paragraphs.zh: 以色列人只相信一个神。他们是一 神论者。 你可以在他们最重要的祈祷中看到 这一点： 意思是“以色列人，听着，主是我 们的上帝，我们唯一的上帝。” 以色列的第三位国王是大卫的儿子 所罗门。他在首都耶路撒冷建造了 以色列的神殿。 根据传统说法，他于公元前970-931 年统治以色列。 所罗门之后，以色列王国一分为二。 南方是犹大王国，首都为耶路撒冷。

paragraphs.en: Judah with its capital at Jerusalem. In the north was the Kingdom of Israel with its capital at Samaria. Around the time of the two Kingdoms we get more non-biblical evidence for the existence of Israel. We have found a stele from around 840 BC. It was made by the King of Moab, one of the enemies of the Israelites, and he boasts about how his god "Chemosh" gave him a victory against the Israelite town of Nebo. This artifact is the oldest extra-biblical reference to the Israelite God Yahweh. The next bit of evidence for the Israelite Kingdom comes from the Tel-Dan Stele, which was carved sometime between 870 and 750 BC. This stele is a fragment, so we don't have the full message, but the part
paragraphs.zh: 北方是以色列王国，首都在撒玛利 亚。 在两国并立的时代，我们找到了更 多能够证明以色列存在的非圣经证 据。 我们找到了一块大约来自公元前 840 年的石碑：米沙石碑，它是由 摩押王制造的，是以色列人的敌人 之一。他夸耀自己的神“基抹”是 如何帮助他在以色列的尼波镇取得 胜利。这块石碑是圣经之外最早关 于耶和华神的记载。 另一个证明以色列存在的证据是但 丘石碑，创作于公元前 870-750 年 之间。 这是一块石碑碎片，因此我们并没 有完整的信息，但我们掌握的这部

paragraphs.en: that we do have describes a war against Israel, and mentions three Kings from the bible Jehoram, Ahab, andAhaziahu. It also mentions a "House of David." This is a strongest evidence outside of the Bible that there was actually a King David. But of course, if you are going to discuss the ancient Israelites, you need to discuss the most important source of all for their history, the Bible. So join me next time as I give you an overview of this book, and we do a summary of the first stories in it. Thank you all very much.
paragraphs.zh: 分描述了一场与以色列的战争，并 且提到了三个《圣经》中的王：约 兰，亚哈和阿哈兹耶阿胡。 它还提到了“大卫王朝”。 这是圣经之外最有力的证据，证明 历史上确实有大卫王。 但是，如果你想要了解古代以色列 人，我们必须讨论其历史最重要的 来源——《圣经》。 下一节课，我将给大家介绍《圣经》， 并且总结其中一部分故事。 谢谢大家。

paragraphs.en: Hello everyone, and welcome back to our lecture series on World History. Today we are going to discuss the Bible. It’s hard to know when exactly the story I’m going to tell you about took place. But if the people in them were real, they lived between 2000 and 1000 BC. Unfortunately, it’s hard to get more precise than that. Regardless of your religious beliefs, the Bible is an important book to read. It is the most influential book in history, as well as the most widely read book on planet Earth. So the goal of this class is to begin giving you some basic familiarity with this book.
paragraphs.zh: 大家好，欢迎回到我们的世界历史 系列课程。今天我们要讨论的是《圣 经》。 我要给你们讲的故事，很难确切地 知道是什么时候发生的。但是如果 里面的人是真是的，他们生活在公 元前2000年到公元前1000年之间。 不幸的是，很难得到比这更准确的 数据。 无论你的宗教信仰是什么，《圣经》 都是一本重要的读物。它是史上最 具影响力的书，也是地球上阅读量 最大的书籍。 这节课的目的是让你们对这本书有 一些基本的了解。

paragraphs.en: However, its a little inaccurate to call the Bible a "Book."Actually its a collection of 66 books that were written by dozens of different authors over hundreds of years. The Bible has two parts. The first part contains 39 books. These books are the holy scriptures of the Jewish people. Since the original language of the Jews was Hebrew, we can call this part of the Bible the "Hebrew Bible". But the Christians add another 27 books into the Bible. These books are about Jesus Christ and they are written in Greek instead of Hebrew. The Christians call the first part the "Old Testament" and they call the second part the "New Testament". We don't have time to do a synopsis of the whole Bible of course, so
paragraphs.zh: 然而，把《圣经》叫做一本“书” 有点不准确。事实上，《圣经》由 66 本书组成，是数百年来几十位不 同作家的作品集合。 《圣经》有两部分。第一部分包含 39 本书。这些书是犹太人的圣经。 由于犹太人的母语是希伯来语，我 们可以称这部分《圣经》为“希伯 来《圣经》”。 但是基督徒在《圣经》中又增加了 27本书。这些书是关于耶稣基督的， 用的是希腊语而不是希伯来语。 基督徒称第一部分为“旧约”，第二 部分为“新约”。 当然，我们没有时间做整部《圣经》 的概要，所以今天我们要做的是对

paragraphs.en: today we are just going to do a summary of the first book: Genesis. The first 11 chapters of Genesis are creation stories. God creates the world. Then he creates Adam and Eve. He puts them in the Garden of Eden, where they sin by eating the forbidden fruit of the knowledge of good and evil. Then they are driven out of the garden. Adam and Eve have two sons: Cain andAbel. But then Cain kills his brother and commits the first murder. Then the world becomes so evil that God decides to drown it with a flood. But God spares Noah who puts a pair of each animal on a giant boat called the Ark. Noah and his family survive the flood and God
paragraphs.zh: 第一卷《创世纪》的总结。 《创世纪》的前 11 章是创世故事。 上帝创造了世界。然后创造了亚当 和夏娃。他们被上帝放在伊甸园里， 在那里他们吃了关于善恶知识的禁 果而犯罪。然后被赶出花园。 亚当和夏娃有两个儿子:该隐和亚 伯。但是该隐杀了他的兄弟，犯下 了第一桩谋杀罪。 后来，世界充满了罪恶，上帝决定 用洪水将其淹没。诺亚受上帝宽恕， 将每种动物雌雄各一对放入一只方 舟。最终，诺亚和他的家人在洪水 中幸存下来，上帝也保证不会再用 洪水泛滥世界。

paragraphs.en: promises he will never flood the world again. Then mankind builds a great tower to heaven called the Tower of Babel. But God strikes them with confusion so they all start speaking different languages and can no longer cooperate. All of these are ancient stories, some of which the Sumerians also had, that the ancient Jews used to explain the origin of the world. Then by Chapter 12, the creation stories are over, and the Bible jumps ahead in time to tell the story of one Family, led by a man named Abraham. God speaks toAbraham and tells him that he will makeAbraham the ancestor of a great nation.
paragraphs.zh: 之后，人类建造了一座通往天堂的 塔，叫做巴别塔。但是上帝让他们 说不同的语言，人们陷入混乱与迷 惑，再也不能相互合作，巴别塔轰 然倒塌。 这些故事都非常古老了，其中一些 苏美尔人也有，古代犹太人则用它 们来解释世界的起源。 到了第 12 章，创世故事结束了，《圣 经》前进了一段时间，讲述了亚伯 拉罕家族的故事。 神对亚伯拉罕说，他要让亚伯拉罕 成为一个伟大民族的祖先。

paragraphs.en: Abraham is already an old childless man when God speaks to him, and he doesn't have any children. But he accepts God's message on faith. God tellsAbraham to leave his home in the city of Ur, and to travel with all of his people and animals to Canaan. Here is what that journey looks like on a map. In the 21st Chapter of Genesis, God performs a miracle andAbraham's wife Sarah gives birth at the age of 90. They name their son Isaac. Then Isaac grows up and he marries a woman named Rebekah. Rebekah gives birth to twin boys. The older brother is named Esau and the younger brother is named Jacob.
paragraphs.zh: 此时，亚伯拉罕已经是一个老人了， 并且没有子嗣。但他怀着信念接受 了神的信息。 上帝让亚伯拉罕离开他在乌尔城的 家，带着所有的族人和牲畜到迦南 去。 从地图上可以看到亚伯拉罕的旅 程。 在《创世纪》第 21 章，上帝创造了 奇迹，亚伯拉罕的妻子撒拉在 90 岁 时生下了孩子。他们给儿子取名为 以撒。 以撒渐渐长大，娶了一个名叫利百 加的妇人为妻。 利百加生了一对双胞胎男孩。哥哥 名叫以扫，弟弟名叫雅各。

paragraphs.en: Esau grows up to be a hunter, while Jacob becomes a herder. Isaac favors Esau, but Rebekah favors Jacob. Esau is very hairy, but Jacob has smooth skin. Because Esau is the first-born, he has the right to claim his father's inheritance and blessing. But Jacob and Rebekah make a plan to trick Esau out of his inheritance. Once Isaac is very old and has lost his sight, he tells Esau to go out and hunt some food for him. But while Esau is away, Rebekah tells Jacob to put some wool on his face and hands. And then Jacob comes into Isaac’s tent and tricks his father into thingking that he is Esau.
paragraphs.zh: 以扫长大后成为了一名猎人，雅各 则成为一名牧人。以撒更爱以扫， 利百加却偏爱雅各。 以扫全身毛发旺盛，雅各的皮肤则 非常光滑。 以扫是长子，所以他有权得到父亲 的遗产和赐福。雅各和利百加商议， 要用诡计获取属于以扫的遗产。 以撒年纪老迈，眼睛瞎了，就吩咐 以扫出去找食物吃。趁着以扫不在， 利百加吩咐雅各把羊毛粘在脸上和 手上。 雅各进了以撒的帐篷，就欺骗父亲， 让父亲以为他是以扫。

paragraphs.en: So Isaac gives Jacob the blessing meant for Esau. When Esau comes back and finds out that Jacob has stolen his blessing, he is very angry, and swears that he will kill his brother in revenge. So Rebekah tells her son to run away and go live with her brother Laban. Jacob makes a long journey and reaches his uncle Laban. He begins working for Laban as one of his herders. Laban has two daughters, Leah (the older daughter) and Rachel (the younger). Rachel is very beautiful and Jacob falls in love with her. So he agrees to work for his uncle for 7 years in exchange for being allowed to marry Rachel.And since he is a
paragraphs.zh: 以撒把雅各当成了以扫，给他赐福。 以扫回来，见雅各偷去了他的福分， 非常愤怒，起誓要杀他哥哥复仇。 利百加就吩咐儿子逃跑，去投奔她 的哥哥拉班。 雅各走了很长的路，终于见到了他 的舅舅拉班。他开始为拉班工作， 成为他的牧人。拉班有两个女儿， 大女儿名叫利亚，小女儿叫做拉结。 拉结很漂亮，雅各爱上了她。为了 可以娶拉结，他同意为他的叔叔工 作 7 年来交换。因为雅各的工作非 常有价值，拉班同意了。

paragraphs.en: valuable worker, Laban agrees. But when the wedding comes, Laban tricks Jacob on the wedding night and he ends up sleeping with Leah instead! Leah was not as attractive as Rachel, and Laban wanted to make sure his older daughter had a husband too. So Laban tells Jacob that he can also marry Rachel in 7 days, if Jacob agrees to work for Laban for another 7 years, and Jacob agrees. Now Jacob loves Rachel more than Leah, and God sees this. So he blesses Leah instead. Rachel is unable to have any children, but Leah give Jacob child after child. This causes jealousy between the sisters, so Rachel and Leah start competing for Jacob's affection, and they give him their maidservants
paragraphs.zh: 但当婚礼来临的时候，拉班在新婚 之夜欺骗了雅各，雅各最后和利亚 睡在了同一张床上！利亚没有拉结 漂亮，拉班想让他的大女儿也能嫁 出去。 拉班告诉雅各，如果雅各同意再为 他工作 7 年，就可以在 7 天内迎娶 拉结。 上帝看见，雅各爱拉结胜过爱利亚， 于是他反而祝福予利亚。拉结不能 生育，利亚给雅各生了一个又一个 的孩子。 于是拉结和利亚出于嫉妒，为了得 到雅各的注意互相竞争，并把她们 的女仆辟拉和悉帕献给了雅各。这 二人又怀孕生子。

paragraphs.en: Bilah and Zilpah, who also gets pregnant and gives birth to sons. By the end of this time, Jacob has 4 wives, but his favorite wife Rachel is still without any children. And then, finally, Rachel gets pregnant and gives Jacob a son, who she calls Joseph. Around this time, Jacob now has a large family and many animals. But he has still not reconciled with his brother Esau. So Jacob sends a messenger to Esau and decides to meet him, after sending Esau many gifts and asking for his forgiveness. The night before Jacob is supposed to meet Esau, he cannot sleep.And he goes outside where the Bible tells us that he "wrestled with God."
paragraphs.zh: 就这样，雅各有了 4 个妻子，但他 最爱的拉结仍然没有孩子。 后来，拉结终于怀孕，给雅各生了 一个儿子，取名叫约瑟。 此时，雅各已经有了一个大家庭和 许多牲畜。但他还没有与他哥哥以 扫和好。 于是雅各派遣使者去见以扫，并向 他送了许多礼物，又求他饶恕。 在他俩见面的前一夜，雅各无法入 眠。他走到屋外，《圣经》里告诉我 们他“与神扭打在一起”。

paragraphs.en: He grabs hold of God and says that he will not let go until God blesses him. And so God does bless him. But God also gives Jacob a new name. God calls Jacob by the name "Israel" which means "wrestles with God." From here on, the Bible refers to Jacob as "Israel" instead.And his sons are the "sons of Israel" And the next day Jacob, now called "Israel" meets with his brother. He is afraid of violence, but instead, Esau forgives Israel, and the brothers are reconciled. Unfortunately, after the good of making peace with his brother, Israel is hit by two tragedies.
paragraphs.zh: 他抓住上帝，说除非上帝赐福于他， 否则就不放手。 于是上帝赐福与他。 但神也给雅各取了一个新的名字。 神称雅各为“以色列”，意思正是“与 神扭打”。 从这章开始，《圣经》把雅各称为“以 色列”。他的儿子是“以色列的儿 子”。 次日，现在叫做“以色列”的雅各， 和他的兄弟相会了。他非常害怕遭 到以扫的报复，但以扫原谅了以色 列，兄弟两人和好了。 不幸的是，在兄弟和好之后，以色 列遭遇了两场悲剧。

paragraphs.en: His wife Rachel gets pregnant again, and gives birth to her second son, named Benjamin. Benjamin lives, but Rachel dies while giving birth to him. And then in that same chapter, Isaac also dies. So Israel loses his father and wife one after the other. After Benjamin is born, Israel has 12 sons. This is what the family tree looks like. Each of these sons would grow up to be an ancestor of an entire tribe of people. These descendants of Israel would be known as the Israelites. And they would be grouped into 12
paragraphs.zh: 他的妻子拉结再次怀孕，生下了次 子便雅悯。 便雅悯活了下来，但拉结在生他的 时候难产死去了。 在同一章中，以撒也死了。就这样， 以色列一个接一个地失去了他的父 亲和妻子。 便雅悯出生后，以色列就有了 12 个 儿子。 这就是他的家谱。 每个儿子长大后，都是一个部落的 祖先。 以色列的这些后裔就是以色列人。 他们分为了 12 个以色列部落，每个

paragraphs.en: tribes of Israel, one tribe for each of the 12 sons. Time passes, and Joseph grows up to be a handsome young man.And God has given him the gift of interpreting dreams that can tell the future. Israel loves Joseph the most. So as a sign of his love, he gives Joseph a special coat with many different beautiful colors. But this makes all of Joseph's brothers very jealous. So one day all of his brothers except Benjamin (who was at camp with his father) decide to hurt Joseph. They throw him into a pit. Then they rip up his coat and pour goat blood on it to make it look like a lion killed Joseph.
paragraphs.zh: 部落对应 12 个儿子中的一个。 随着时间的流逝，约瑟长成了一个 英俊的年轻人。上帝赐给他解梦的 能力，能预知未来。 以色列最爱约瑟。为了表示他的爱， 他送给约瑟一件美丽多彩的外套。 但这使得约瑟的兄弟们都很嫉妒。 终于有一天，约瑟的兄弟们（除了 与父亲同营的便雅悯）都联合起来 要害约瑟。 他们把约瑟扔进一个坑里，然后扯 破他的外套，把山羊血倒在上面， 让约瑟看起来是被狮子杀死的。

paragraphs.en: And then, while they are thinking about what to do next. Slave traders come by. So the brothers sell Joseph into slavery, and he is taken as a slave into Egypt. Imagine what it must feel like to be sold into slavery by your own brothers! While in Egypt, Joseph was unfairly thrown in prison in Egypt. But God protects Joseph, and in prison the prison warden treats him kindly. Another prisoner had been the cup-bearer of the Pharaoh. But Pharaoh became angry with him and threw him into prison. One day in prison, this prisoner has a strange dream, and Joseph interprets the dream to tell him it means that he will soon be free.
paragraphs.zh: 他们在考虑下一步该怎么做时，奴 隶贩子来了。于是兄弟们把约瑟卖 了，他便被带到埃及去做奴隶了。 想象一下被你自己的兄弟们卖为奴 隶的感觉吧！ 不幸的是，在埃及时，约瑟蒙冤入 狱。但上帝保护约瑟，监狱长待他 很好。 有一个囚犯是为法老斟酒的，惹怒 了法老，被下放到了监狱里。 有一天在监狱里，他做了一个奇怪 的梦，约瑟为他解梦，告诉他：这 意味着他很快就可以自由了。

paragraphs.en: The dream comes true and the cup-bearer is released. But once he is free, the cup-bearer forgets about Joseph, who remains in prison. But then, two years later, the Pharaoh has a terrible nightmare. He dreams of seven fat cows, and then seven very thin cows come along and eat the seven fat cows. This dream frightens Pharaoh who does not know what it means. And nobody in his court can understand its meaning either. But then the cup-bearer remembers Joseph and suggests that perhaps Joseph can interpret the dream. Joseph is called in from the prison, and he explains the dream to Pharaoh. Joseph explains that Egypt will have
paragraphs.zh: 这个梦最终成真，斟酒人被释放了。 但是，当这个人自由后，很快就把 仍在监狱里的约瑟忘记了。 但两年后，法老做了一个可怕的噩 梦。 梦里出现了七只肥硕的牛，然后它 们被七只非常瘦的牛吃掉了。 这个梦吓坏了法老，他不知道这是 什么意思。在他的宫廷里也没有人 能给出答案。 但斟酒人想起了约瑟，并向法老建 议：约瑟也许能解释这个梦。 约瑟从监狱里被召出来，并向法老 解释了这个梦。 约瑟解释说，肥牛代表了埃及将有

paragraphs.en: 7 years of good harvests. These are represented by the fat cows. But then Egypt will have 7 years of famine, which will use up all the food reserves. These are represented by the skinny cows. Joseph explains that Pharaoh must save up grain for the next 7 years, so that Egypt will be ready for the coming famine. Pharaoh is impressed and he makes Joseph a member of his court. Over the next 7 years, Joseph rises in power until he is one of the leading men in Egypt. He marries an Egyptian woman and tries to forget his family in Canaan. And then, 7 years later, the famine comes. But because of Joseph, Egypt is ready and has enough food.
paragraphs.zh: 7 年的丰收。但是瘦牛代表在那之 后埃及将经历 7 年的饥荒，并耗尽 所有的粮食储备。 约瑟解释说，法老必须攒下 7 年的 粮食，好让埃及为即将来临的饥荒 做好准备。法老对他刮目相看，把 约瑟立为他的臣仆。 在接下来的 7 年里，约瑟逐渐掌权， 最终成为埃及的领袖之一。 他娶了一个埃及女人，试图忘记他 在迦南的家庭。 7 年后，饥荒开始了。但因约瑟的 缘故，埃及做好了准备并有足够的 粮食。

paragraphs.en: But back in Canaan, the situation is desperate, and Joseph's family begins to starve. So several of his brothers come into Egypt looking for food. When they come to Egypt they meet with Joseph, but they do not recognize him. But Joseph recognizes them. Of course, he is angry at them. But he also misses his family, and he wants to know about his father and younger brother Benjamin. So Joseph plays several tricks on his family, but he also sends them food and demands they send him Benjamin. And eventually he cannot keep it to himself any longer and reveals himself to his brothers. When Israel learns that his beloved son is still alive he rushes to Egypt,
paragraphs.zh: 但是在迦南，情况很危急，约瑟的 家人开始挨饿。所以他的几个兄弟 来到埃及寻找食物。 他们来到埃及，遇见了约瑟却没有 认出他。但是约瑟认出了他们。 约瑟当然对他们很生气。但他也想 念他的家人，他想知道他的父亲和 弟弟便雅悯的近况。 约瑟捉弄了一下他的家人，他给家 人送去粮食，但让他们把便雅悯送 来。最后他没有再保守秘密，向他 的兄弟们揭露了自己的身份。 虽然以色列已经很老了，但当得知 他心爱的儿子还活着的时候，他还

paragraphs.en: even though he is a very old man, and both father and son are overcome with tears when they see each other again. The family survives the famine thanks to Joseph, and they live in Egypt for another 17 years. And then when Israel is very old, he asks Joseph to bring him his sons, so that he can bless his grandsons in the same way that Isaac blessed him. And then Israel dies, old and full of years, but he asks for his family to send his body back to Canaan, and bury it there along with the rest of his family. After their father dies, Josephs older brothers are still afraid that Joseph will take revenge of them. And full of guilt they throw themselves at
paragraphs.zh: 是赶到了埃及。父子俩再次见面时， 都泪流满面。 多亏了约瑟，一家人得以在饥荒中 幸存，他们在埃及又住了 17 年。 当以色列年纪老迈时，他让约瑟领 他的两个儿子来，像以撒给他祝福 一样，以色列也给他的孙子们祝福。 然后以色列去世了，他要求家人把 他的遗体送回迦南，和他的家族一 起埋葬在那里。 父亲死后，约瑟的兄弟们仍然害怕 约瑟会报复他们。他们满心愧疚， 俯伏在弟兄的脚前。

paragraphs.en: their brother's feet. But Joseph is not interested in revenge. He forgives his brothers, telling them these words: "Don't be afraid.Am I in the place of God? You intended to harm me, but God intended it for good, to accomplish what is now being done, the saving of many lives. So then, don't be afraid. I will provide for you and your children." And so the book of Genesis ends with the family living in Egypt.And Joseph dies as an old man, and is buried in Egypt. Okay everyone, that lesson was a little long, but since this is one of the most important stories in the world, I wasn't sure how to make it any shorter. I already cut out a lot just to make it this short. But
paragraphs.zh: 但是约瑟对复仇不感兴趣。他原谅 了他的兄弟，并对他们说: “不要害怕。谁能揣测出上帝的旨 意呢?你们有意加害于我，这却是上 帝的安排，正因如此我才能成就现 在所行的事，拯救了许多人的性命。 所以，不要害怕。我一定会供养你 们和你们的儿女。 《创世纪》这本书到这里也结束了。 约瑟寿终正寝，葬在了埃及。 好了同学们，这节课有点长，但由 于这是全世界最重要的故事之一， 我实在不知道应该怎样把它缩短。 我已经删去了很多内容，所以这已 经很短了。但如果还想更深入地了 解这节课的内容，怎么办?

paragraphs.en: perhaps you'd like to learn more? Well, then you perhaps should go see the tomb of the Patriarchs in Israel. This is the place where Abraham, Sarah, Isaac, Rebekah, Israel and Leah are all buried. Of course they were buried there 3000 years ago, but Jewish tradition has honored that spot ever since. Their bones lie in a cave beneath this building. You might also like this lecture series by Canadian psychologist Jordan Peterson. He talks about the psychological symbolism of these stories and really makes you see them in a deeper way. And of course, you could get a copy of the Bible and read the book of Genesis for yourself. You will see that I had to skip over a lot of
paragraphs.zh: 那你也许应该去看看位于以色列的 列祖之墓。亚伯拉罕、撒拉、以撒、 利百加、以色列、利亚都葬在这里。 他们在 3000 年前就已经葬在此处， 犹太人至今一直非常崇敬这个地 方。他们的遗骨被安置在这个建筑 下面的洞穴里。 你也可以看看加拿大心理学家乔 丹·彼得森的系列课程。他谈到了 这些故事的心理象征意义，能帮助 你更深刻地看懂它们。 当然，你也可以买一本《圣经》，自 己读一读《创世纪》。你会发现，由 于篇幅有限，我跳过了很多有趣的 故事！

paragraphs.en: interesting stories in order to keep this lecture from being too long!

titleZh: 第五讲：亚述王国|在胜利边缘转身离开

paragraphs.en: Hello everyone, today we are going to learn about a people who had a big impact on the Israelites and made several appearances in the Bible. These people were the Assyrians. In this lesson we will be focusing on the 300 years between 911 and 609 BC. Here is what that looks like on our timeline. The last time we talked about the Assyrians, it was during the Bronze Age Collapse. TheAssyrians actually emerged from the collapse stronger than before. But their big weakness was internal conflict, so Civil War kept them often divided and weak until the 900s BC.
paragraphs.zh: 大家好，今天我们要讨论的这个民 族对以色列人产生了重大影响，并 且多次出现在《圣经》中。他们就 是亚述人。 这节课，我们要把目光集中在公元 前 911-609 之间的 300 年间。这是 这段时间在时间线上的样子。 上次我们谈到亚述人的时候，是在 青铜时代崩溃之际。实际上，亚述 人比以前更强大了。 但他们最大的弱点是内部冲突。因 此，直到公元前 10 世纪，亚述还经 常因为内战而四分五裂，逐渐式微。

paragraphs.en: That was when a strong King named Adad Nirarri II re-unified the Assyrians and began pushing their borders outwards again. This final phase ofAssyrian History is called the Neo-Assyrian Empire, and it began in 911 BC. Adad Nirari's grandson was a King named Ashurnasirpal II. He is a particularly famous King among historians because he commissioned artists to create giant wall-relief carvings that celebrated his victories. Many of these reliefs are on display today in the British Museum. This one showsAshurnasirpal II attacking an enemy city with his army. Here on the left is Crown Prince Shalmanaser III firing a bow while
paragraphs.zh: 这时，亚述出现了一位强大的国王： 阿达德尼拉里二世，他统一了亚述， 并且开始扩大版图。亚述历史的最 后阶段被称为新亚述帝国，开始于 公元前 911 年。 阿达德尼拉里的孙子是亚述纳西拔 二世。 亚述纳西拔二世在历史学家中非常 著名，因为他命艺术家们创作了巨 大的浮雕雕刻以庆祝胜利。这些浮 雕如今在大英博物馆展出。 这块浮雕展示了亚述纳西拔二世及 其军队正在袭击敌方城市。 左侧是王储沙尔马那塞尔三世正在 保镖的护卫下射箭。

paragraphs.en: protected by a body-guard. On the right, we see depictions of Assyrian soldiers using ladders to scale a wall. This shows defenders shooting arrows from their walls. And here is a depiction of what will happen to you if you resist the Assyrian army. You will be shot full of arrows, or crushed by horses, or decapitated, or massacred while running away. Here areAssyrian soldiers cutting off and collecting enemy heads after the battle is over. Here on the left, are women and children being led away as slaves. On the top right we see prisoners of war and plunder being taken away.
paragraphs.zh: 右侧，我们可以看到亚述士兵用梯 子爬墙的画面。 从此可以看出，防守者从城墙射箭。 这段描述的是关于对抗亚述军队的 后果。 你会被箭射成刺猬，或被马踏死， 或被斩首，或在逃跑时被屠杀。 这是亚述士兵在战斗结束后砍掉并 收集敌人的头颅。 左侧，妇女和儿童被带走充当奴隶。 右上方，亚述士兵掠走战俘和战利 品。

paragraphs.en: And on the bottom right, this carving depicts anAssyrian victory parade. When this King,Ashurnasirpal II, came to the throne in 883 BC, his first task was to crush a rebellion in the city of Suru. He did this with staggering ferocity and cruelty. We know this because he was proud of it and wrote it all down! Ashurnasirpal wrote this about how he treated the rebels: “I put up a pillar at the city gate and I skinned the chiefs who rebelled against me, and covered the pillar with their skins. I walled up others in the middle of the pillar itself and some of them I impaled on stakes and arranged them around the pillar. I cut the limbs off the officers who had rebelled.”
paragraphs.zh: 雕塑右下角描绘了亚述的胜利游 行。 公元前 883 年，亚述纳西拔二世即 位后，他的第一个任务就是要镇压 苏鲁的叛乱。他以惊人残暴和残忍 的方式做到了这一点。我们能够知 道这一事实，是因为他为此感到骄 傲，并且写了下来！ 亚述纳西拔写下了他是如何对待叛 军的： “我在城门口立起一根柱子，剥下 叛军首领的皮，覆盖在柱子之上。 我把他们中的一些填堵在柱子里， 一些穿刺在柱子上，还有一些绑起 来围着柱子绕。我还砍断了叛军首 领的四肢。”

paragraphs.en: He continues: “I carried off their captives, from some I cut off their noses, their ears, and their fingers. Of many, I put out their eyes. I made one pillar of the living and another of heads and I bound their heads to tree trunks round about the city. Their young men and maidens I consumed with fire. The rest of their warriors I consumed with thirst in the desert of the Euphrates.” And remember, all of these wall reliefs depicting this violence were originally painted in vivid color. These were the palace decorations! Ashurnasirpal II's successor was Shalmaneser III. In 853 BC, Shalmanaser fought the Battle of Qarqar against an alliance of 12 smaller Kingdoms, including
paragraphs.zh: 他继续道： “我带走了他们的俘虏，割下他们 的鼻子，耳朵，和他们的手指。还 有很多人，我挖掉了他们的眼睛。 我用活人做成了一个柱子然后堆以 头颅，我还把他们的头颅做成了三 个树干，围绕在城市周围。我用火 焚烧他们的青年男女，剩下的扔到 沙漠里渴死了。” 别忘了，所有这些描述暴力场面的 浮雕最初都是用鲜艳生动的色彩画 作的，并且作为宫廷装饰品！ 亚述纳西拔二世的继任者是沙尔马 那塞尔三世。 公元前 853 年，以沙尔马纳塞三世 为首的亚述军队在数个阿拉伯部落 和以色列亚哈王的带领下，在卡尔

paragraphs.en: severalArab tribes with camels, and the Israelites led by KingAhab. Twelve years later, he returned to Canaan and attacked Damascus, the Phoenicians, and the Israelites, who as you might remember were divided into the Northern Kingdom of Israel and the Southern Kingdom of Judah. The Israelites were defeated and paid tribute to Shalmaneser in return for him agreeing to spare their Kingdom. Israel became a vassal state of the Assyrians, and the oldest image in the world of a Biblical character comes from the Black Obelisk of Shalmaneser III. The picture at the top of this slide shows us a scene from that obelisk, in which the King of Israel bows
paragraphs.zh: 卡尔与一个由 12 个小国组成的盟 军发生了战争。 12 年后，他回到迦南，攻击了大马 士革、腓尼基人和以色列人。你应 该还记得，这里被分为北方的“以 色列国”和南方的“犹大国”。 以色列人战败了。为了让沙尔马那 塞尔三世放过他们的王国，他们开 始向沙尔马那塞尔三世朝贡。 以色列成为了亚述人的附庸国，而 世界上最古老的圣经人物形象则来 自于沙尔马那塞尔三世的黑色方尖 碑。 这张幻灯片顶部的图片向我们展示 了黑色方尖碑中的场景之一：以色 列的国王向亚述皇帝俯低了身体。

paragraphs.en: low to theAssyrian emperor. Here is the actual carving up close, so you can see it for yourself. For 100 years, Israel paid tribute to Assyria in exchange for peace. But then in the late 700s, the King of Israel grew tired of paying and sent messages to the King of Egypt, looking for a better deal. TheAssyrian King Sargon II found out about this and attacked Israel in 721 BC. He overwhelmed the Israelite army and destroyed the capital city of Samaria. Then he deported the population of Israel and resettled them in other parts of the Empire. The Kingdom of Judah had been the home of 2 of the Tribes of Israel:
paragraphs.zh: 这是雕刻近景，你可以亲自看一看。 百年来，以色列向亚述纳贡，以换 取和平。但到了公元前八世纪晚期， 以色列国王厌倦了向埃及国王纳贡 以寻求更好的出路。 亚述王萨尔贡二世发现了这一点， 并且在公元前 721 年攻击了以色 列。 他击溃了以色列军队，摧毁了以色 列首都撒玛利亚。 然后，他驱逐了以色列人，把他们 分散安置在帝国不同的地方。 犹大王国曾是两个以色列部落的家 园：犹大和便雅悯。而以色列王国

paragraphs.en: Judah and Benjamin. While the Kingdom of Israel had been the home of the other 10. But now the other 10 tribes were deported by theAssyrians, and except for refugees who made it south to Judah, they disappeared from history. These became known as the "10 Lost Tribes of Israel". Now the only surviving Israelites were the people in the Kingdom of Judah. These people were known as the "Judeans" which is the origin of the word "Jew". At that time, the King of Judah was King Hezekiah, and he knew very well that theAssyrians would be back, and this time they would be coming for his Kingdom and the capital city of Jerusalem.
paragraphs.zh: 则是另外 10 个部落的所在地。 但是，这 10 个部落被亚述人驱逐出 境，除了被驱逐到犹大南部的难民 之外，其他人都从历史中消失了。 他们被称为“失踪的以色列十支 派”。 现在，犹大王国的人民成为了仅有 的以色列幸存者。 这些人就是如今我们熟知的“犹太 人”。 当时，犹大王国由希西家王统治。 他非常清楚，亚述人还会回来的， 并且他们的目标就是犹大王国和首 都耶路撒冷。

paragraphs.en: So Hezekiah began frantically preparing the defenses of his city. He tore down houses to expand the walls. He made sure that the water reservoir was inside the new walls to protect his fresh water supply. Furthermore, he sent his workers to building a tunnel that was 533 meters long and ran underground to the Gihon Spring. This provided Jerusalem with a second fresh water source. And then in 701 BC, the awful trial came. The Empire turned its attention once more to Canaan, and theAssyrian army began its march south. This invasion was such a traumatic event for little Judah that it is recorded by 3 different books in the Bible: Isaiah, 2 Kings, and 2
paragraphs.zh: 于是希西家开始疯狂地准备城市的 防御工事。他拆毁房屋，扩建围墙， 以确保储水设备位于新墙内，从而 保证淡水供应。 此外，他还命工人们建造了一条长 533 米的地下隧道，直通基训泉。 这为耶路撒冷提供了第二个淡水来 源。 然后在公元前 701 年，考验降临了。 帝国再次把注意力转向迦南，亚述 军队开始向南进军。 这场入侵给弱小犹大王国留下了巨 大的创伤，以至于在《圣经》的《以 赛亚书（圣经旧约）》、《列王纪（上、 下）》和《历代志（上、下）》三卷

paragraphs.en: Chronicles. The story begins with these words: "In the fourteenth year of King Hezekiah's reign, Sennacherib king ofAssyria attacked all the fortified cities of Judah and captured them." Town after town was captured, surrendered or destroyed as the Assyrian juggernaut cut a path of destruction through the Judean countryside. We know fromAssyrian sources, that their army overwhelmed 46 fortified towns on their march towards Jerusalem. One by one, the cities of Judah were being crushed beneathAssyrian might. Now only 1 fortress remained guarding the road between the Assyrians and Jerusalem. This was
paragraphs.zh: 中都提及了这一事件。 故事以这些文字开始： “希西家王第 14 年，亚述的西拿基 立王攻击了犹大王国所有的防御工 事，并且俘虏了犹大人民。” 一个又一个城镇被占领、投降、被 摧毁，亚述的巨人在犹太的村庄里 开辟了一条毁灭之路。 我们从亚述的信息来源得知，他们 在向耶路撒冷行军的过程中，沿途 摧毁了 46 个设防城镇。犹大的城 邑，一个接着一个，被亚述人的强 大威力摧毁 。 现在，在亚述和耶路撒冷之间的道 路上，只有一个堡垒还坚守着。那 就是战略要塞城镇：拉吉。

paragraphs.en: the strategic fortress-town of Lachish. Atremendous battle raged as the Judeans fought desperately to save their homes, their lives, and their faith. Archaeologists have excavated Lachish and they have seen the giant earthen ramp that theAssyrian built to capture the city. TheAssyrians fired massed volleys of arrows at the city. The defenders returned fire. TheAssyrians built armored carts to get close to the walls. The Judeans threw torches down to burn the battering rams. TheAssyrians also used psychological warfare to terrify the inhabitants. We know fromAssyrian carvings that they took Judean prisoners and impaled them on
paragraphs.zh: 当犹太人拼命地拯救他们的家园、 生命和信仰时，一场激烈的战争爆 发了。 考古学家发掘出了拉吉，发现了亚 述建造的巨大土坡，用来占领这座 城市。 亚述不断向拉吉射击，后者还击。 亚述人建造了装甲车来接近城墙。 犹太人把火把扔下去，烧掉了撞锤。 亚述人还用心理战来恐吓居民。我 们从亚述的雕刻中得知，他们带走 了犹太囚犯，活生生地把他们钉在 了钉子上。

paragraphs.en: spikes while they were still alive. The point was to show their slow and agonizing death to their horrified countrymen standing on the walls. And then, and then... theAssyrians broke through. With the walls of Lachish breached, theAssyrians poured in, and a terrible slaughter of the people began. Archaeologists have found a cave near-by which is filled with over 1500 skeletons, a mass grave, the victims of a massacre. In fact, the fires that burned Lachish were so hot, that many of the stones in the city were turned into quicklime. And then the survivors of the killing
paragraphs.zh: 关键之处在于要向城墙上惊恐的犹 太人展示其同胞缓慢而痛苦的死亡 过程。 然后，然后……亚述人攻破了城防。 随着拉吉的城墙被攻破，亚述人蜂 拥而入，对人民的可怕屠杀开始了。 考古学家们发现了一个洞穴，是一 个集体墓穴，里面有 1500 多具骨 架，都是一场大屠杀的受害者。 事实上，一场大火烧毁了拉吉，城 中许多石头都烧成了石灰。 幸存者们被带到了亚述，成为奴隶。

paragraphs.en: were carried off toAssyria as slaves. We know so much about this battle because Sennacherib commissioned several wall-relief carvings for his palace to celebrate this victory. Now only Jerusalem was left.And once theAssyrians took it, this would be the end of the line for the Jews. Their capital city would be destroyed, King Hezekiah would be tortured to death, the people would be either massacred or enslaved, and the Jews and their religion would vanish from the face of the earth. Hezekiah was desperate, he offered Sennacherib 10,000 kilograms of silver and 1,000 kilograms of gold in exchange for peace. This was all the precious metal that he had. He even chiseled the gold off of the temple door to collect it.
paragraphs.zh: 我们能够比较完整地了解这场战 争，是因为西拿基立在他的宫殿装 饰有多块浮雕，以庆祝这场胜利。 现在只剩下耶路撒冷了。一旦亚述 人占领了这座城市，这将是犹太人 的末日。他们的首都将被摧毁，希 西家王将被折磨致死，人民或被屠 杀，或被奴役，犹太人及其宗教将 从地球上永远消失。 希西家绝望了，他向西拿基立供奉 了一万公斤的银和一千公斤的金子 来换取和平。这是他所拥有的所有 贵金属。为了集齐这个数字，他甚 至将神庙门上的金子凿了下来。

paragraphs.en: Hezekiah said: “I have done wrong. Withdraw from me, and I will pay whatever you demand of me.” Sennacherib accepted Hezekiah's payment... ... and then he attacked Jerusalem anyway. I cannot imagine the terror that the Judeans must have felt that morning in 701 BC as theAssyrian army arrived. Every other city in their Kingdom had fallen. They had heard horrifying stories of their countrymen being skinned alive, burned to death in their homes, raped, beheaded, or impaled on spikes. And now this terrifying, unstoppable enemy was at their
paragraphs.zh: 希西家说道： “我错了。退出我的国家，我愿意 付出任何代价。” 西拿基立接受了希西家的供奉…… ……然后继续进攻耶路撒冷。 我无法想象，在公元前 701 年的早 晨，当亚述军队到达耶路撒冷时， 犹太人内心的恐惧。他们王国的其 他城市都已经全部沦陷了。 他们听到了可怕的故事：他们的同 胞被活活剥皮，在家中被烧死，被 强奸，被斩首，或者被钉在钉子上。 现在，这个可怕的、不可阻挡的敌 人出现在了家门口！

paragraphs.en: gates! Adelegation was sent out to meet theAssyrian commander.And he told the Judeans that they could surrender and become slaves, and be sent elsewhere in the empire. Otherwise, they would "have to eat their own shit and drink their own urine" before the end of this siege. Then Sennacherib sent a letter to Hezekiah mocking his religion. The letter went: “Do not let the god you depend on deceive you when he says, ‘Jerusalem will not be given into the hands of the king ofAssyria.’Surely you have heard what the kings of Assyria have done to all the countries, destroying them completely.And will you be delivered? Did the gods of the
