paragraphs.zh: 对西西里岛的入侵。 这次入侵是雅典在战争中遭受的 最为灾难性的失败，一次性损失了 200 艘战船和数千名士兵。 这一失败使得亚西比德遭到了雅 典人的憎恨，却又对他无能为力， 因为他死于公元前 404 年，也就是 战争的最后一年。 随着战争的结束，雅典人陷入了痛 苦之中，亚西比德的很多敌人也都 在寻找替罪羊。于是他们把注意力 集中在亚西比德的老师苏格拉底 身上。 这是由于对亚西比德的愤怒，加之 苏格拉底不断的对社会的批判，导 致了这位哲学家在雅典树敌颇多。

paragraphs.en: Athens. In 399 BC Socrates was put on trial and found guilty of impiety and corrupting the youth. After being found guilty Socrates had many chances to escape. His enemies did not want him dead as much as they wanted him gone. But Socrates was already 71 years old, and suffering from illness. So instead of going into exile he decided to teach his students one more lesson, and show them how to face death with courage. He told his students "All of Philosophy is training for death." And then he drank poisonous hemlock. His final words were to his student Crito, he said: "Crito I owe a rooster toAsclepius,
paragraphs.zh: 公元前399年，苏格拉底受到审判， 被判犯有亵渎神明和腐化青年罪。 苏格拉底被判有罪后，有很多机会 可以逃脱。他的敌人更希望赶走 他，而不是将他置于死地。 但是苏格拉底已经 71 岁了，并且 饱受疾病的折磨。 因此，他决定不再流亡，而是给他 的学生们再上一课，教会他们如何 勇敢地面对死亡。他告诉他的学生 “所有的哲学都是为死亡而做的 训练。” 然后他喝下了有毒的铁杉。最后他 对学生克里托说： “克里托，我还欠阿斯克勒庇俄斯

paragraphs.en: please don't forget to pay the debt." And then he died. The Death of Socrates marks the end of the Golden Age ofAthens, and here is where we will end this class. Join me next time as we talk about Socrates's students and the greatest King in Greek history:Alexander the Great. I hope you guys enjoyed that lesson and want to learn more. If you want to see that painting: the Death of Socrates, it is on display in the Metropolitan Museum ofArt. I also recommend you check out Thucydides and his great work "The Peloponnesian War". And try reading 1 or 2 Greek plays,
paragraphs.zh: 一只公鸡，别忘了把这笔债还上。” 然后他去世了。 苏格拉底之死标志着雅典黄金时 代的结束，我们这节课也步入尾 声。 下节课我们将要谈谈苏格拉底的 学生、希腊历史上最伟大的国王： 亚历山大大帝。 我希望你们喜欢这节课，并且还想 深入学习。 如果你想看《苏格拉底之死》这幅 画的话，它正在大都会艺术博物馆 展出。 我还建议大家看看修昔底德和他 的伟大作品《伯罗奔尼撒战争》。 大家还可以尝试读一两部希腊戏

paragraphs.en: they are actually quite funny and modern feeling, even though they are 2500 years old! Thank you very much!
paragraphs.zh: 剧，尽管它们已经有 2500 年的历 史了，但仍然非常有趣，不乏现代 感！ 谢谢大家！

titleZh: 第十一讲：哲人与君王|亚里士多德给亚力山大大帝当家教

paragraphs.en: Hello everyone, and welcome back to our lecture series on world history. Today we will be learning about several philosophers andAlexander the Great. The years between 399 and 332 BC will be the focus of this lesson. Last time, I ended our lesson on Classical Greece with the death of Socrates. The picture that you are looking at is one of the most famous paintings in the world. It was painted in 1787 by Jacques Louis David and it is called Le Mort de Socrate which is French for "The Death of Socrates"
paragraphs.zh: 大家好，欢迎回到我们的世界历史 系列讲座。 今天我们会了解几位哲学家以及 亚历山大大帝。 这一讲我们将关注公元前 399 年 -332 年这段历史。 在上次古希腊一讲的结尾，我讲到 了苏格拉底之死。 这幅画是世界上最著名的的画作 之一。 它由画家雅克·路易·大卫创作于 即法语的“苏格拉底之死”。

paragraphs.en: In the painting, the old man sitting on the left is Plato, he was one of Socrates' students. But this painting is strange because Plato was only 28 years old when Socrates died. Also, Plato was not with Socrates that day, because he was sick in bed. However, if you look carefully at the painting, you see that Plato is facing away from the death of his teacher. In fact, the composition of the painting almost makes it seem like the scene is bursting out of Plato's mind. Maybe the artist is implying that the whole scene comes from Plato's memories. And that is fitting, because almost
paragraphs.zh: 在这幅画中，坐在左侧的老人是柏 拉图，他是苏格拉底的学生之一。 然而这样就有些奇怪了，因为苏格 拉底逝世时柏拉图仅有 28 岁。 而且，苏格拉底逝世那天柏拉图也 并不在场，因为他正卧病在床。 但是，如果你仔细审视这幅画，你 可以看到柏拉图是背对着自己垂 死的老师的。 事实上，这幅画的构图使画中情景 如同柏拉图脑海中浮现出来的一 般。 也许艺术家是在暗示整个场景不 过是柏拉图的回忆。 这倒也符合史实，因为我们所知道

paragraphs.en: everything we know about Socrates comes from the books that Plato wrote about him. Plato's books are written as dialogues: two characters will have a discussion about an idea. Often times Socrates is one of the people talking. Plato's most famous work, The Republic, is a conversation with Socrates about life, justice, and the best way to organize a society. In 387 BC Plato founded a school called "theAcademy" in honor of "Akademos"，a legendaryAthenian hero. This school lasted for 300 years and attracted some of the greatest minds in ancient Greece. In 384 BC, three years after Plato
paragraphs.zh: 的关于苏格拉底的一切都来自于 柏拉图所写的关于他的著作。 柏拉图的书是对话体的：两个人物 就某种观点展开讨论。 很多时候， 苏格拉底就是讨论者 之一。 柏拉图最著名的作品《理想国》， 就是他与苏格拉底关于生命、正 义，以及最佳社会组织形式的对 话。 公元前 387 年，柏拉图创立了一所 名为“学院”的学校，以纪念“阿 卡德莫斯”——一位具有传奇色彩 的雅典英雄。 这所学校延续了 300 年，吸引了很 多古希腊最伟大的思想家。 公元前 384 年,柏拉图创立学院三

paragraphs.en: founded theAcademy, a baby boy was born in the city of Stagira. His name was Aristotle. WhenAristotle was 17 years old, he came south to study under Plato. Aristotle adored Plato and he stayed at theAcademy for the next 20 years until Plato died in 347 BC. Aristotle wrote books on many different subjects including physics, astronomy, biology, logic, aesthetics, poetry, ethics, rhetoric, psychology, linguistics, economics, politics and government. WhenAristotle was 41 years old, he was hired by King Phillip II of Macedon and became the private tutor for Phillip's 13-year old son, Alexander. Alexander would grow up to
paragraphs.zh: 年后,一名男婴出生在斯塔吉拉 城。他的名字是亚里士多德。 亚里士多德 17 岁时，他来到希腊 南部，师从柏拉图。 亚里士多德崇拜柏拉图，他在学院 呆了 20 年，直到柏拉图在公元前 347 年去世。 亚里士多德的著作涵盖许多不同 的科目，包括物理学、天文学、生 物学、逻辑学、美学、诗歌、伦理、 修辞学、心理学、语言学、经济学、 政治和政府。 当亚里士多德 41 岁的时候，他被 马其顿国王菲利普二世雇佣，成为 他13岁儿子亚历山大的私人教师。 亚历山大长大后将成为亚历山大

paragraphs.en: becomeAlexander the Great, one of the greatest conquerors in history. So look at that: 4 men, 4 generations, 4 of the most influential people in history, all connected by teacher-student relationships. I often think about the years between 550 and 300 BC, because many of the great world philosophers lived during that time, not just in Greece, but also inAsia. The Buddha, Confucius, Sun zi, Mo zi, Meng zi, Socrates, Plato, Aristotle: they all lived at this time. The Buddha was only 12-years older than Confucius! While Mo-zi and Socrates were almost the exact same age! Those 250 years really were an
paragraphs.zh: 大帝，他是历史上最伟大的征服者 之一。 看看他们吧：四个男人，四个世代， 四个历史上最具影响力的人，都是 由师生关系联系在一起的。 我常常思考公元前550-300年这段 时间，因为很多最伟大的哲学家都 生活在这一时期，不仅限于古希 腊，亚洲亦是如此。 佛陀、孔子、孙子、墨子、孟子、 苏格拉底、柏拉图、亚里士多德： 他们全都生活在这一时期。 佛陀仅仅比孔子大了 12 岁！ 而墨子和苏格拉底几乎是同岁！ 那 250 年真的是“哲学的时代”。

paragraphs.en: "Age of Philosophy." Now let's return to politics. WhileAristotle was studying with Plato, the Greek world was changing. As we learned earlier, the Peloponnesian War had badly weakened bothAthens and Sparta. In the meantime,Alexander's father, Phillip II, was turning Macedon into a mighty force. From 364 until 336 BC, he waged a series of wars and conquered all of Greece except Sparta. Some parts he ruled directly, other parts he organized into a vassal state called the "League of Corinth." Phillip II was the first King to really unify Greece.
paragraphs.zh: 现在我们继续讨论政治。 在亚里士多德跟随柏拉图学习时， 希腊正在发生变化。 我们之前学到过，伯罗奔尼撒战争 极大地削弱了雅典和斯巴达。 与此同时，亚历山大的父亲菲利普 二世让马其顿的实力愈加强大。 从公元前 364 年到 336 年，他发动 数次战争，征服了除斯巴达以外的 希腊全境。 部分领土由他直接统治，其它领土 则被他划分为一个被称为“柯林斯 同盟”的附庸国。 菲利普二世是第一位真正统一希 腊的君主。

paragraphs.en: During those years, Phillip married a Princess named Olympias. They were married in 357 BC, and their sonAlexander was born the next year in 356 BC. Olympias was fiercely devoted to her son and taught him that he would change the world. Aristotle was Alexander's tutor during these years. But then, in October 336 BC, Phillip II was assassinated while attending a wedding. So his crown passed into the hands ofAlexander, who was only 20-years old. Few men in history have possessed Alexander's combination of ambition, courage, self-confidence and military genius.
paragraphs.zh: 在那一时期，菲利普迎娶了一位名 叫奥林匹亚丝的公主。 他们于公元前 357 年成婚，他们的 儿子于次年出生。 奥林匹亚丝全心全意地养育这个 儿子，她告诉儿子，他将来会改变 世界。 当时，亚里士多德担任了亚历山大 的老师。 但在公元前 336 年 10 月，菲利普 二世在参加婚礼时被暗杀。 于是，他的王位就传给了年仅 20 岁的亚历山大。 历史上很少有人像亚历山大那样 集雄心、勇气、自信和军事天才于 一身。

paragraphs.en: Alexander was not content to be just the King of Greece, he wanted to conquer the whole Earth! Alexander began his war in 335 BC by going north to defeat the Thracians and Gauls. But while he was in the north, subduing the Gauls, Thebes and Athens rebelled. SoAlexander marched south. He defeated the Thebans in battle and burned their city to the ground. This terrifiedAthens and they surrendered without a fight. ThenAlexander left Greece under the command of a general, and prepared for the invasion of Persia. In 334 BC Alexander crossed the Hellespont with 48,000 soldiers.
paragraphs.zh: 亚历山大不满足于仅仅做希腊的 国王，他想征服全世界! 亚历山大于公元前335年北上击败 了色雷斯人和高卢人，开始了他的 战争。 但是当他在北方征服高卢人时，底 比斯和雅典反叛了。 于是亚历山大又驱兵南下。 他击败了底比斯人，并将他们的城 市焚为焦土。 这让雅典人大为骇然，便不战而降 了。 亚历山大指派一名将军留守希腊， 准备去侵略波斯。 公元前 334 年，亚历山大率领四万 八千名名士兵越过达达尼尔海峡。

paragraphs.en: When he reached the shores ofAsia, he threw his spear into the sand, showing that he would claim the continent through war! At that point they were near the legendary site of Troy, soAlexander stopped at the tomb ofAchilles to pay his respects. In fact,Alexander believed that Achilles was his ancestor, and his personal role model. Alexander was such a fan of Achilles that he even slept with a copy of the Illiad under his pillow. Afew days after enteringAsia, a Persian army of about 40,000 men arrived to stopAlexander. The two armies met on May 3rd, 334 BC at the Battle of the Granicus River.
paragraphs.zh: 当他到达亚洲海岸时，他把长矛插 进了沙滩，表明他将通过战争来占 领这片大陆! 他们当时正处在传奇之城特洛伊 附近，于是亚历山大驻足像阿喀琉 斯的坟墓致敬。 事实上，亚历山大认为阿喀琉斯是 他的祖先，也是他的个人榜样。 亚历山大是阿喀琉斯的崇拜者，他 甚至在枕头下放了一本《伊利亚 德》，每日枕着入睡。 在亚历山大进入亚洲几天后，一支 约 4 万人的波斯军队赶来阻击他。 两军于公元前334 年 5 月3 日在格 拉尼卡斯河战役中开始了交锋。

paragraphs.en: The fighting was fierce and Alexander fought alongside his men with his own sword and spear, as he did in every one of his battles. He even killed several Persian officers during the fighting. But then a Persian nobleman named Rhoisakes struckAlexander in the head with an axe. Alexander's helmet saved his life, but he was momentarily stunned. At that moment another Persian nobleman named Spithridates came atAlexander from the side. Spithridates raised his arm to kill the stunned King, but just before he struck, an officer named Cleitus the Black sliced his arm off, killing him and savingAlexander's life.
paragraphs.zh: 战斗很激烈，亚历山大披坚执锐与 他的士兵并肩作战，而他在每场战 斗中都会身先士卒。 他甚至在战斗中杀死了几名波斯 军官。 但后来一个名叫罗萨克斯的波斯 贵族用斧头砍中了亚历山大的头。 亚历山大戴着头盔，保住了性命， 但他也被打晕了。 就在这时，另一个名叫斯庇特里达 的波斯贵族从侧方杀向亚历山大。 斯庇特里达举起武器要杀死这位 被打晕的国王，但是就在他要一击 得手之时，克雷塔斯砍掉了他的手 臂并将他杀死，从而救了亚历山 大。

paragraphs.en: Alexander recovered his senses after the blow, and pushed on to win the battle. The victory at the Battle of the Granicus leftAlexander in command ofAnatolia. Alexander moved south, capturing the provincial capital at Susa and the other majorAnatolian cities. The people living here were Greek, so they greetedAlexander as a liberator on his march south. After the battle of the Granicus Alexander's next major resistance came at Halicarnasus. Halicarnasus was a major port, and as you can see in this illustration, it was famous for its tomb to the Persian governor Mausolus. In fact, this tomb was one of the 7
paragraphs.zh: 亚历山大恢复了意识，他继续作 战，赢得了战斗。 格拉纳克斯战役的胜利使亚历山 大掌管了安纳托利亚。 亚历山大南下，占领了省首府苏萨 和其他主要的安纳托利亚城市。 住在这里的人都是希腊人，所以他 们在亚历山大南下行军时把他当 作解放者来欢迎。 在格拉纳克斯战役之后，亚历山大 在哈利卡那斯遇到了下一波强烈 的抵抗。 哈利卡那苏斯是一个重要的港口， 正如你在这幅图片中看到的，它以 波斯总督茂苏洛斯的陵墓而闻名。 事实上，这座古墓是古代世界七大

paragraphs.en: wonders of the ancient world, and it is the origin of the English word "Mausoleum" which means a magnificent tomb. The tomb was built byArtemisia II in 353 BC, to express the grief she felt after the death of her husband Mausulos. Artemisia II was a descendant of Artemisia I, who was a naval commander in Xerxes invasion of Greece 127 years before. The Persians fought hard at Halicarnasus, but after several months they were forced to retreat. After he captured the city, Alexander turned it over toAda, the younger sister ofArtemisia II, who swore loyalty to him in return. The next year, in 333 BC,
paragraphs.zh: 奇 迹 之 一 ， 也 是 英 文 单 词 “Mausoleum”的来源，原意是“宏 伟的坟墓”。 这座坟墓是公元前353年由艾特西 亚二世建造的，用来表达她在丈夫 莫苏罗斯去世后的悲痛之情。 艾特西亚二世是艾特西亚一世的 后代，后者曾在 127 年前薛西斯入 侵希腊时担任海军指挥官。 波斯人在哈利卡那苏斯顽强作战， 但几个月后他们被迫撤退了。 亚力山大攻陷城池后，就把城交给 艾特西亚二世的妹妹亚达，她亦宣 誓效忠亚历山大。 第二年，在公元前 333 年，亚历山

paragraphs.en: Alexander marched through Anatolia. On November 5th, he fought a battle with a second PersianArmy near the town of Issus. This PersianArmy had 100,000 soldiers compared toAlexander's 40,000, and it was led by Darius III, the Persian Emperor himself. At first, the battle went badly for the Macedonians. But thenAlexander saw an opening and led a cavalry charge directly at Darius himself. When Darius sawAlexander and his men riding towards him, he panicked and fled the battle. When the Persian soldiers saw their King running away, they lost their courage, and the battle turned into a
paragraphs.zh: 大穿过了安纳托利亚。 11 月 5 日，他在伊苏斯镇附近与另 一支波斯军队作战。 这支波斯军队有 10 万士兵，由波 斯皇帝大流士三世亲自指挥，而亚 历山大只有 4 万人。 起初，战况对马其顿一方不利。 但后来亚历山大看到了一个空当， 直接率领骑兵向大流士本人冲锋。 当大流士看到亚历山大和他的人 马朝他冲来，他惊慌失措，逃离了 战场。 当波斯士兵看到他们的国王逃跑 时，士气顿丧，继而溃败。

paragraphs.en: Persian defeat. After the battle,Alexander captured Darius’wife Stateira I, his daughters Stateira II and Drypetis, and even his mother Sisygambis! Darius had been over-confident, and he had brought his family with him on the campaign. Now they wereAlexander's prisoners. However,Alexander treated all four women with great respect. They were royalty and were extremely valuable prisoners. The Battle of Issus ensured Alexander's control over all of Canaan andAnatolia, with one exception. The Phoenician city of Tyre still
paragraphs.zh: 战争结束后，亚历山大俘虏了大流 士的妻子坦吉拉一世、女儿坦吉拉 二世和德莱佩蒂斯，甚至还有他的 母亲西西甘比斯！ 大流士过于自信了，他让全家老小 随同出征。 现在他们全都成了亚历山大的俘 虏。 然而，亚历山大对四个女人都很尊 敬。 她们是皇族，是极有价值的囚犯。 伊苏斯战役确保了亚历山大对迦 南和安纳托利亚的控制，只有一个 例外。 腓尼基人的推罗城仍然在反抗他。

paragraphs.en: held out against him. So two months after his victory at Issus,Alexander began the siege of Tyre. At the time, Tyre was on an island, so they felt protected against any invasion. ButAlexander would tolerate no resistance. After his victory at Issus, he had acquired a navy. So he used it to cut off all trade with Tyre. ThenAlexander started building a kilometer-long earthen causeway to connect Tyre to the mainland. For the rest of the year,Alexander's causeway inched closer and closer to the island.
paragraphs.zh: 因此，在他在伊苏斯战役中获胜两 个月后，亚历山大开始围攻推罗 城。 当时，推罗是一座岛屿，所以他们 觉得自己不会受到任何入侵。 但亚历山大不会容忍任何抵抗。 他在伊苏获胜后，收编了一支海 军。 现在他用这支海军阻断了推罗城 的一些贸易往来。 后来，亚历山大开始修建一条长达 一公里的土堤，把推罗和大陆连接 起来。 一年间，亚历山大的长堤一点一点 地靠近小岛。

paragraphs.en: The Tyrians tried to stop them, and even once burned downAlexander's siege towers. But he just rebuilt them and kept coming. Eventually the causeway got close enough to the city that Alexander could move up his siege engines and attack the walls directly. And then after punching a hole in the walls,Alexander's army broke though and slaughtered the garrison. Alexander was so furious at the Tyrian resistance, that he slaughtered 8,000 men, women and children when he took the city. And then he sold the remaining 30,000 survivors into slavery. After Tyre, the road was open to
paragraphs.zh: 推罗人试图阻止他们，甚至一度烧 毁了亚历山大的攻城塔。 但他还是一直重建、一直卷土重 来。 最终，堤道离这座城市足够近了， 亚历山大可以用他的攻城车直接 攻击城墙。 后来，亚历山大的军队在墙上打穿 了一个洞，攻进城去，杀死了守军。 亚历山大对推罗人的抵抗非常愤 怒，当他占领这座城市时，他屠杀 了 8000 名男女老幼。 然后他把剩下的3万名幸存者贩卖 为奴隶。 攻陷推罗后，前往埃及的道路就打

paragraphs.en: Egypt. The only resistance left was the fortress of Gaza. Alexander took the city after a month-long siege in October of 332 BC by building a giant earthen mound which would allow his army to breach the walls. During the siegeAlexander himself was injured in the shoulder, but in the end the fortress was taken. Alexander killed the Persian commander by dragging him to death behind his chariot; it was a deliberate imitation of howAchilles had treated Hector's body in the Illiad. Then all of the male inhabitants of Gaza were massacred, and all of the women and children were sold into
paragraphs.zh: 通了。 仅存的抵抗力量是加沙的堡垒。 公元前 332 年 10 月，亚历山大建 造了一个巨大的土丘，让他的军队 可以攻破城墙。在长达一个月的围 攻之后，亚历山大占领了这座城 市。 在围城过程中，亚历山大的肩膀受 了伤，但最后堡垒被攻陷了。 亚历山大把波斯司令官拖在战车 后面，把他拖死了;这是在效仿《伊 利亚特》中，阿喀琉斯对赫克托的 尸体的作为。 那时，加沙所有的男性居民都被屠 杀，所有的妇女和儿童都被卖为奴 隶。

paragraphs.en: slavery. Once Gaza fell, the rest of Egypt quickly surrendered. Like the Ionian Greeks, the Egyptians welcomed Alexander as a liberator. While he was in Egypt,Alexander founded a city which he named after himself: the city ofAlexandria. ThisAlexandria would go on to become one of the largest cities in the Mediterranean, and an intellectual and cultural center of the Greek world. But all of that lay in the future. It was now the end of the year, in 332 BC. In 3 years of fightingAlexander had conquered half of the Persian
paragraphs.zh: 加沙一经沦陷，埃及其他地区很快 就都投降了。 和爱奥尼亚的希腊人一样，埃及人 欢迎亚历山大，把他视为解放者。 在埃及期间，亚历山大建立了一座 城市并以自己的名字将其命名为 亚历山大港。 这座亚历山大港将继续成为地中 海最大的城市之一，同时也是古希 腊地区的知识和文化中心。 但这一切都是后话了。 时间来到了公元前 332 年的末尾。 三年征讨，亚历山大征服了波斯帝 国的半壁江山。

paragraphs.en: empire. Now he prepared to conquer the rest. But that's a story for our next class. Please join me as we finish the story ofAlexander the Great. Thank you very much. Okay everyone, I hope you enjoyed that lesson and want to learn more. I started the lesson with the painting "The Death of Socrates", you can see this painting for yourself at the Metropolitan Museum ofArt. Additionally, there are 3 ancient writers who tell us about Alexander the Great. These are our primary sources. The first and most important is the
paragraphs.zh: 现在他准备征服余下的疆土。 但这将是我们下节课的主要内容。 欢迎继续收听，我们将讲完亚历山 大大帝的故事。 谢谢大家。 好了，各位，我希望你们喜欢这一 课，并且想学到更多知识。 这一讲的开头我提到了画作《苏格 拉底之死》，你可以在大都会艺术 博物馆看到这幅画。 另外，有三位古代作家向我们讲述 了亚历山大大帝的故事。 这些是我们参考的主要文献资料。 第一，也是最重要的，是罗马历史

paragraphs.en: Roman HistorianArrian and his book: The Campaigns of Alexander. The Second is Parallel Lives in Plutarch. This isALSO a very important source for Roman history. The third is The History of Alexander by Quintus Curtius Rufus. Additionally, I highly recommend the BBC Documentary In the Footsteps of Alexander the Great, as present by Michael Wood. It was made in 1998, so its 20 years old at this point, but it’s still very good. Okay that's it. Thank you !
paragraphs.zh: 学家阿里安和他的著作: The 山大的战役》）。 第二个是第二个是普鲁塔克的 Parallel Lives（《希腊罗马名人 传》）。 这也是研究罗马史的重要资料。 第三部是昆图斯·库尔提乌斯·鲁 （《亚历山大的历史》）。 另外，我强烈推荐英国广播公司纪 AlexandertheGreat（《亚历山大 大帝的足迹》），由迈克尔·伍德主 讲。 该片制作于 1998 年，所以它是 20 年前的片子了，但是它看起来仍然 十分优秀。 这一讲到此结束。谢谢大家！

titleEn: The Empire of Alexander the Great
titleZh: 第十二讲：亚历山大大帝|不败的男人，向未知世界进发

paragraphs.en: Hello, everyone and welcome back to our lecture series on World History. Today is the second part of our story aboutAlexander the Great. We will be focusing on howAlexander built his empire and what happened to it immediately after his death. This means we will be focusing on approximately 30 years between 332 and 301 BC. Alexander spent the winter of 332 camped with his army on the Nile. Then he left his main force behind to re-provision and rest and took a small group of friends with him on an adventure. They crossed the Egyptian desert on a trip to the Siwa oasis.
paragraphs.zh: 各位好，欢迎回到我们世界历史系 列讲座。 今天是我们关于亚历山大大帝的 第二部分讲述，我们主要会聚焦在 他是如何建立了庞大的帝国并且 在他死后该帝国将何去何从。这就 意味着我们要放眼于公元前332年 到公元前 301 年这将近 30 年的时 间线。 公元前 332 年冬，亚历山大将他的 军队驻扎在了尼罗河畔。 随后他留下大部队休养生息，作后 防之用，率领一小支由朋友组成的 队伍踏上了冒险之路。 他们穿越埃及沙漠，前往锡瓦绿 洲。

paragraphs.en: Siwa is a remarkable place. Underground water comes to the surface there and creates a lush forest in the middle of a barren desert. At the oasis there is an oracle of the Egyptian GodAmun-Ra. This is whoAlexander had come to see. The Oracle toldAlexander was both a living god and the legitimate pharaoh of Egypt. Encouraged by this prophecy, Alexander and his friends then travelled back across the desert and rejoined the army on the Nile. Then in 331 BC, the army left Egypt and headed east into Canaan and Mesopotamia. They encountered no serious resistance until they reached
paragraphs.zh: 锡瓦是一个神奇的地方。地下水流 淌到地面上，在贫瘠的沙漠中形成 了一片茂密的森林。 在锡瓦绿洲有埃及神阿蒙·拉的神 谕，而这正是亚历山大此行要去探 访的。 神谕说亚历山大生来就是活着的 上帝，生来就是埃及的合法长老。 受到了这一预言的鼓舞，亚历山大 和他的朋友再次穿越沙漠，返回尼 罗河畔与军队汇合。 到了公元前 331 年，亚历山大军队 离开埃及，向东出发，前往迦南和 美索布达米亚。 一路上，他们攻城掠地，没有遭遇 太多的抵抗，直到他们到达了美索

paragraphs.en: northern Mesopotamia. There, near the town of Gaugamela, Darius arrived with an army to meet Alexander once more in battle. For a third time,Alexander fought against a Persian army which outnumbered him, and for a third timeAlexander prevailed because of better leadership. When the battle was lost, Darius turned and fled. He would never again be able to raise an army that could seriously challenge Alexander. As Darius fled east over the Zagros mountains,Alexander moved south and captured the Persian city of Babylon. From Babylon,Alexander moved further east to capture one of the
paragraphs.zh: 不达米亚北部。 在高加米拉城镇附近，大流士带着 军队严阵以待，要再次与亚历山大 一决高下。 这是亚历山大第三次与敌方人数 远胜于他的波斯军队作战，也是亚 历山大第三次凭借着更胜一筹的 指挥以少胜多。 战败后，大流士仓皇而逃。从这之 后，他再也没能重新组织出一支能 够与亚历山大抗衡的军队。 当大流士向西边的 逃跑时，亚 历山大向南入侵，并占领了波斯城 市巴比伦。 亚历山大从巴比伦继续向东出发， 去占领波斯的首都之一——苏萨

paragraphs.en: Persian capitals at Susa. Next he moved east to Persepolis, the other Persian capital.And here the Persians made one last attempt to stop him. The battle occurred on January 20th, 330 BC.AsAlexander and his men were marching through a mountain pass called the Persian Gates, a small group of Persian soldiers ambushed them. The Persians, even though badly outnumbered, managed to inflict heavy losses onAlexander's men. They held off the Greeks for 30 days, untilAlexander found a way around them, and was able to beat them with his larger army. Now the last obstacle between Alexander and Persepolis was gone. The Persian Capital was captured in
paragraphs.zh: 城。 接下来他向东前往另一个波斯首 都珀塞波利斯。在珀塞波利斯，波 斯人做了最后的抵抗，试图阻止亚 历山大。 最后的战役发生于公元前 330 年 2 月 20 日，当亚历山大带领军队途 径一个名为波斯门的山口时，一小 群波斯士兵在此伏击。 这群波斯人即便是人数远不及对 手，却仍设法成功地给亚历山大的 军队造成了重大损失。他们抵抗了 希腊人 30 天，直到亚历山大找到 了解决办法，能够用更强大的军队 击败他们。 现在亚历山大和珀塞波利斯之间 的障碍已经扫除。公元前 330 年初 期，波斯首都就被占领了。

paragraphs.en: early 330 BC. Persepolis was a magnificent city, with great palaces, garden, temples and monuments. It was the ceremonial capital of the Empire. After staying in Persepolis for five months,Alexander and his men became very drunk one evening at a party. And then on a drunken impulse, they decided that the Persians should pay for burning downAthens 150 years before. So they began setting fire to the palace. The fire spread and most of the city burned down. Alexander regretted his decision the next morning when he had sobered up. But by that point half of the city was gone.
paragraphs.zh: 珀塞波利斯是一座宏伟的城市，拥 有伟大的宫殿、花园、寺庙和纪念 碑。 它是帝国的仪式之都。 在珀塞波利斯待了 5 个月后，亚历 山大和他的部下在一个晚宴上喝 醉了。 在醉酒的冲动下，他们决定波斯人 应该为 150 年前烧毁雅典付出代 价。 所以他们放火烧了这个宫殿，火势 蔓延，大部分城市都被烧毁。 第二天早上清醒后，亚历山大后悔 不已。 但那时，这个城市的一半 已经化为灰烬了。

paragraphs.en: ButAlexander had more pressing issues to worry about. In June he continued his pursuit of Darius.And in July of 330 he closed in. As Darius fled fromAlexander, he lost the remainder of his power. His followers treated him with contempt for losing an empire, and one of his governors, and a cousin named Bessus, took him prisoner. Then Bessus tied Darius up and placed him in a cart. But just at that momentAlexander's cavalry came near. So in a panic Bessus stabbed Darius with his spear, and then fled north with a small force. AGreek soldier found the dying king a few minutes later. Darius asked this soldier for some water, and the soldier brought it to him in his helmet.And then Darius
paragraphs.zh: 但是亚历山大有更紧迫的事情要 去操心。公元前 330 年 6 月，他继 续追击大流士，7 月，他逐步逼近。 当年大流士逃走的时候，他失去了 其余人的支持，他原先的追随者以 对待亡国君主的方式对待他，他的 一名总督伙同他的侄子贝苏斯将 他囚禁。 贝苏斯将大流士绑起来放在马车 上。正在此时亚历山大的骑兵追了 上来，慌乱之中贝苏斯用长矛刺伤 了大流士，随后携一小支队伍向北 逃去。 几分钟后，一个希腊士兵发现了将 死的国王。 大流士向这名士兵要水喝，士兵用 头盔盛了水给他喝，随后大流士就 死了。

paragraphs.en: died. With the death of Darius III, the Achaemenid Persian Empire ended. This was a great disappointment to Alexander who wanted to capture Darius alive. Alexander took Darius' signet ring off of his hand, and proclaimed himself the new Persian emperor. ThenAlexander sent Darius' body back to Persepolis, and gave him a magnificent funeral. Afterwards,Alexander moved north is pursuit of Besus and travelled as far as the Caspian Sea. In the summer of 329 BC, Alexander captured Besus and executed him. Alexander moved north and defeated Scythian nomads at the
paragraphs.zh: 随着大流士三世的死亡，阿契美尼 德波斯帝国就此终结。 对亚历山大来说，没能生擒大流士 是一个极大的遗憾。 亚历山大从大流士的手上拿走了 印章戒指，宣称自己是新的波斯皇 帝。 随后他将大流士的尸体送回 波斯波利斯，并将其风光大葬。 随后，亚历山大继续向北追击贝苏 斯，一路追到了里海。 公元前 329 年夏，亚历山大抓住了 贝苏斯并将其处决。 亚历山大继续向北前进，并于公元 前 329 年 10 月在药杀水战役中击

paragraphs.en: battle of Jaxartes in October in 329 BC. NowAlexander had defeated the very last opposition to him. He was the unchallenged emperor of the Persian EmpireAND Greece. He had put together the largest empire that the world had ever seen. He had never lost a battle.And he was the richest and most powerful man on Earth. But it was not enough!Alexander wanted to conquer the whole world, and he wanted to keep going. Alexander's war was half a war of conquest, but is was also half a journey of exploration.Alexander wanted to keep going until he reached the Great Ocean and came to the edge of the world.
paragraphs.zh: 败了赛西亚游牧民族。 亚历山大已经击败了最后一个对 手，现在他是波斯帝国和希腊无可 争议的统治者。 他统一了当时世界上最大的帝国。 他从未输过一场战斗。 他是当时 地球上最富有，最有权势的人。 但是这还不够！亚历山大想要征服 全世界，他想要继续前进。 亚历山大的战争是一半是征服之 战，一半是探索之旅。 亚历山大 想要继续前进，直到他看见大洋， 抵达世界的边缘。

paragraphs.en: For the next three years, from 329 to 326 BC,Alexander and his army explored and fought their way across what is now modern Pakistan andAfghanistan. For three yearsAlexander and his men marched across deserts and plains. They climbed mountains and traversed steamy jungles. They fought half a dozen battles, and they won every time. In May of 326 BC, they reached the Indus River. The border of India. Today, that particular tributary of the Indus is called the Jhelum river. Back then the Greeks called it the "Hydapses." Here is what it looks like. And on the bank of the Hydapses river,Alexander fought a battle with
paragraphs.zh: 接 下 来 的 3 年 中 ， 即 公 元 前 329-326 年，亚历山大带领军队一 路打到了如今的巴基斯坦和阿富 汗地区。 三年来，亚历山大和部下穿越了沙 漠和平原。 他们翻山越岭，穿越 水汽蒸腾的丛林。 他们一共打了 六场战斗，每次都赢了。 公元前 326 年 5 月，他们到达了印 度河——印度的边境。 如今，印度河的这条特殊支流被称 为杰赫勒姆河。 当时希腊人称它为“海德帕斯”。 图片显示了这条河的样子。 在海德帕斯河岸上，亚历山大与印 度国王波鲁斯进行了一场战斗。

paragraphs.en: an Indian King named Porus. Porus was a brave King and he had war elephants. It was a fierce battle. BUT,Alexander's soldiers were the best in the world at this point. They were all hardened, experienced veterans.And they defeated Porus's army. Alexander captured King Porus in the battle. But he admired the Indian King for his bravery. So he allowed Porus to keep his lands in return for swearing loyalty to him. This is the best map that the Greeks had at the time. As you can see,Alexander stood on the edge of the known world. No Greeks had ever travelled this far before.AndAlexander wanted to keep going.
paragraphs.zh: 波鲁斯是一个勇敢的国王，他还有 战象。 这是一场激烈的战斗。 但是，亚历山大拥有当时世界上最 优秀的士兵。他们个个经验丰富， 作战优良。 最后，亚历山大的军 队击败了波鲁斯的军队。 亚历山大在战斗中俘虏了波鲁斯 国王。 但亚历山大钦佩印度国王 的勇敢，因此允许波鲁斯保留他的 土地，前提是要波鲁斯对他许以效 忠。 这是当时希腊人最好的地图。 正如你所看见的，亚历山大到达了 已知世界的边缘。没有一个希腊人 曾走得如此远。然而，亚历山大想 要继续前进。

paragraphs.en: Alexander didn't know it, but he still had a-ways to go before he reached the Ocean at the end of the world. He would have had to defeat the Nanda empire of India, and then cross the jungles of south-eastAsia or the Himalayan mountains. AND THEN he would have had to invade China, which was in the middle of the Warring States period. But I have no doubt thatAlexander would have kept going until he either reached the Pacific Ocean or died trying. SoAlexander urged his men onwards. They would plunge into the unknown world and fight everyone who stood in their way. But at this point, one of his generals named Coenus approached him.
paragraphs.zh: 亚历山大不知道的是，在他到达世 界尽头的海洋之前还有很长的路 要走。 他将不得不打败印度的南 达帝国，然后越过东南亚的丛林或 是翻越喜马拉雅山脉。 然后，他将不得不入侵战国时期的 中国。 但我毫不怀疑亚历山大会继续前 进直到最后，要么到达太平洋，要 么死。 所以亚历山大一直敦促他的部下。 他们要闯入未知的世界，并与阻挡 他们的人作斗争。 但在这时，他的一位将军科纳斯找 到了他。

paragraphs.en: Coenus toldAlexander, that the men did not want to keep going. They wanted to turn around. These soldiers had followed their King for 8 years. They had marched over 5000 kilometers. They had fought more than a dozen battles and won them all. Soldiers who had joined as boys, had grown up into men while servingAlexander. But these men were human, made of flesh and blood; they were homesick; they were scared of marching into the unknown; they wanted to go home. WhenAlexander urged them onwards intoAsia, they refused. They had gone far enough. Alexander was furious at his men. He flew into a rage and sulked in his tent for several days.
paragraphs.zh: 科纳斯告诉亚历山大，部下们不想 继续前进，他们想要回去。 这些士兵跟随他们的国王已经8年 了。他们已经行进了5000多公里。 他们已经打了十几次战斗并且全 胜。 一些士兵加入军队时尚是男 孩，现如今已经长大成人。 但这些人是有血有肉的人；他们想 家了； 他们害怕进入未知世界； 他们想回家。 当亚历山大催促他们前往亚洲时， 他们拒绝了。他们已经走得够远 了。 亚历山大勃然大怒，他在帐篷中暴 跳如雷，愠怒数日。

paragraphs.en: But in the end, he had to accept the will of his army. They would go no further. And so 326 BC, Alexander turned back, and they began the two-year-long journey home. In Spring of 324 BC, the army reached Mesopotamia, and Alexander established his capital city at Babylon. Alexander's wars were complete. He ruled the largest empire in history. ButAlexander was not done. He immediately set to work planning new military campaigns. He would raise a new army and he would take this one west, conquering all of Europe until he reached theAtlantic Ocean.
paragraphs.zh: 但是最后，他不得不接受士兵的意 愿。他们不会更进一步了。 所以在公元前 326 年，亚历山大踏 上归程，开始了长达 2 年的回乡之 路。 公元前 324 年春，军队到达了美索 布达米亚，亚历山大定都巴比伦。 亚历山大的仗打完了。现在他统治 着历史上最大的帝国。 但是亚历山大还没完。他立即着手 策划新的军事行动。 他将组建一支新的军队，并将这支 军队带到西部，征服整个欧洲，直 到他到达大西洋。

paragraphs.en: Or perhaps he would go south and launch a new campaign intoArabia. But thenAlexander suddenly became very ill on May 30th, 323 BC. For 12 days he lingered in bed, suffering from a mysterious illness. Eventually his generals realized that he was not going to recover. So all of his men formed a line and silently filed-past him. Alexander looked at all of them as walked by. He was too weak to speak much, but he made eye-contact with each soldier as he passed. His men knew they were saying goodbye to their beloved King, to the man they had followed for 12
paragraphs.zh: 或者，他会向南出发，向阿拉伯国 家发动新的战争。 但是在公元前 323 年 5 月 30 日， 亚历山大突然病了。 他缠绵病榻 12 天，患上了一种奇 怪的疾病。 最终，他的将士意识到他无法痊愈 了。 所以他所有的部下都排成一排，然 后默默地依次走过亚历山大面前。 亚历山大看着走过的所有人。 他 太虚弱，无法说话，但是他与每个 面前经过的士兵进行了目光接触。 他的部下知道他们正在向心爱的 国王说再见，正在向已经追随了 12 年的男人说再见。

paragraphs.en: years. ButAlexander was still quite young. His wife Roxanna was pregnant, but the baby had not yet been born. Alexander had made no plans for the succession, so nobody knew who the heir would be for the world's largest Empire. Alexander's generals, his wives, his mother, they all crowded around the dying emperor. They asked him: "Who, who should be your heir?" Alexander's final words: "To...the...strongest." And with that, he died. And so,Alexander the Great died, on June 12th, 323 BC. He was only 32 years old. He left behind him the world's
paragraphs.zh: 但是亚历山大是那么的年轻。他的 妻子罗克珊娜才刚身怀六甲，孩子 尚未出生。 亚历山大从未对继承人有过打算， 所以没人知道谁将是这个世界上 最大的帝国的下一任主人。 亚历山大的将军们，妻子们，母亲， 都围在垂死的国王前。他们问： “谁，谁应该是你的继承人？” 亚历山大临终之言是： “给……最……强壮的人。” 说完，他就去世了。 就这样，亚历山大于公元前 323 年 6 月 12 日去世了。年仅 32 岁。 他身后留下了自己打下的世界上

paragraphs.en: largest empire, and no clear line of succession. So of course, a civil war broke out as his generals began to fight each other over the world's greatest prize. These civil wars between his generals went on for more than 20 years. During this fighting Alexander's wives, his mother, and his child were all killed. When the majority of the fighting ended in 301 BC, Alexander's Empire had been divided into 4 Kingdoms, each ruled by a different one of his generals. His general Ptolemy ruled Egypt. His general Cassander ruled Greece and Macedon. His general Lysimachus ruled Thrace and EasternAnatolia.
paragraphs.zh: 最强大的帝国，而且没有明确继承 人。 很显然，随着他的将军们开始互相 争夺世界上最伟大的奖赏，内战爆 发了。 他的将军们之间的内战持续了 20 多年。 在这场内战中，亚历山大 的妻子、母亲和孩子都被杀死了。 公元前 301 年，当大多数战斗结束 时，亚历山大的帝国已经被瓜分为 4 个王国，每个王国都由他曾经的 将军统治。 他的将军托勒密统治了埃及。 他的将军卡山德统治着希腊和马 其顿。 他的将军莱西麦克斯统治了色雷 斯和安纳托利亚东部。

paragraphs.en: And his general Seleucus ruled Mesopotamia, Persia, and Alexander's territory in the Central Asia. Anew era of History had begun. The Greeks had become the most powerful culture in Western Eurasia. Greek Kings ruled all the land in-between the Italian Peninsula and the Indus River. This period in history lasted until the rise of the Roman Empire, 300 years later. Because the Greeks were so culturally dominant, and because the Greeks called themselves "Hellenes", historians call this period in human History: "The HellenisticAge". During this time all of the people
paragraphs.zh: 他的将军塞琉古统治了美索不达 米亚，波斯以及亚历山大在中亚的 领土。 历史的新时代开始了。 希腊人已 成为欧亚大陆西部最强大的人。 希腊国王们统治了意大利半岛和 印度河之间的所有土地。 这段历史一直持续到300年后罗马 帝国崛起。 因为希腊人在文化上占据主导地 位，并且因为希腊人称自己为“希 腊人”，所以历史学家将这一时期 的人类历史称为：“希腊化时代”。 在此期间，意大利和印度之间的所

paragraphs.en: between Italy and India were in contact with each other and traded with each other and learned from each other. This mixing of ideas and cultures generated a great deal of learning, and culture, and wealth. So please join me next time, as we learn about the HellenisticAge. Thank you very much! I hope this lesson made you want to learn more aboutAlexander the Great. This time I don't have any new sources to share with you. I just want to remind you of some great history resources that I have shared in the past. First, I recommend the Primary source for most of our knowledge of Alexander. The book "The Campaigns ofAlexander" by
paragraphs.zh: 有人都相互来往，相互贸易，互相 交流学习。 这种思想和文化的融 合产生了大量的知识，文化和财 富。 所以请加入我的下一节课程，我们 会学习更多希腊化时代的知识。十 分感谢！ 希望通过这节课，你想要更多地了 解亚历山大大帝。 这次我没有新的资源分享。我只想 提醒你我之前分享过的一些历史 资源。 首先是我曾推荐的阿里安的《亚历 山大的战役》一书，这是关于亚历 山大的大部分知识的主要来源。

paragraphs.en: Arrian. I also recommend the Documentary "In the Footsteps ofAlexander" produced by the BBC and hosted by the historian Michael Wood. And I recommend the podcast "Hardcore History" which has several episodes onAlexander the Great.
paragraphs.zh: 我还推荐过由英国广播公司制作 并由历史学家迈克尔·伍德主持的 纪录片《亚历山大的足迹》。 我还推荐过播客“硬核历史”，其 中有几集是关于亚历山大大帝的。

titleZh: 第十三讲：希腊化时代|图书馆里的天才，领先时代 1000 年

paragraphs.en: Hello and welcome back to our lecture series on World History. Today we will be learning about the world thatAlexander the Great created: The Hellenistic World. The Hellenistic Era lasted from the death ofAlexander in 323 BC, until the fall of the last Hellenistic Kingdom in 30 BC, so it was a period of about 300 years. This is a special time in history, becauseAlexander's conquest allowed a mixing of ideas and cultures across all the lands between Greece and India. We can see this most clearly in Greek cultural influence on India. Here is a Greek column with a sphinx on the top. Here is an Indian
paragraphs.zh: 1000 年 大家好，欢迎回到世界历史系列课 程。今天我们要学习的是亚历山大 大帝创立的天下：希腊化时代。 希腊化时代是从公元前323年亚历 山大大帝的死亡开始的，一直到公 元前 30 年最后一个希腊王国灭亡 而结束。因此，这是一段长约 300 年的历史。 这是历史上的一个特殊时期，亚历 山大的胜利使得在希腊和印度之 间所有土地上的思想和文化出现 了大融合。在希腊文化对印度的影 响中，我们可以清楚地看到这一 点。 这是一个希腊圆柱，上面有一个狮 身人面像。这是几十年后竖起的印

paragraphs.en: column erected a few decades later. Here are the ruins of an Indian temple with Greek-style columns. And in this slide, on the left we have a statue of the Greek hero Hercules strangling the Nemean Lion, except this statue was carved in Mathura India! The picture on the right is an Indian statue of the Buddha carved in the Greek-style. The Hellenistic era also begins a big change in Greek statues. I suspect this might be from Indian cultural influence, but that's harder to prove. Art historians divide Greek sculpture into three periods: Archaic, Classical and Hellenistic. Here are examples of each. Notice how the Hellenistic statues are
paragraphs.zh: 度圆柱。 这里是印度寺庙的废墟，里面有希 腊风格的圆柱。 左侧，是有一个希腊英雄大力神赫 拉克勒斯的雕像，他勒死了尼米亚 猛狮，而这尊雕像是在印度的马图 拉雕刻的！ 右边的图片是一个印度雕像，上面 雕刻着希腊风格的佛像。 在希腊化时代，希腊雕像也开始有 了重大改变。我猜测这与印度文化 的影响有关，但这一点很难证实。 艺术历史学家将希腊雕塑分为三 个时期：古风时期、希腊古典时期、 希腊化时期。每种风格我都找了一 个图例。请注意看希腊的雕像是多 么的栩栩如生，复杂，有曲线。

paragraphs.en: much more lifelike, complicated, and curvy. One of the most famous Greek statues, the Nike of Samothrace was made at this time. Meanwhile, at the Greek island of Rhodes, a giant bronze statue of the sun god Helios was erected next to the harbor in 280 BC. This statue was called the Colossus of Rhodes, and it stood for 54 years until an earthquake knocked it down. This period also gives us another marvelous artifact. It is the Seikilos Epitaph. You can see it here in this picture on the left. It is a tombstone in the shape of a marble column, only about a meter tall. It was erected by a man named
paragraphs.zh: 希腊最著名的雕像之一——萨莫 色雷斯的胜利女神就是在这个时 期筑成的。 期间，一座巨大的太阳神赫利俄斯 的青铜雕像于公元前280年在希腊 的罗德斯岛港口树立了起来。 这座雕像被称为“罗德斯巨型雕塑 像”，它在这里屹立了 54 年，直到 一场地震击倒了它。 这个时期还给我们留下了另一件 不可思议的工艺品：塞基洛斯的墓 志铭。你可以在左边这张图上看 到。 这是一块大理石柱子的墓碑，只有 一米高。是一个名为塞基洛斯的男 子为了纪念他妻子欧忒耳珀而建

paragraphs.en: Seikilos in loving memory of his wife Euterpe. It is special because the epitaph is the words to a short song, and with it is the musical notation. This is the oldest song in the world which we can perform today. The 4 verses of the song go like this: While you live, shine, have no grief at all; life exists only for a short while, and time demands its toll." Okay, back to politics. As we learned last time,Alexander the Great died at the age of 32. Afterwards, his generals fought each other and the empire split into 4 kingdoms: The Kingdom of Ptolemy, Cassander, Lysimachus, and Seleuc us.
paragraphs.zh: 的。 这块墓碑非常特特殊，因为墓志铭 的内容是一首歌的歌词和乐谱。这 是世界上最古老的歌曲，我们今天 可以演奏。这首歌的前四行是这样 的： 你在人世时， 光芒四射，无忧无愁。 然生命短暂如斯， 天不假年，有命无寿。 好了，让我们回到政治层面。 我们上节课说到，亚历山大大帝在 32 岁时去世了。后来，他的将军们 互相争斗，帝国分裂成四个王国： 托勒密王国、卡山德、雷西马克和 塞琉古。

paragraphs.en: Each of these Kingdom had different fates. Let's take a look at them in more detail. First was the territory won by Alexander's general Seleucus I Nicator, historians call it the Seleucid Empire after him. The Seleucids were a Greek elite who ruled the main part of the old Persian Empire. There were 31 Seleucid monarchs in the dynasty, which lasted for 10 generations. They often fought wars with the Indian Kings to their East, or the other Greeks Kings to their West. Unlike theAchaemenid Persians, the Seleucids were much less tolerant of the different customs and religions in their empire.
paragraphs.zh: 每一个王国都有不同的命运。让我 们来仔细地看一看。 首先是亚历山大大帝的将军之一： 塞琉古一世打下的版图，历史上称 为塞琉古帝国。 塞琉古是希腊王国的中流砥柱，它 统治了古波斯帝国的核心区域。 这个王朝有过 31 任塞琉古君主， 延续了 10 代人。他们经常与其东 部的印第安国王或是西部的希腊 国王打仗。 与阿契美尼德的波斯人不同，塞琉 古对帝国中存在的不同风俗和宗 教的容忍程度要低得多。

paragraphs.en: In 167 BC, their oppressive policies caused a Jewish revolt in Canaan. The rebels were led by a leader named Judas Maccabeus. They drove the Greeks out of Canaan and re-established the Kingdom of Israel. This newly Independent Israel survived for another 130 years before being conquered by the Romans and made into a vassal state. Today, this successful rebellion against the Greeks is celebrated by Jews all across the world in the holiday of Hanukkah. But losing Israel was only the beginning of Seleucid troubles. Twenty years later, in 141 BC, a Persian rebellion overthrew them in centralAsia.
paragraphs.zh: 在公元前 167 年，他们的压迫政策 导致了迦南的犹太人起义。 叛军由一个名叫犹大·马加比的人 领导。他们把希腊人赶出了迦南， 重新建立了以色列王国。 这个新独立的以色列王国在罗马 人入侵、成为附属国之前，幸存了 130 年。 今天，全世界的犹太人都在光明节 庆祝这场反对希腊人的胜利。 但是失去以色列仅仅是塞琉古的 麻烦的开始。20 年后，在公元前 141 年，波斯起义推翻了他们在中 亚的统治。

paragraphs.en: These Persian rebels were led by a man named Mithridates. He established the Parthian dynasty and re-instituted native Persian control of centralAsia. The Seleucids hung on for another 80 years in parts ofAnatolia, but they never again controlled the Persian heartland. The Western Hellenic Kingdoms survived a little longer, but all of them were eventually conquered by the Romans between 197 and 31 BC. I think the most interesting of these kingdoms was the Kingdom of Ptolemy in Egypt. For 11 generations the descendants of Ptolemy were the pharaohs of Egypt.
paragraphs.zh: 这些波斯叛军由一个叫米特里达 特的人领导。他建立了帕提亚王 朝，重新确立了波斯对中亚的控 制。 塞琉古王朝在安纳托利亚部分地 区的统治又持续了 80 年，但他们 再也没能统治波斯的核心区域。 西方的希腊王国幸存了一段时间， 但在公元前 197-31 年间，希腊王 国全境最终都被罗马人征服了。 我认为这些王国中最有意思的是 埃及托勒密王朝。 托勒密的后人接连 11 代都成为了 埃及的法老。

paragraphs.en: This Greek dynasty adopted the royal Egyptian custom of brothers marrying sisters, which is a little gross, but it did ensure that power stayed in the family! All of the Kings were named Ptolemy in honor of their ancestor, and all of Queens were named Cleopatra. Two of these Kings and Queens are interesting for other reasons. Ptolemy V ordered the creation of the Rosetta stone, which was vital for historians learning how to read ancient hieroglyphics 2000 years later. And Cleopatra the VII was the very last Ptolemaic monarch, and a lover of Julius Caesar. But I think the most interesting
paragraphs.zh: 这个希腊王朝采用了埃及皇室的 习俗，兄弟姐妹结婚，这有点恶心， 但它确实确保了家族的权力。 所有的国王都叫托勒密，以纪念他 们的祖先，所有的女王都叫克利欧 佩特拉。 其中两位国王和女王因为一些别 的原因引起了我的注意。 托勒密五世下令建筑罗塞达石，这 对于历史学家在 2000 年后学习如 何阅读古代象形文字起着至关重 要的作用。 克利欧佩特拉是托勒密王朝最后 一任君主，也是凯撒大帝的情人。 但我认为托勒密最有趣的一点在

paragraphs.en: thing that Ptolemy did was to found the Great Library ofAlexandria. According to legend, while he was in Nineveh,Alexander had admired the Library ofAshurbanipal. He had decided that he would build his own Great Library after the war was over. Alexander died before he could carry out this dream, but Ptolemy decided to make it happen once he became King of Egypt. Because the Nile produced so much grain, Egypt became the breadbasket of the Mediterranean. Ships came from all across the world to buy Egyptian grain. So Alexandria grew into a huge port and the largest city in the Mediterranean. In fact, the port was so busy, that
paragraphs.zh: 于，他建立了亚历山大图书馆。 据传说，当他在尼尼微的时候，亚 历山大很欣赏亚述巴尼帕的图书 馆。他已经决定，在战争结束后， 他将建造自己的伟大图书馆。 亚历山大在实现这一梦想之前就 去世了，但托勒密决定在他成为埃 及国王后实现这一梦想。 尼罗河生产出了大量的粮食，埃及 成为了地中海的粮仓。世界各地的 国家都派船前来购买粮食。因此， 亚历山大成为一个巨大的港口和 地中海最大的城市。 港口过于繁忙，于是托勒密下令建

paragraphs.en: Ptolemy ordered the construction of a great tower, and on the top of that tower was a fire that burned day and night. Because it was so high up, this fire could be seen by ships that were miles out to sea. This was the Great Lighthouse ofAlexandria. Ptolemy created a law for any merchant who wanted to trade in Alexandria. When you came into the port, the police would search your ship in order to collect taxes. But they would also look for any books that you might have on-board. If they found any, they would take that book to the Library. At the library scribes would make a copy and then return the book to the merchant. In this wayAlexandria developed
paragraphs.zh: 造一座巨塔，塔顶日夜燃烧着大 火。 塔建得非常高，这火可以被几英里 外的船只看到。这是亚历山大灯 塔。 托勒密针对所有想在亚历山大经 商的商人制定了一条法律。只要你 想进港，就会有警察搜查船只，以 便征缴税款。 同时，他们还会寻找你可能拥有的 书籍。如果他们找到了，就会把那 本书带到图书馆去。抄写员会抄一 份，然后把书还给商人。 通过这种方式，亚历山大有了世界

paragraphs.en: the largest library in the world with over a million books. So, scholars came toAlexandria from all across the world in order to use those books in their research, this made the Library ofAlexandria the world's top research institution. The Great Library ofAlexandria survived for at least 700 years.And during that time, some remarkable scholars passed through its doors. These men and women came up with ideas that were 1000 years ahead of their time. Let's take a look at some of them. Aristarchus of Samos correctly believed that the Earth orbited the Sun. He was the first person in history to propose this idea, called the Heliocentric theory. He also figured out the correct order
paragraphs.zh: 上最大的图书馆，拥有一百多万册 图书。 于是，学者们从世界各地来到亚历 山大，利用图书馆内的书籍进行研 究，这使得亚历山大图书馆成为了 世界顶尖的研究机构。 亚历山大图书馆至少存在了 700 年。在这段时间里，一些杰出学者 经历了在图书馆的研究，萌生了超 越时代千年的想法。让我们对其中 几位稍作了解。 萨摩斯的阿利斯塔克斯正确地相 信地球绕太阳运行。他是历史上第 一个提出日心说这个想法的人。 他还算出了行星的正确顺序。他还

paragraphs.en: of the planets.AND he theorized that the stars were just other suns that are very far away. Another one of these brilliant scholars was a man named Euclid. We are not sure if Euclid was just one genius, or a group of mathematicians, but his book, The Elements, became the world's first math textbook. There was Archimedes. He figured out a how to measure the volume of an irregular shape by placing it in water. He is also credited with inventing theArchimedes screw, and made many contributions to mathematics and physics. There was Hero ofAlexandria, the father of mechanics.
paragraphs.zh: 推测出，其实天空中的繁星只是遥 远的恒星。 另一位杰出的学者是欧几里得。 我们尚不能确定欧几里得是一个 天才，还是一群数学家。但他的著 作《几何原本》成为了世界上第一 本数学教科书。 还有阿基米德。他想出了测量不规 则物体体积的方法：将其放入水 中。 人们还认为是他发明了阿基米德 式螺旋抽水机，对数学和物理做出 了许多贡献。 这是亚历山大港的希罗，力学之 父。

paragraphs.en: He developed theAeolipile, the world's first steam engine! Unfortunately, he was too far ahead of his time, and hisAeolipile was just a curiosity rather than a machine that could do work. If only somebody had built on his idea! These thinkers might have started the Industrial Revolution in 50AD! We humans would now be 1500 years more advanced than we are today! Then there was Hypatia. She lived a few centuries after these other scholars, in the last decades of the Library's existence, but she did interesting work in geometry and astronomy, and is the earliest female mathematician in the historical record.
paragraphs.zh: 他发明了汽转球，是世界上第一台 蒸汽机！ 不幸的是，他的发明太超前了，因 此他的汽转球在当时只被当做一 种珍玩，而不能作为机器投入生 产。 要是有人能将他的理论投入生产 就好了！这些思想家可能把工业革 命提前到公元前 50 年！ 我们人类就会比现在先进1500年！ 接下来是希帕蒂娅。她生活在这些 思想家之后的几个世纪，即图书馆 存在的最后几十年，但她在几何学 和天文学方面做了有趣的工作，是 历史记录中最早的女数学家。

paragraphs.en: But my favorite Hellenistic thinker, was Eratosthenes. He was born in 276 BC and died 194 BC. He was a mathematician, geographer, poet, astronomer, and music theorist.And during his life, he was also the chief librarian at Alexandria. One day Eratosthenes was reading a report in the library which said that far to the south, at the border town of Syene, at noon on the summer solstice, you can see the reflection of the sun at the bottom of a deep well and sticks in the ground cast no shadows. The sun is directly over-head at that time. Eratosthenes wondered about this. Do sticks inAlexandriaALSO cast
paragraphs.zh: 但我最喜欢的希腊思想家，是埃拉 托色尼。 他生于公元前 276 年，死于公元前 194 年。他是一位数学家、地理学 家、诗人、天文学家和音乐理论家。 他曾是亚历山大的首席图书管理 员。 有一天，他正在图书管理读一份报 告。报告中写道，在遥远的南部， 赛尼城的边境，每当夏至正午，你 可以看到太阳在深井底部的倒影， 而地面上的枝条没有影子。 当时，正是正午。 埃拉托色尼对此很好奇。在亚历山 大夏至的中午，树枝也没有影子

paragraphs.en: no shadows at noon on the Summer solstice? So he did an experiment and he found out that they do cast a small shadow. If you make a triangle out of the stick and the shadow, inAlexandria the angle opposite to the shadow has a measure of 7.2˚ degrees. While that same angle in Syene would be 0˚ degrees because there is no shadow. Eratosthenes knew that he had just gathered some very valuable information. Now he wanted to know how far apart Syene andAlexandria were. So he hired a man to measure the distance.
paragraphs.zh: 吗？ 于是他做了一个实验，他发现确实 有小阴影。 如果你在亚历山大将枝条搭成一 个三角形，它和阴影之间的角度是 而在赛尼城，这个角度是 0°，因 为没有任何阴影。 埃拉托色尼意识到自己收集了一 些非常有价值的信息。 现在他想知道赛尼城和亚历山大 相隔多远。 于是他雇了一个人来测量距离。

paragraphs.en: The man returned and told Eratosthenes that there were 5000 stadia between the two places. 5000 stadia is equal to 800 kilometers. Eratosthenes understood that the distance from Syene toAlexandria was the the same as 7.2˚ degrees around the earth. Since the earth is a circle, and a circle has 360˚ degree Eratosthenes saw that he could set up a ratio. This distance fromAlexandria to Syene divided by the Distance around the Earth is the same as to 7.2˚ degrees divided by 360˚ degrees. The ratio of 7.2 divided by 360 simplifies to 1/50th. And then Eratosthenes plugged in
paragraphs.zh: 这个人回来告诉埃拉托色尼，这两 处相隔 5000 视距。 5000 视距相当于 800 千米。 埃拉托色尼发现，塞尼到亚历山大 的距离相当于圆心角为 7.2°的地 球周长。 将地球看做一个 360°的圆，埃拉 托色尼就可以建立一个比率。 塞尼到亚历山大的距离÷地球周 7.2 除以 360 的比值可以简化为 随后埃拉托色尼带入了塞尼到亚

paragraphs.en: the distance fromAlexandria to Syene, which is 800 kilometers. Finally, Eratosthenes solved the equation and concluded that the Earth was 40,000 kilometers around. That's the right answer! The actual size is 40,075 Kilometers! Eratosthenes estimate was 99% accurate! Pretty good work for 2200 years ago! So you can see that the Hellenistic era was a rich and sophisticated period in history. In fact, some Greeks even had enough wealth to travel for fun. They were histories first tourists!
paragraphs.zh: 历山大的距离，也就是 800 千米。 最后他解开了这个等式，发现地球 周长为 40,000 千米。 这就是正确答案！ 地球的实际周长是 40,075 千米！ 埃拉托色尼估算的准确率达到了 对于 2200 年前的技术而言，这是 相当了不起的成就了！ 所以你可以看到，希腊化时代是人 类历史上一个丰富而复杂的时期。 事实上，一些希腊人甚至有足够的 财富去旅游。他们是历史上的第一 批游客！

paragraphs.en: And people wrote guidebooks for these ancient travelers. One of these writers named Philo of Byzantium recommended that people go see the Seven Wonders of the World. These seven sites were: The Great Pyramid of Giza, the Hanging Gardens of Babylon, the Temple of Artemis at Ephesus, the Statue of Zeus at Olympia, the Mausoleum at Halicarnassus, the Colossus of Rhodes, and the Great Lighthouse ofAlexandria. Because ofAlexander the Great, these were all now part of the Greek world! Okay everyone, I hope you enjoyed this look at the Hellenistic age. Next class, we shall leave the Greek world behind for a lesson and explore India.
paragraphs.zh: 人们为这些古代旅行者写旅游指 南。其中一位作家，名叫拜占庭的 斐洛，他建议人们去看世界七大奇 观。 这七个地点分别是：吉萨大金字 塔、巴比伦的空中花园、以弗所的 阿耳特弥斯庙、奥林匹亚宙斯神 像、哈利卡索斯的陵墓、罗德斯岛 的巨像和亚历山大灯塔。 因为亚历山大大帝，这些都成为了 希腊世界的一部分！ 好了，希望大家喜欢希腊化时代的 这一课。 下一课，我们将离开希腊，转而探 索印度。

paragraphs.en: Thank you very much! Alright,guys!I hope you guys enjoyed that lesson and want to learn more.If you do,I have some recommendations. First of all,the Youtube channel Extra-Credit has a great series on Euclid and his ideas. The 1980 television series Cosmos,hosted by Carl Sagan has a great lesson on Eratosthenes in the first episode. And this video on TedED is an interesting little lesson on Archimedes. Thanks very much,see you nest lesson!
paragraphs.zh: 谢谢大家！ 希望大家喜欢这节课，如果想要了 解更多，我可以推荐一些资源。 道出品了关于欧几里及其理念 的伟大系列。 1980 年的电视连续剧《宇宙》的第 一集中提到了埃拉托色尼，由卡 尔·萨根主持。 这是TED上一节关于阿基米德的有 趣的小课。 非常感谢，下节课再见！

paragraphs.en: Lecture Hello, and welcome back to our lecture series on World History. Today we are going to step beyond the Greek-speaking world and see what was happening in India for the past 2000 years. We will be discussing the time period between 1900 BC and 100 CE. This is what that looks like on our timeline. Last semester I taught you about the Indus Valley Civilization, which flourished from about 3300 to 1900 BC. But then the Indus Valley Civilization experienced a decline around 1900 BC. The causes of this decline are still unknown. Then a few hundred years after this
paragraphs.zh: 大家好，欢迎 回到世界历史系列 课程。今天我们将跨过希腊世界， 去看看过去的 2000 年间印度发生 了什么。 我们将讨论公元前 1900 年到公元 100 年之间的历史。这是时间线上 的样子。 上一期，我讲过关于印度河流域文 明的知识，它在公元前 3300 年到 1900 年间蓬勃发展。 但在公元前 1900 年左右，印度河 流域文明经历了一次衰落。造成这 种衰落的原因尚不清楚。 在印度衰落几百年后，一个新的民

paragraphs.en: decline, a new people began to enter India, these are a people we have seen many times before in our historical journey: The Indo-Europeans. After the Indo-Europeans arrived, Indian history enters a new Era that historians call the Vedic Period. This period lasted from 1500 to 600 BC. The sub-group of Indo Europeans who arrived in India are known as the Indo-Aryans. This map shows us the different groups of Indo-Europeans. The group in blue are the Indo-Aryans. The IndoAryans brought with them their own religious beliefs, which mixed together with indigenous Indian beliefs and formed the religion of Hinduism.
paragraphs.zh: 族开始进入印度，这是我们在历史 旅程中见过很多次的民族:印欧 人。 随着印欧人的到来，印度历史进入 了一个被历史学家称为吠陀时期 的新时代，即公元前 1500 至公元 前 600 年。 到达印度的印欧人亚群是印度雅 利安人。 这张地图向我们展示了印欧人的 不同群体。蓝色部分即为印度雅利 安人。 印度雅利安人带来了他们自己的 宗教信仰，这些信仰与当地的印度 信仰混合在一起，形成了印度教。

paragraphs.en: We call this period in Indian History the "Vedic Period" because the Indo-Aryans brought with them a religious literature and a set of hymns, stories, and other traditions. These collections of religious texts are called "the Vedas". There are four collections of Vedas: The Rigveda, The Yajurveda, the Samveda, and theAtharvaveda. Schools, and elaborate systems developed for preserving, memorizing and passing on these texts during the Vedic period. All of these stories were expressed in the language of the Indo-Aryans which was "Sanskrit." Sanskrit it the mother language for all of the languages of
paragraphs.zh: 我们把印度历史上的这段时期称 为“吠陀时期”，因为印度雅利安 人带来了一种宗教文学和一系列 赞美诗、故事和其他传统。 这些宗教文献被称为《吠陀经》。 吠陀经有四集:  《梨俱吠陀》、《夜柔吠陀》、《娑摩 吠陀》和《阿闼婆吠陀》。 在吠陀时期，为了保存、记忆和传 承这些经文而建立了学校和复杂 系统。 所有这些故事都通过一种印度雅 利安的语言来传播：梵文。 梵语是印度北部所有语言的母语。

paragraphs.en: Northern India. This is similar to how Latin is the mother language for French, Spanish, Italian, Portuguese and Romanian. At first, all of this literature was preserved in the oral tradition. But sometime around the 1st millennium BC, the idea of the alphabet reached India from the Phoenicians. And now the Indians had a way to begin writing down their religious tradition. Like the Greeks, the Indians also developed their own epic poems. The two great Epics are called the Ramayana and the Mahabharata. The Mahābhārata tells the story of a war between two sets of princes:
paragraphs.zh: 好比拉丁语是法语、西班牙语、意 大利语、葡萄牙语和罗马尼亚语的 母语。 起初，所有这些文学作品都口口相 传。但是大约在公元前 1000 年左 右，字母表从腓尼基传到了印度。 现在印度人有办法来记录他们的 宗教传统了。 和希腊人一样，印度人也发展了自 己的史诗。 这两部伟大的史诗被称为《罗摩衍 那》和《摩诃婆罗多》。 《摩诃婆罗多》讲述了持国百子和 般度五子的战争。

paragraphs.en: the 100 Kauravas and five Pandavas. The 3rd Pandava brother is named Arjuna, and within the Mahābhārata he has a long conversation with his chariot driver named Lord Krishna. This conversation is another long poem that discusses deep philosophical ideas. It is called the Bhagavad Gita. The Ramayana is another great epic poem. This one tells the story of the divine prince Rama and his struggle to rescue his wife Sita from the demon king Ravana. Then the second part of the story has to do with Rama's troubles trusting Sita after he rescues her. Of course the story also is used to
paragraphs.zh: 般度第三子名为阿朱那，在《摩诃 婆罗多》中，他与名为克利须那神 的战车夫有一段很长的对话。 这段对话是另一首长诗：《博伽梵 歌》，讨论了深刻的哲学话题。 《罗摩传》也是一部伟大史诗。这 个故事讲述了神圣的罗摩王子和 他从恶魔国王拉瓦那手中拯救妻 子西塔的故事。 《罗摩传》的第二部分讲述了罗魔 王在拯救西塔之后遭遇的信任危 机。 当然，这个故事也用于讨论深刻的

paragraphs.en: discuss deep philosophical ideas. From 600 - 200 BC, India underwent a second period of urbanization as cities began being built all across the sub-continent. Eventually these cities grew in strength and Kingdoms began to develop. There were 16 of these Kingdoms which are called "Mahajanapadas", which mean "the great foothold" of a tribe. During this period of the 16 Mahajanapadas and Second Urbanization, a wealthy prince was born named Siddhartha. He was the son of a King or Chief named Śuddhodana. Uddhodana wanted his son to have a life free from human suffering and
paragraphs.zh: 哲学理念。 公元前 600-200 年，随着城市开始 在整个次大陆上建立起来，印度进 入了城市化的第二个阶段。 最终，这些城市越来越强大，逐渐 发展成了王国。 其中有 16 个王国被称为“十六大 国”，意为一个部落的“大据点”。 十六大国和第二次城市化阶段，一 个富有的王子出生了，名为悉达 多。他是净饭王的儿子。 净饭王希望儿子能够过上远离人 世痛苦与宗教束缚的生活，以便成

paragraphs.en: religious teachings, since he thought that would make his son a better King. So Siddhartha lived a life of pure pleasure until he was 29 years old. He even got married and had a son. But when Siddhartha was 29, he left the palace for the first time, because he wanted to see the outside world. He snuck out with his charioteer Channa. During his time outside Siddhartha saw an old man, a sick man, a corpse, and a wandering ascetic. Since these were his first experiences with human suffering, Siddhartha was shocked by what he saw. But he was already a mature adult, so he was able to think critically
paragraphs.zh: 为一个更优秀的国王。 于是，在悉达多 29 岁之前，他一 直过着快乐的生活。他甚至还结了 婚，有了一个儿子。 但当悉达多 29 岁时，他第一次离 开了王宫。因为他想看看外面的世 界。 他和车夫阐陀一起溜了出去。 他外出时，看见一个老人，一个病 人，一具尸体，一个流浪的苦行者。 由于这是他初次见到人间苦难，他 被其所见震惊了。 但他毕竟是一个成年人了，他能够 批判性地思考人类的苦难，而不是

paragraphs.en: about human suffering, without just accepting it as part of life the way children do. Siddhartha decided that he had to travel the world and confront this problem of human suffering. So he snuck out of the palace again with his charioteer Channa. Then he cut his hair, put on the robes of a beggar, and sent Channa home. He would dedicate himself to the spiritual questions of life. So Siddhartha became a wandering ascetic and went to the town of Rājagaha, the capital of Magadah. At Rājagaha Siddhartha met at teacher named Alara Kalama and studied meditation under him. Siddhartha did Yogic exercises, he meditated, he studied, and he fasted
paragraphs.zh: 像孩子那样，接受苦难为生活的一 部分。 悉达多决定环游世界，经历人间疾 苦。于是他再次和车夫阐陀一起溜 了出去。 然后他剪了头发，穿上乞丐的长 袍，把阐陀送回了家。他决定献身 于对生命的思考。 于是悉达多成了一个流浪的苦行 僧，来到了摩揭陀国的首都王舍 城。 在王舍城，悉达多遇到了一位名叫 阿罗逻伽蓝的老师，并在他的指导 下学习冥想。 悉达多练习瑜伽，冥想，学习，并 且长时间禁食。

paragraphs.en: for very long periods. At one point he is said to have become so skinny from fasting that he could touch his spine by pressing on his stomach. Siddhartha lived this way for 6 years and even gained several students of his own. But he discovered that this sort of extreme deprivation brought only pain, not enlightenment. Siddhartha became so weak from fasting that he lost most of his strength. But then a village girl named Sujata saw him and mistook him for a tree spirit which she thought had granted her wish of a child. In gratitude, she brought Siddhartha a bowl with milk and rice.
paragraphs.zh: 据说，他曾经由于禁食变得异常消 瘦，按压胃部就能触碰到他的脊 椎。 悉达多过了 6 年这样的生活，并且 收了几个学生。 但他发现，这种极端的剥夺只带来 了痛苦，而非启蒙。 由于禁食，悉达多失去能量，变得 极度虚弱。但一位名叫苏耶妲的乡 村女孩遇到了他，将其误认为为她 带来子嗣的树神。苏耶妲怀着感激 之情带来了一碗牛奶和米饭。

paragraphs.en: So Siddhartha decided to start eating again. After seeing him break his fast, Siddhartha's students asked him what he was doing. He admitted that he didn't really have the answers to the problems of life. So his five students left him. Siddhartha decided that he would go sit under a pipal tree and meditate until he figured it all out. If you are curious, this is the kind of tree that Siddharta sat under. Siddhartha sat there under that tree and meditated for 49 days and nights. According to tradition, he was attacked by demons who tried to distract him and prevent him from achieving enlightenment.
paragraphs.zh: 于是悉达多决定重新开始进食。 看到悉达多开戒，学生们质问他在 做什么。悉达多承认他并不明白生 命的答案。 于是他的五个学生离开了他。 悉达多决定坐在菩提树下冥想，直 到他把一切搞明白。 悉达多就是坐在这种树下冥想的。 悉达多坐在菩提树下，冥想了 49 个日夜。 根据传说，他受到了恶魔的攻击， 他们试图分散他的注意力，阻止他 获得启迪。

paragraphs.en: But then, after 49 days of meditation, he had it. At this point, Siddhartha became known as the "Buddha" which means "enlightened one". He explained what he had learned as the 4 Noble Truths: 1) Life is filled with suffering 2) Suffering comes from unsatisfied desires 3) To end suffering you must give up on these desires. 4) You can make progress by following the eight-fold path. The eightfold path consists of doing these things: Right view,Right intention,Right speech,Right action,Right
paragraphs.zh: 但是，经过了 49 天的冥想，悉达 多开悟了。 此时，悉达多成为了人们熟知的 “佛陀（释迦摩尼）“，意味“醒 悟的人”。 他解释了他学到的四圣谛： 1）生活充满了痛苦 2）痛苦来自于不满足的欲望 3）为了结束痛苦，你必须放弃这 些欲望。 4）你可以通过八正道来取得进步。 八正道包括： 正见、正思维、正语、正业、正命、 正精进、正念、正定

paragraphs.en: livelihood,Right effort,Right mindfulness,Right concentration Then the Buddha went back to share what he had learned with his friends, students and teachers. First he met Trapusa and Bahalika two merchant brothers on a business trip. They offered him his first meal after his meditation, and were persuaded by his ideas to become his first students. Then Buddha tried to find his former teachers to share his discoveries, but they had already died. Then he found his five former students and delivered his first sermon to them. Together they formed the first sangha: the community of Buddhist
paragraphs.zh: 然后，佛陀回去与他的朋友、学生 和老师分享他学到的东西。 首先，他在一次商务旅行中遇到了 特拉帕萨和巴利卡两个商人兄弟。 在他冥想之后，他们给了他第一顿 饭，并被他的想法说服，成为他的 第一批学生。 然后，佛陀试图找到他以前的老师 来分享他的发现，但他们已经死 了。 然后，他找到了他的五个以前的学 生，并向他们做了第一次布道。 他们一起组成了第一个僧伽：佛教 僧侣的群体。

paragraphs.en: monks. Together these monks travelled around India teaching, attracting students, and developing Buddhist communities. This ministry of the Buddha lasted for 45 years.And Buddhist tradition tells many stories of the things he said and did in that time. And then, in 483 BC, at the age of 80, he died. His final words were these: "All things decay, strive onwards with vigilance." After the Buddha died, his ideas began to gradually spread across Asia. They moved outwards from the Buddhist heartland of the Ganges River Valley and then spread across India. Then they moved into CentralAsia,
paragraphs.zh: 这些僧人一起在印度各地传教，吸 引学生，发展佛教团体。 传教持续了 45 年。佛教的传统讲 述了他在那段时间里所讲和所做 的事情。 公元前 483 年，佛陀在 80 岁时圆 寂。他的遗言是：“万物腐朽，自 强不息。” 佛陀圆寂后，他的思想开始逐渐传 遍亚洲，从恒河流域的佛教中心地 带向外迁移，然后传播到印度各 地。 随后传入中亚，从那里，这种宗教

paragraphs.en: and from there the religion trickled into western China. The faith also spread into Sri Lanka and from there into South-EastAsia. And then it gradually spread into China, and from there it moved into Korea, Japan and Vietnam. In the decades after the Buddha's death, a great Kingdom developed in India called the Nanda Empire. The Nanda Empire was located in the eastern part of India because the Western Part was dominated by the Persian Empire. This was the biggest power in India whenAlexander the Great reached the Indus river valley. Alexander turned around and left India, but just five years later, a Great Indian King rose in power.
paragraphs.zh: 慢慢传入了中国西部。 这种信仰也传播到了斯里兰卡，又 从斯里兰卡进入了东南亚。 然后它逐渐传播到中国，从那之后 又进入了韩国，日本和越南。 在佛陀圆寂后的几十年里，印度发 展了一个伟大的王国，称为难陀王 朝。 难陀王朝位于印度东部，而西部则 由波斯帝国统治。 当亚历山大大帝到达印度河流域 时，这是印度最大的强国。 亚历山大转身离开了印度，但就在 五年后，一位伟大的印度国王上 台。他的名字叫旃陀罗笈多，他只

paragraphs.en: His named was Chandragupta Maurya, and he was only 16 years younger thanAlexander the Great. Chandragupta Maurya overthrew the Nanda dynasty and fought wars withAlexander's successor Seleucus. Eventually Chandragupta Maurya and Seleucus arranged a marriage alliance and Chandragupta married a Greek princess. Chandragupta's conquests were continued by his son Bindusara and his grandsonAshoka. Ashoka was a particularly effective general, and he pushed the limits of his Kingdom to their greatest extent. By the time he was done with his conquering,Ashoka had created the Largest Empire in Indian history:
paragraphs.zh: 比亚历山大大帝小 16 岁。 旃陀罗笈多推翻了难陀王朝，与亚 历山大的继任者塞琉古斯交战。 最后，旃陀罗笈多与塞琉古斯安排 了一场联姻，旃陀罗笈多娶了一位 希腊公主。 旃陀罗笈多的儿孙频头娑罗王和 阿育王继续了他的征程。 阿育王是一位特别有影响力的将 军，他将自己王国的疆域扩张到了 极致。 在他征服印度的时候，阿育王已经 建立了印度历史上最大的帝国:孔 雀王朝。

paragraphs.en: the Mauryan Empire. But this empire would only survive for 50 years after Ashoka's death and broke apart in 180 BC. OnceAshoka had built his empire he renounced his Hindu beliefs and became a Buddhist. He then made Buddhism the official religion of his empire. According to tradition, it was a Buddhist monk named Nigrodha who converted him. Ashoka then built thousands of stupas around his empire. These are structures that contain the remains of a great Buddhist teacher and can also be places of meditation and prayer. Ashoka built the Great Stupa at Sanchi, which is one of the oldest
paragraphs.zh: 但这个帝国在阿育王死后仅存活 了 50 年，并于公元前 180 年分裂。 阿育王建立了自己的帝国后，他放 弃了自己的印度教信仰，成为了一 名佛教徒。然后他把佛教作为他帝 国的官方宗教。 按照传统，是一位名叫尼格鲁达的 和尚使他皈依。 然后阿育王在他的帝国周围建造 了数千座佛塔。这些建筑既可用来 存放高僧的舍利，也可以是冥想和 祈祷的场所。 阿育王在桑吉建造了大佛塔，这是 印度最古老的石制建筑之一。

paragraphs.en: stone structures in India. Ashoka also began to issue edicts inscribed on pillars which he put up all across the Kingdom in Buddhist Monasteries. These are called the Pillars of Ashoka, and they were usually topped with sculptures of lions. One of these lion sculptures at the town of Sarnath includes 4Asiatic Lions standing back-to-back. This lion sculpture is such an important artifact, that in 1950 the Indian Parliament decided to make it the National Emblem of India. You frequently see these lions on official documents in India. Often times they sit above the words "Satyameva Jayate" written in the Devanagari script.
paragraphs.zh: 阿育王还开始颁布雕刻在全国各 地的佛教寺院的柱子上的法令。 这些柱子被称为阿育王柱，顶部通 常装饰有狮子的雕塑。 其中，野鹿苑的狮子雕塑上有四只 背靠背的狮子。 狮子雕像是一件非常重要的文物， 印度议会在 1950 年决定把它作为 印度的国徽。 你可以经常在印度的官方文件上 看到这些狮子。很多时候，他们坐 在梵文文字“Satyameva Jayate” 上面。

paragraphs.en: This means "TruthAlone Triumphs". The inscriptions on these pillars usually contain various messages. But they are all intended to promote a peaceful country and law-abiding citizens. The Mauryan Empire also produced some great works of sculpture as you can see here. Okay, that concludes our lesson on India. This is just a quick look at a civilization with history and literature and art that go very, very deep. I hope you will now want to do some more reading on your own! Now it’s time for us to leave India behind and return to the
paragraphs.zh: 意思是“真理必胜。” 这些柱子上的铭文通常包含各种 信息。但它们都旨在促进一个和平 的国家和守法的公民。 如你所见，孔雀王朝也创造了一些 伟大的雕塑作品。 好了，关于印度的内容就到这里 了。 这是对一个有着极其深刻的历史、 文学和艺术之文明的快速一瞥。 希望现在你想自己了解更多！ 现在，是时候离开印度，回到地中 海了，因为接下来我们将学习罗马

paragraphs.en: Mediterranean, because next we are going to learn about the Founding of Rome! I hope you enjoyed that lesson on ancient India, and want to learn more. If you do, I have some resources to share with you. First I recommend the Cartoon History of the Universe, Book 2. The first part of this book covers the history of India, including the life of the Buddha. There are also the epic poems of the Ramayana and Mahabharata. These are classics of world literature that are worth reading. And then there is the wonderful cartoon Sita Sings the Blues by Nina Paley. It tells the story of the Ramayana
paragraphs.zh: 的建立! 我希望你们喜欢这堂关于古印度 的课，并且想学到更多。如果是这 样的话，我有一些资源可以和你分 享。 首先我推荐《宇宙漫画史》，第二 册。这本书的第一部分介绍了印度 的历史，包括佛陀的生平。 还有《罗摩衍那》和《摩诃婆罗多》 史诗。这些是世界文学的经典之 作，值得一读。 还有精彩的卡通《蓝调之歌》，作 者是尼娜·帕利。 它讲述了罗摩衍那的故事，包括了

paragraphs.en: with Nina's own thoughts and criticisms of the story included. Nina criticizes the story through a feminist perspective and then also mixes in 1930s jazz and blues songs. Okay everyone, I hope you enjoyed this lesson. Thank you very much!
paragraphs.zh: 尼娜自己的想法和对故事的批评。 尼娜从女权主义的角度对这个故 事进行了批判，并将其融入了 20 世纪 30 年代的爵士乐和蓝调歌曲 中。 好了，各位，希望你们喜欢这节课。 谢谢大家!

titleZh: 第十五讲：罗马的崛起|喝母狼乳汁长大的兄弟，为何自相残杀？

paragraphs.en: Hello everyone and welcome back to our lecture series on World History today we are going to learn about the Rise of ancient Rome. We will be focusing on the years between 753 and 264 BC. In the year 750 BC, the two most advanced cultures in the Italian Peninsula were the Etruscans in the north, and the Greek colonies in the south. Two hundred and fifty years later, both the Greeks and the Etruscans had increased their territory. On the eastern coast were less organized tribal groups called the Samnites and Umbrians, and to the north were the Gauls, whose culture covered much of Europe.
paragraphs.zh: 大家好，欢迎回到我们的世界历史 系列课程，今天我们来学习古罗马 的崛起。 让我们把目光转向公元前 753-264 年的这段时间。 公元前 750 年，意大利半岛上最为 发达的两个文化分别是北部的伊 特鲁里亚文化和南部的希腊文化。 250 年来，希腊人和伊特鲁里亚人 都扩大了他们的领地。 在东部海岸有一些组织较为松散 的部落，他们是撒姆尼人和翁布利 亚人，而在北部则是高卢人，他们 的文化覆盖了欧洲的大部分地区。

paragraphs.en: All of these people spoke Indo-European languages. As we learned in a previous lesson, the Greeks were in Italy because they and the Phoenicians were the main seafaring civilizations in the post Dark-Age Mediterranean. And Italy was a major focus for Greek colonization efforts in the years between 800 and 500 BC. Naturally there was a lot of trade between the Greeks and the Etruscans. And when people trade, they don't just exchange goods, they also exchange ideas. Just as the Greeks had learned about the alphabet from the Phoenicians, the Etruscans now learned about the alphabet from the Greeks.
paragraphs.zh: 这些族群的语言都属于印欧语系。 我们在上节课学到，希腊人之所以 出现在意大利，是因为他们和腓尼 基人一样，是后黑暗时代地中海地 区的主要航海文明。 在公元前 800-500 年间，意大利是 希腊的主要殖民地区。 自然而然，希腊人和伊特鲁里亚人 之间会有不少贸易往来。 贸易不仅仅是商品的交换，还存在 着文化的交流。 正如希腊人从腓尼基人那里学来 了字母系统一样，伊特鲁里亚人现 在也从希腊人那里学来了字母系 统。

paragraphs.en: I wish I could tell you more about the Etruscans, but they got absorbed into later Roman culture, and their settlements were all built-over. So most of what we know comes from some Etruscan tombs and what other cultures wrote about them. Here though are some Etruscan artifacts.As you can see they were excellent craftsmen. By the year 500 BC, a city called Rome was flourishing off of this Etruscan-Greek trade. The Romans at that time were under Etruscan control, but spoke a slightly different language called "Latin". The city of Rome exists because of the Tiber river, the 3rd largest river in Italy.
paragraphs.zh: 我还想告诉你更多关于伊特鲁里 亚人的事情，但是他们被后来的罗 马文化所吸收，殖民地也都被推倒 重建了。所以我们所了解的信息大 部分来自于伊特鲁里亚人的墓葬 以及其他文化对他们的描述。 这里有一些伊特鲁里亚人的手工 艺品。可以看出，他们是优秀的工 匠。 到公元前 500 年，一个叫罗马的城 市在伊特鲁里亚和希腊的贸易中 繁荣起来。 当时罗马处于伊特鲁里亚人的控 制之下，但当地人说的是一种略微 不同的语言，叫做“拉丁语”。 罗马城是因为意大利第三大河— —台伯河而存在的。

paragraphs.en: The Tiber marked the boundary between the Etruscan, Latin and Sabine cultures, and was also an important river for trade and communication because it connects the interior of Italy to the western coast. 25 kilometers from the ocean, there is a ford where the river can easily be crossed. So that was that natural place for a human settlement, and one has existed there for thousands of years. Eventually this settlement grew into Rome. Actually, there were probably several settlements near that ford. There are seven hills in Rome, and according to tradition each of these hills had a separate village.And eventually these villages grew together and became the city of
paragraphs.zh: 台伯河是伊特鲁里亚文化、拉丁文 化和萨宾文化之间的分界线，也是 贸易和交流的重要河流，因为它将 意大利内陆和西海岸连接了起来。 离海岸线 25 公里的地方有一个浅 滩，很容易就能过河。所以几千年 来，一直是人类定居的天然地点。 最终，这个殖民地发展成了罗马 城。 其实，在那个浅滩附近可能有好几 个定居点。 罗马有七座山丘，按照传统，每座 山丘都有一个独立的村庄。最终这 些村庄一起发展成为了罗马城。

paragraphs.en: Rome. The Romans admired both Etruscan and Greek culture, and they shamelessly copied both of them. For example, the Romans copied the idea of theAlphabet from the Etruscans, just as the Etruscans had copied it from the Greeks. And I imagine the LatinAlphabet looks a little familiar to you right? That's because the LatinAlphabets is almost identical to the English alphabet which you are reading right now! The only difference is that the English alphabet added the letters J, U and W in the 1500s. In fact,ALL the western European countries use the LatinAlphabet in their writing, as do the Turks, the
paragraphs.zh: 罗马人既崇拜伊特鲁里亚文化，也 崇拜希腊文化，于是他们厚着脸皮 地照搬这两种文化。 例如，罗马人从伊特鲁里亚人那里 复制了字母系统的概念，就像伊特 鲁里亚人从希腊人那里抄来了字 母系统一样。 这个拉丁字母表看起来有点熟悉， 对吧? 那是因为拉丁字母和你正在阅读 的英语字母表几乎一样! 唯一的区别是英语字母表在 16 世 纪时增加了字母 J, U 和 W。 事实上，所有的西欧国家在书写时 都使用拉丁字母，土耳其人、印尼 人、菲律宾人、越南人以及使用汉

paragraphs.en: Indonesians, the Filipinos, the Vietnamese and the Chinese for Hanyu Pinyin. And because of colonialism, the LatinAlphabet has also spread from Europe to all of theAmericas, most ofAfrica and India. So the Latin alphabet is the most widely used alphabet in the world today. This map shows you all the countries that use it in green. But the Romans also admired and copied the Greeks! You can see this most clearly in their architecture. Look at how similar this Roman temple is to this Greek temple. And the Romans also copied the Greek religion. They had all the same gods and goddesses as the
paragraphs.zh: 语拼音的中国人也一样。 由于殖民政策，拉丁字母也从欧洲 传到了美洲全境，以及非洲和印度 的大部分地区。 因此，拉丁字母表是当今世界上使 用最广泛的字母系统。这张地图用 绿色标出了所有使用它的国家。 但是罗马人也崇拜和模仿希腊人! 你可以在他们的建筑风格中看出 来。看看这两个罗马神庙和希腊神 庙有多相似吧。 罗马人也复制了希腊的宗教。他们 和希腊人有着一样的众神，不同处 只是给他们起了拉丁文的名字。

paragraphs.en: Greeks, they just gave them Latin names. The Romans were such admirers of Greek culture that they tried to connect the story of their city to Greek literature. And they did this through the Trojan War. We know this legend, because around the year 1AD, the Roman poet Virgil wrote an epic poem that tells us the story. It is called the Aeneid. The poem is about a character who also appears in the Illiad, the Trojan warriorAeneas. In the poem,Aeneas is a strong warrior, but when Troy fell to the Greeks he had to escape. So he grabbed his friends and
paragraphs.zh: 罗马人是希腊文化的崇拜者，他们 希望把关于他们城市的故事与希 腊文学联系起来。 他们通过特洛伊战争做到了这一 点。 我们之所以知道这个传说，是因为 大约在公元 1 年，罗马诗人维吉尔 通过一首史诗告诉了我们这个故 事。这首史诗叫做《埃涅伊德》。 这首诗的主角是特洛伊勇士埃涅 阿斯，他也是出现在荷马史诗《伊 利亚特》中的人物。 在诗中，埃涅阿斯是一个强壮的战 士，但当特洛伊被希腊人攻陷时， 他不得不逃离家乡。 所以他带上朋友和家人，把他年迈

paragraphs.en: family, threw his elderly father on his shoulders and escaped the burning city of Troy. ThenAeneas, his friends, and his family travelled around the Mediterranean and had many adventures before they ended up living in Latium, the part of Italy near Rome. BecauseAeneas was a hero, he married the daughter of the Latin king and became the new King of the Latin-speakers after the old King died. For 15 generations,Aeneas's descendants were the Kings of the Latins. Other Roman legends continue the story after this point. In the 15th generation afterAeneas,
paragraphs.zh: 的父亲扛在肩膀上，逃离了燃烧中 的特洛伊城。 后来，埃涅阿斯以及他的朋友和家 人在地中海区域跋涉奔波，历经险 阻，最后在罗马附近的意大利拉丁 姆地区居住下来。 因为埃涅阿斯是个英雄，所以他得 以迎娶拉丁国王的女儿，并在老国 王去世后，成为拉丁语系地区的新 国王。 后来，埃涅阿斯的后裔当了 15 代 的拉丁国王。 其他的罗马传说也是从此时开始， 延续了这个故事。 到了埃涅阿斯的第 15 代王朝，邪

paragraphs.en: an evil prince namedAmulius overthrew his older brother named Numitor to become the King. But, his older brother had a daughter named Rhea Silvia. So Amulius forced Rhea to join the Vestal Virgins, a religious order that forbade marriage. This way, Rhea would not be able to have any children who could be a threat toAmulius. But the war-god Mars fell in love with Rhea and made her pregnant. Then Rhea gave birth to twin boys. Of course, her uncleAmulius wanted them killed. So he told a servant to do that. But the servant could not bring himself to kill two babies, so instead he placed them in a basket on the
paragraphs.zh: 恶的王子阿穆利乌斯推翻了他的 哥哥努米托，成为国王。 但他的哥哥有一个女儿，名叫瑞 亚·西尔维亚。因此，阿穆利乌斯 逼迫瑞亚成为了一名维斯太贞女， 这是一种禁止结婚的宗教命令。 这样，瑞亚就不会有孩子，也就不 可能对阿穆利乌斯构成威胁了。 但战神马尔斯爱上了瑞亚，并让她 怀孕了。 然后瑞亚生下了一对双胞胎男孩。 她的叔叔阿穆利乌斯吩咐一个仆 人去杀死他们。 仆人舍不得杀那两个婴孩，就把他 们放在篮子里，送到台伯河里去 了。

paragraphs.en: Tiber and sent them down the river. The basket floated down the river where a she-wolf discovered the babies. But instead of killing them, the she-wolf nursed the twin boys like her own puppies. The babies were then discovered by a shepherd named Faustulus and he brought them home to his wifeAcca Larentia to raise as their own sons. The twins were named Romulus and Remus. Romulus and Remus grew up and learned their true identity. So they joined forces with their grandfather and killed their wicked grand-uncle Amulius. Then the two boys went off to found their own city, but they get into an argument about where to build it.
paragraphs.zh: 篮子浮于水面，顺流而下，被一只 母狼发现了。母狼并没有杀死这两 个孩子，而是像对待自己的幼崽一 样哺育着他们。 随后，牧羊人福斯图卢斯发现了这 两个孩子，并把他们带回家，交给 了他的妻子阿卡·劳伦缇雅，像对 待亲生儿子一样抚养他们。 这对双胞胎名叫罗穆卢斯和雷穆 斯。 罗穆卢斯和雷穆斯长大后得知了 自己的真实身份。于是他们与祖父 一道，杀死了邪恶的叔祖父阿穆利 乌斯。 然后两个男孩出发去建立他们自 己的城市，但他们在选址上产生了 争执。

paragraphs.en: Remus started making fun of Romulus's location by jumping back and forth across his city wall while it was being built. This made Romulus so angry that he threw a spear at his brother Remus and killed him. Then Romulus founded the city alone, and named it "Rome" after himself. It became a haven for criminals, debtors, runaway slaves, and all those who were unwelcome in other Italian communities. According to legend, Romulus founded Rome onApril 21st, 753 BC. These early Romans were a violent and uncivilized people.
paragraphs.zh: 雷穆斯取笑罗穆卢斯的选址，当他 建造城墙的时候，在城墙上跳来跳 去。 这使罗穆卢斯非常生气，他向他的 兄弟雷穆斯扔了一枪，杀死了他。 随后罗穆卢斯独自建立了这座城 市，并以他自己的名字命名为“罗 马”。 这个地方成为了罪犯、债务人、逃 亡的奴隶以及在意大利其他地区 不被接受的人们的天堂。 传说罗穆卢斯于公元前 753 年 4 月 21 日建立了罗马。 这些早期的罗马人都非常暴力和 不文明。

paragraphs.en: So the second King of Rome, Numa Pompillius, was not sure how to make them more peaceful. He went to the nymph Egeria and she told him to use religion. So Numa Pompillisu introduced many religious traditions and festivals. He kept the Romans so busy with religion that they had no time left over to get into fights. Numa gave Rome 42 years of peace, and a reputation as a very pious city. After Numa Pompillius, Rome had 5 more Kings. The last 3 belonged to an Etruscan family called the Tarquins. The Romans might have had this
paragraphs.zh: 罗马的第二任国王努玛·庞皮利乌 斯，不知道该怎样使人们和平相 处。 他向仙女埃杰里亚请教，仙女告诉 他要依靠宗教的力量。 因此，努玛·庞皮利乌斯引入了许 多宗教传统和节日。 他让罗马人忙于宗教事务，无暇顾 及打架斗殴。 努玛让罗马得到了 42 年的和平时 光，罗马也被赞誉为一个虔诚的城 市。 在努玛·庞皮利乌斯之后，罗马还 经历了 5 任国王。 最后 3 任属于一个叫做塔昆的伊特 鲁里亚家族。 罗马人可能有关于伊特鲁里亚三

paragraphs.en: legend of the 3 Etruscan Kings, because the city was dominated by the Etruscans early in its history. So this legend might reflect a hazy cultural memory. The 7th and last King was a man named Tarquin the Proud and he was a terrible tyrant. So the strongest families in Rome banded together under the leadership of a man named Lucius Junius Brutus, and threw Tarquin out of power in 509 BC. Coincidentally, this was just one year before theAthenians got rid of their king in 508 BC! So in that year, Rome became independent from the Etruscans. Instead of a King, the city would be led by the 300 most prominent men.
paragraphs.zh: 王的传说，因为这座城市在它的早 期历史中被伊特鲁里亚人统治。 所以这个传说可能反映了一种朦 胧的文化记忆。 第七任也是最后一任国王叫做傲 慢王塔克文，是一个可怕的暴君。 于是罗马城里最强大的各个家族 在卢修斯·朱尼厄斯·布鲁特斯的 领导下团结起来，在公元前 509 年 把塔克文赶下了台。 巧合的是，这和雅典人在公元前 508 年罢黜国王只相差了一年的时 间! 所以在那一年，罗马从伊特鲁里亚 人中独立出来了。 这座城市将由 300 名最杰出的人 士，而不是一个国王来领导。这些

paragraphs.en: These men would be called "Senators" and they would have meetings in a "Senate" to decide what to do. Since it is hard for 300 people to quickly make decisions, the people would also elect two men called consuls to lead the city for one year at a time. There are many legendary stories about the first century of the Republic. There was Horatio who bravely defended a bridge by himself to save Rome during a critical battle. There was Scaevola, who placed his right hand in a fire and burned it without flinching to show an enemy king the courage of all Romans. And there was, Cincinnatus, who
paragraphs.zh: 人被称为“参议员”，他们将在“参 议院”开会，来决定今后的安排。 由于 300 人很难迅速做出决策，人 们还会每年选举两名执政官来领 导这座城市。 这个共和政体成立的第一个世纪 里有许多传奇故事。 有一人，名叫霍雷肖，在一场关键 战斗中勇敢地独自保卫一座桥，拯 救了罗马。 有一人，名叫斯凯沃拉，他毫不畏 缩地把右手放在火中烧掉，以此向 敌方的国王展示罗马人的勇气。 还有辛辛纳图斯，他为把罗马从危

paragraphs.en: became a dictator to save Rome from a crisis, and then gave up all his power afterwards and went back to his farm. But these are only legends, because in 390 BC, a tribe of Gauls invaded Italy. They defeated the Roman army and sacked Rome. In this sacking, they destroyed all of the written records. So proper Roman history only begins after 390 BC. After the sack of Rome by the Gauls, the city recovered quickly under the leadership of Marcus Furius Camillus. For his efforts rebuilding the city, the Romans honored him with the title "Second Founder of Rome".
paragraphs.zh: 机中拯救出来而成为了一名独裁 者，事成后又放弃了所有的权力， 解甲归田。 但这些仅仅是传说，因为在公元前 390 年，一个高卢部落入侵了意大 利。 他们打败了罗马军队并洗劫了罗 马。在这个过程中，他们销毁了所 有的文字史书。 因此，真正意义上的罗马历史是从 公元前 390 年开始的。 在高卢人洗劫罗马之后，城市在马 库斯·弗里乌斯·卡米卢斯的领导 下迅速重建。为了表彰他重建罗马 城的功劳，罗马人授予他“罗马第 二任创始人”的称号。

paragraphs.en: The Romans fought wars with the other Latin speaking cities and conquered them all by 338 BC. They gave the Latins the same legal rights of citizenship, except the right to vote. This integrated them into the growing Roman state.And it provided Rome with more soldiers. After absorbing the other Latin-speaking cities, the Romans fought another five decades of war with their Samnite neighbors. Now the Romans were in control of all central Italy. They also weakened their Etruscan neighbors to the north. Now their other neighbor, the Greek colonies of southern Italy began to get nervous about Rome's growing power.
paragraphs.zh: 罗马人与拉丁语系的其他城市开 战，并在公元前 338 年征服了所有 的城市。 他们给予这些拉丁人除了投票权 以外同样的公民权利。这使得他们 完全融入了这个不断壮大的罗马 国家。这也为罗马提供了更多的士 兵。 在吞并了拉丁语系的城市之后，罗 马人又与他们的邻居撒姆尼人打 了 50 年的战争。 现在罗马人控制了整个意大利中 部。他们还削弱了北部的伊特鲁里 亚人。 现在他们的另一个邻居，意大利南 部的希腊殖民地开始对罗马日益 增长的实力感到担忧。

paragraphs.en: So they asked the Greek King Pyrrhus of Epirus for help! Pyrrhus came over to Italy with an army of 25,000 men and 20 war elephants. He won a big victory over the Romans at the Battle of Heraclea and then again at the Battle of Asculum. But even though Pyrrhus won those battles, he lost too many soldiers to keep fighting. After the Battle ofAsculum he even said: "If we are 'victorious' in one more battle with the Romans, we shall be utterly ruined." So Pyrrhus was forced to leave Italy.
paragraphs.zh: 所以他们向希腊伊庇鲁斯国王皮 拉斯求助! 皮拉斯率领 2.5 万人的军队和 20 头战象来到意大利。 他在赫拉克勒亚战役和阿斯库卢 姆战役中击败了罗马人，获得了胜 利。 尽管皮拉斯赢得了这些战役，但他 失去了太多的士兵，无法继续战 斗。 在阿斯库卢姆战役之后，他甚至 说: “如果我们再一次在与罗马人的 战斗中‘得胜’，我们将被彻底毁 灭。” 因此皮拉斯被迫离开了意大利。

paragraphs.en: This is the origin of the term "Pyrrhic Victory". APyrrhic victory is when you win the battle but lose too many soldiers, so its almost the same as a defeat. Or more broadly, a Pyrrhic victory can be when you achieve your goal, but sacrifice too much to do it. Pyrrhus left Italy, and Rome completed its conquest of the south and of the last Etruscan cities in the north. By 264 BC, Rome controlled all of Italy south of theArno river. Now that the Romans had conquered most of the Italian Penninsula, they began to look overseas for the first time.And their next natural target was the Island of
