paragraphs.zh: 但是我仍然认为这座桥是 2000 年 前由罗马人建造的，从那以后人们 就一直在使用和照顾它。 还有这座横跨台伯河的庞氏法布 里库斯桥，它建于公元前 62 年。 这座桥至今仍在使用！ 罗马人还建造了被称为渡槽的水 道，用来将淡水输送到几十英里外 的罗马城市。 这些渡槽里的水都是依靠重力输 送的，所以罗马人需要在整体上保 持一个轻微的向下倾斜的角度。 大多数时候，这些渡槽是建在地下

paragraphs.en: were built underground, but sometimes the need to maintain that long downward slope meant that the Romans would build tall elevated sections in certain areas. ThisAqueduct of Segovia, in Spain, is a good example of that. The Pont du Gard in southern France is another good example. And one aqueduct, theAqua Virgo was restored in 1453 and still supplies Rome with water today! The Trevia fountain in Rome is watered by this aqueduct. Here is a picture of the construction of one of these aqueducts. And of course the Romans didn't just build pipes to bring fresh water in, they also built sewers to take sewage water out.
paragraphs.zh: 的，但有时为了保持斜坡的连贯 性，罗马人会在某些地区修建高架 的渡槽。 西班牙塞戈维亚的这个引水渠就 是一个很好的例子。 法国南部的嘉德水道桥则是另一 个经典范例。 其中，圣女引水渠于 1453 年修复， 至今仍在为罗马供水！ 罗马的特莱维喷泉就是由它供水 的。 这是渡槽建造过程的示意图。 当然，罗马人不只是建造管道来引 入淡水，他们还建造下水道来排出 污水。

paragraphs.en: Here is the Cloaca Maxima, which carried waste-water out of the city and into the Tiber River. And there was plenty of sewage, because Rome at its peak had a population of a million people, all pooping in toilets like these. Here is an illustration of such toilets in use. But perhaps I didn't need one, after all some things about human beings NEVER change! The Romans also enjoyed public entertainment. They built stadiums all across their empire. The most famous is the Coliseum in Rome itself. As we've mentioned before these stadiums were sometimes used for gladiator fights. Or for feeding condemned prisoners
paragraphs.zh: 这是麦西玛下水道，用于将污水从 城市排入台伯河。 由于罗马在其鼎盛时期有100万人 口，因此每天都会产生大量的污 水。当时的人们都在这样的厕所里 方便。 这是如何使用这种厕所的示意图。 但或许大家并不需要示意也能明 白，毕竟道理都懂，人类的某些共 同点是亘古不变的！ 罗马人也享受公共娱乐。他们在帝 国各地兴建体育场，其中最著名的 就是位于罗马的竞技场了。 我们之前也提到，这些体育场有时 被用作角斗士的比赛场地。 或者将死刑犯喂给野生动物。

paragraphs.en: to wild animals. But they were even sometimes filled with water and then displayed mock naval battles! But not all Roman entertainment was so bloody. In fact, the most popular sport was chariot racing. And the greatest chariot track was the Circus Maximus in Rome itself. The Romans also enjoyed the theater, like the ancient Greeks before them. And another important part of Roman social life were the public baths, supplied by that excellent water infrastructure. Unfortunately, the era of the five good emperors ended with the reign of emperor MarcusAurelius.
paragraphs.zh: 但有时候场内甚至装满了水，用来 展示模拟海战! 但并不是所有罗马的娱乐活动都 如此血腥。事实上，最流行的运动 是战车比赛。 最壮观的战车跑道位于罗马的马 克西穆斯竞技场。 和之前的古希腊人一样，罗马人也 喜欢戏剧。 罗马社会生活的还有一个重要组 成部分，那就是由完善的用水基础 设施所提供的公共澡堂。 不幸的是，五贤帝的时代在马可·奥 勒留之后就结束了。

paragraphs.en: MarcusAurelius is also famous for writing the book "Meditations", a classic work of Philosophy illustrating the Stoic school of thought. But after MarcusAurelius died, the empire did not go to an adopted successor, but instead went to his son Commodus. This was a mistake since Commodus was a bad leader. And then the Empire was struck by a terrible plague, which kept flaring up in epidemics all across the empire from 165 to 180AD. We think this was the first appearance of smallpox, and it may have killed 25% of the population. But it wasn't just limited to Rome, the plague spread across Eurasia through trade routes and made it all the way to Han China.
paragraphs.zh: 马可·奥勒留也因写下《沉思录》 一书而闻名，这本书是哲学经典著 作，阐释了斯多葛学派的思想。 但是马可·奥勒留死后并没有将帝 国交给一个收养的继承人，而是传 给了他的儿子康茂德。这是一个错 误，因为康茂德是并不是一个称职 的领导人。 随后，帝国遭受了一场可怕的瘟 疫。公元 165 年到 180 年，瘟疫在 帝国各地不断蔓延。 我们认为这是天花在历史上的首 次出现，它可能导致了当时 25%的 人口死亡。 但它不仅发生于罗马，瘟疫通过贸 易路线传遍欧亚大陆，并一路蔓延 到汉朝的中国。

paragraphs.en: There were seven major outbreaks in the Han dynasty between 151 and 185. As in Rome, perhaps 1/4 of China died from this first appearance of smallpox. In response to this horrible disease, a faith healer named Zhang Jue started a religious movement called the Yellow Turbans. These Yellow Turbans rebelled against the government from 184 to 205AD, and this set in motion a chain of events that brought down the Han Dynasty. But I'm sure you don't need me to tell you THAT story, you can read all about it in Romance of the Three Kingdoms. But keep in mind, Luo Guanzhong's
paragraphs.zh: 公元 151 年至 185 年间，汉朝共出 现了 7 次疫情大爆发。 和罗马的情况一样，大概有四分之 一的中国人死于首次出现的天花。 为了应对这种可怕的疾病，一位名 叫张角的太平道教主发起了一场 名为“黄巾起义”的宗教运动。 黄巾军在公元 184 年到 205 年期间 和官府对抗，并引发了一系列推翻 汉朝的事件。 其实不需要我多讲，你可以在《三 国演义》里读到所有的故事。 但要注意的是，这部经典名著由罗

paragraphs.en: classic was written around 1350 AD, nearly 1200 years AFTER the events it describes. Therefore, I think his story missed that important detail: The emergence of smallpox is what really brought down the Han dynasty. Anyway, back to Rome. Marcus Aurelius chose his son Commodus as his successor. But Commodus was not a good ruler, and the empire began to experience frequent civil wars as ambitious men struggled for power and tried to become the emperor. This period of chaos began in 192 AD when Commodus was assassinated. From that year until 284AD when
paragraphs.zh: 贯中写于公元 1350 年左右，比事 件的发生晚了近 1200 年。 因此，我认为他的故事忽略了一个 重要的细节：天花的出现才是导致 汉朝灭亡的真正原因。 不管怎样，我们把目光转向罗马。 马可·奥勒留选择他的儿子康茂德 作为他的接班人。 但是康茂德不是一个有力的统治 者，帝国开始陷入频繁的内战，野 心勃勃的人们争夺权力，想要成为 皇帝。 这一混乱时期始于公元 192 年，康 茂德在这一年被暗杀了。 从那一年到公元284年戴克里先登

paragraphs.en: Emperor Diocletian came to power, the empire had 57 emperors in just 92 years. This period was so bad that historians call it the crisis of the 3rd century. Romans frequently fought Romans in this period. As all these emperors struggled to pay for all this fighting, they tried to make the money go farther by mixing more and more copper into the silver coins. You can see the decline of the empire in the debasement of its currency. During this chaotic time, Christianity continue spreading as a religion. This map shows you areas with
paragraphs.zh: 基，仅仅 92 年的时间里，帝国出 现了 57 位皇帝。 这段时期非常黑暗，历史学家称之 为三世纪的危机。 在这段时期里，罗马人经常自相残 杀。 战争的开支不断增大，各个皇帝开 始把越来越多的铜混入银中以制 作更多的钱币。 你可以从货币贬值中看到帝国的 衰落。 在这混乱的时期里，基督教作为一 种宗教开始广泛传播。 这张地图显示了公元200年时基督

paragraphs.en: Christian communities in 200AD. And this map shows you the situation 100 years later. By 300AD, about 10% of the Roman Empire had joined this new religion. The Crisis of the 3rd century was finally ended by the Emperor Diocletian, who was a strong leader and regained control of the army. He was also very good at killing anyone who was a threat to him. Diocletian ruled for 21 years before retiring in 305AD at the age of 61. One of the keys to his success was Diocletian realized that the empire was too big for 1 man to rule. So he split it into 4 pieces, with 2 senior emperors called Augustus and 2 junior emperors called Caesars.
paragraphs.zh: 教的各个社区。 这张地图展示了100年后基督教的 发展状况。 到公元 300 年，罗马帝国大约 10% 的版图加入了这个新宗教。 三世纪的危机最终随着戴克里先 的登基而结束。他是一位强有力的 领袖，重新掌控了兵权。他能够毫 不犹豫地杀死任何威胁他的人。 戴克里先统治了 21 年，于公元 305 年以 61 岁的年龄退休。 戴克里先意识到帝国之大，由一个 皇帝难以统治。所以他将帝国一分 为四，并指派了 2 个大皇帝，称为 奥古斯都；2 个小皇帝，称做凯撒。 这种四分法被称为“四帝共治”。

paragraphs.en: This division into four was called the "Tetrarchy". Of course Diocletian kept the Eastern part of the Empire for himself because it was the richest part. This system of four emperors worked pretty well if the leaders trusted each other, but often times they would start fighting. After Diocletian retired, the near constant civil wars continued. The next important emperor was a man we now call Constantine the Great. His father had been the Junior emperor in charge of Britain and Gaul under Diocletian, and Constantine emerged as the eventual winner in the civil war
paragraphs.zh: 当然，戴克里先保留了帝国最富有 的的东部地区。 在领导人互相信任的前提下，这种 由四个皇帝组成的制度能够平稳 运行，但多数时候他们会相互发动 战争。 戴克里先退休后，内战持续不断。 下一位重要的皇帝，被后人称为君 士坦丁大帝。 他的父亲曾是戴克里先统治下的 英国和高卢的小皇帝，戴克里先退 休后，君士坦丁最终成为内战的胜 利者。

paragraphs.en: following Diocletian's retirement. Constantine was a strong and effective emperor, and he is most famous for two things: First, in 330AD, he moved the Capital city of the away from Rome! Instead he chose the town of Byzantium on the Bosphorus straits. He renamed the city "Constantinople" after himself, and built strong walls to defend it. Constantinople was a wise choice for a capital city. It could tax all the trade going fromAsia to Europe or from the Black Sea to the Mediterranean. And it was situated on a peninsula which made it easy to defend.
paragraphs.zh: 君士坦丁是一个强大而高效的皇 帝，他因两件事而闻名于世： 首先，在公元 330 年，他把首都从 罗马移走了！ 他选择了博斯普鲁斯海峡上的拜 占庭来取代首都。 他以自己的名字重新命名这座城 市为“君士坦丁堡”，并修建了坚 固的城墙来保卫它。 君士坦丁堡是一个合适的首都选 址。它可以对从亚洲到欧洲的所有 贸易路线征税，也可以对从黑海到 地中海的所有贸易征税。 它坐落在一个半岛上，因此易守难 攻。

paragraphs.en: Also Constantinople was closer to the Eastern part of the Empire, which was the wealthier part. But Constantine was also famous for being the first Christian Emperor. After Constantine's conversion, Christianity became the dominant religion in Europe, and it remains so to this very day. After Constantine died in 337AD, the empire began having trouble again as weak emperors and civil wars began to weaken it, especially in the West. 42-years after Constantine died, a man named Theodosius became emperor in 379AD. He was the last emperor to rule both the Eastern and Western halves of
paragraphs.zh: 君士坦丁堡离帝国东部更近，而东 部是比较富裕的地区。 君士坦丁堡也以第一位基督教皇 帝而闻名。 君士坦丁皈依基督教后，基督教成 为欧洲的主导宗教，直到今天仍然 如此。 君士坦丁在公元 337 年去世后，帝 国再次陷入危机中，软弱的皇帝和 内战削弱了帝国的力量，尤其是在 西部地区。 君士坦丁死后 42 年，狄奥多西在 公元 379 年称帝。 他是最后一个同时统治东罗马帝 国和西罗马帝国的皇帝。

paragraphs.en: the empire. When Theodosius died in 395AD, he divided the empire between his two sonsArcadius and Honorius. So after 395, the Empire was divided into an Eastern half and a Western half. Unfortunately, the western empire came under increasing pressure from tribes of Germanic people who began migrating into the empire. The western empire was too weakened by disease, and bad leadership, and civil war to effectively resist. In 410AD one of these tribes called the Visigoths even broke into the Italian Peninsula and sacked Rome itself! For the first time in 800 years, an
paragraphs.zh: 狄奥多西于公元 395 年去世，他将 帝国一分为二，由他的两个儿子阿 卡狄乌斯和霍诺里乌斯统治。 因此在公元 395 年后，帝国被分为 东半部和西半部。 不幸的是，西罗马帝国受到了来自 日耳曼部落日渐增加的压力，这些 部落开始向西罗马帝国迁徙。 西罗马帝国被疾病、无能的领导人 和内战所削弱，无法有效抵抗。 公元 410 年，其中一个叫做西哥特 人的部落甚至攻入意大利半岛，洗 劫了罗马城！ 这是 800 年来，敌军第一次进入罗

paragraphs.en: enemy army had entered the city of Rome! Of course, Rome was no longer the capital, but it was still the symbolic center of the Roman Empire. So its pillage by barbarians demoralized people all across the empire. Over the next 66 years, tribe after tribe invaded the Western Empire. As Roman power weakened, these tribes began setting up their own kingdoms in Roman territory. By 476, these kingdoms had become fully independent. The western part of the Empire had completely collapsed. The last Western Roman Emperor, RomulusAugustus, was deposed by one of these Germanic Kings in 476 AD.
paragraphs.zh: 马城！ 当然，罗马不再是首都，但它仍然 是罗马帝国的象征中心。因此野蛮 人的掠夺使帝国各地的人民士气 低落。 在接下来的 66 年里，一个又一个 部落不断入侵西罗马帝国。 随着罗马王权的削弱，这些部落开 始在罗马领土上建立自己的王国。 到了 476 年，这些王国纷纷独立。 帝国的西部完全崩溃了。 最后一位西罗马皇帝罗慕路斯·奥 古斯都于公元476年被一名日耳曼 国王废黜。

paragraphs.en: Of course, the Eastern Part of the Empire, with its capital at Constantinople continued, but now the "Roman" empire no longer included the city of Rome! For this reason, after 476AD, historians start calling the Eastern Roman Empire by a new name, they call it the "Byzantine Empire" after "Byzantium" the original name of "Constantinople." And here my friends, is where our story comes to an end. Because the fall of the Western Roman Empire, and the deposing of the last Western Roman Emperor, 476AD marks the traditional end of the Classical period. In 476AD, the Classical Era ended, and the MiddleAges began! But that my friends is a story for
paragraphs.zh: 当然，东部帝国正在其首都君士坦 丁堡延续着，但现在罗马城已经不 再是“罗马”帝国的一部分了！ 因此，公元 476 年以后，历史学家 开始用一个新名字称呼东罗马帝 国，他们称其为“拜占庭帝国”， 即以“君士坦丁堡”的原名“拜占 庭”命名。 朋友们，我们的故事到此为止了。 因为西罗马帝国的覆灭和最后一 位西罗马皇帝在公元 476 年被废 黜，标志着古典时期在传统意义上 结束了。 公元476年，随着古典时代的结束， 中世纪即将揭开序幕！ 但这将是另一个时间，另一个学期

paragraphs.en: another time, and another semester! Thank you all, very much! Okay everyone,I hope you enjoyed that lesson and want to learn more.If you do,I have some recommendations for you! The first is a Primary Source.A Roman historian named Cassius Dio lived from 155 to 235AD and wrote a huge history of Rome. He is a good source because he was an eyewitness to some of this history. The second is a recommendation I’ve given you before.It is the History of Rome podcast by Mike Duncan. The third is a picture book that I was given when I was 7 years old.It’s called The Romans:A
paragraphs.zh: 的故事了！ 谢谢大家！ 好了各位，我希望你们喜欢这个课 程。如果你还想了解更多的话，我 可以给你一些建议！ 首先是我们史料的主要来源。罗马 历史学家卡西乌斯·狄奥生活在公 园 155 年到 235 年之间，他写下了 一部关于罗马的巨著。 他是一个可靠的消息来源，因为他 是这段历史的见证人。 第二个建议我之前也说过。这是麦 克·邓肯的《罗马历史》播客。 第三个就是我7岁时得到的这本图 画书。这本书叫做《罗马人·不列 颠史》，作者是蒂姆·伍德和 R.H

paragraphs.en: history of Britain by Tim Wood and R.H.Barrow. This is the book that first got me fascinated by history when I was a little boy. Finally, there is a great architectural history book called: City: A Story of Roman Planning and Construction by David Macaulay. This book show you how the Romans built their cities, roads, aqueducts, houses and all the rest of their structures. If you like the part of the lesson when I talked about roads and aqueducts, then this is the book for you. Okay everyone, that's all for now, thank you very much!
paragraphs.zh: 巴罗。 正是这本书让当时还是个小男孩 的我第一次对历史着了迷。diao 最后还有一本伟大的建筑史书，叫 做《城市:罗马的规划与建筑》，作 者是大卫·麦考利。 这本书向你展示了罗马人是如何 建造他们的城市、道路、渡槽、房 屋和其他建筑的。 如果你对我讲的关于道路和渡槽 的部分感兴趣，那么这本书你可不 能错过。 好了各位，今天就讲到这里啦，谢 谢大家!
